SY0-601 CompTIA Security+ : 2.43\nTOTAL: CompTIA Security+ Cert (SY0-701) | Udemy, Section 3: Identity and Access Management, Lecture 15: Multifactor\nAuthentication4\nCompTIA Security+ Certification SY0-601: The Total Course [Video], Chapter 3: Identity and Account Management, Section\n2: Enabling Multifactor Authentication5", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator notices that several users are logging in from suspicious IP addresses. After speaking with the users, the\nadministrator determines that the employees were not logging in from those IP addresses and resets the affected users?\npasswords. Which of the following should the administrator implement to prevent this type of attack from succeeding in the\nfuture?", "options": ["Multifactor authentication", "Permissions assignment", "Access management", "Password complexity"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA146", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "An ISOW is a document that outlines the project, the cost, and the completion time frame for a security company to provide a\nservice to a client. ISOW stands for Information Security Operations Work, and it is a type of contract that specifies the\nscope, deliverables, milestones, and payment terms of a security project. An ISOW is usually used for one-time or short-term\nprojects that have a clear and defined objective and outcome. For example, an ISOW can be used for a security\nassessment, a penetration test, a security audit, or a security training.\nThe other options are not correct because they are not documents that outline the project, the cost, and the completion time\nframe for a security company to provide a service to a client. A MSA is a master service agreement, which is a type of\ncontract that establishes the general terms and conditions for a long-term or ongoing relationship between a security\ncompany and a client. A MSA does not specify the details of each individual project, but rather sets the framework for future\nprojects that will be governed by separate statements of work (SOWs). A SLA is a service level agreement, which is a type\nof contract that defines the quality and performance standards for a security service provided by a security company to a\nclient. A SLA usually includes the metrics, targets, responsibilities, and penalties for measuring and ensuring the service\nlevel. A BPA is a business partnership agreement, which is a type of contract that establishes the roles and expectations for\na strategic alliance between two or more security companies that collaborate to provide a joint service to a client. A BPA\nusually covers the objectives, benefits, risks, and obligations of the partnership. Reference = CompTIA Security+ Study\nGuide (SY0-701), Chapter 8: Governance, Risk, and Compliance, page 387. Professor Messer?s CompTIA SY0-701\nSecurity+ Training Course, Section 8.2: Compliance and Controls, video: Contracts and Agreements (5:12).", "type": "mc", "q": "A client asked a security company to provide a document outlining the project, the cost, and the completion time frame.\nWhich of the following documents should the company provide to the client?", "options": ["MSA", "SLA", "BPA", "SOW"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "DA147", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization's RPO for a critical system is two hours. The system is used Monday through Friday, from 9:00 am to 5:00\npm. Currently, the organization performs a full backup every Saturday that takes four hours to complete. Which of the\nfollowing additional backup implementations would be the BEST way for the analyst to meet the business requirements?", "options": ["Incremental backups Monday through Friday at 6:00 p.m and differential backups hourly", "Full backups Monday through Friday at 6:00 p.m and incremental backups hourly.", "incremental backups Monday through Friday at 6:00 p.m and full backups hourly.", "Full backups Monday through Friday at 6:00 p.m and differential backups hourly."], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA148", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst needs to complete an assessment. The analyst is logged into a server and must use native tools to map\nservices running on it to the server's listening ports. Which of the following tools can BEST accomplish this talk?", "options": ["Netcat", "Netstat", "Nmap", "Nessus"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA149", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Badge access and access control vestibule are two of the best ways to ensure only authorized personnel can access a\nsecure facility. Badge access requires the personnel to present a valid and authenticated badge to a reader or scanner that\ngrants or denies access based on predefined rules and permissions. Access control vestibule is a physical security measure\nthat consists of a small room or chamber with two doors, one leading to the outside and one leading to the secure area. The\npersonnel must enter the vestibule and wait for the first door to close and lock before the second door can be opened. This\nprevents tailgating or piggybacking by unauthorized individuals. Reference:\nCompTIA Security+ Study Guide: Exam SY0-701, 9th Edition, Chapter 4, pages 197-1981", "type": "multi", "q": "Which of the following would be the best ways to ensure only authorized personnel can access a secure facility? (Select\ntwo).", "options": ["Fencing", "Video surveillance", "Badge access", "Access control vestibule", "Sign-in sheet", "Sensor"], "answer": [2, 3]}, {"id": "DA150", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A jump server is a device or virtual machine that acts as an intermediary between a user?s workstation and a remote\nnetwork segment. A jump server can be used to securely access servers or devices that are not directly reachable from the\nuser?s workstation, such as database servers. A jump server can also provide audit logs and access control for the remote\nconnections. A jump server is also known as a jump box or a jump host12.\nRADIUS is a protocol for authentication, authorization, and accounting of network access. RADIUS is not a device or a\nmethod to access remote servers, but rather a way to verify the identity and permissions of users or devices that request\nnetwork access34.\nHSM is an acronym for Hardware Security Module, which is a physical device that provides secure storage and generation of\ncryptographic keys. HSMs are used to protect sensitive data and applications, such as digital signatures, encryption, and\nauthentication. HSMs are not used to access remote servers, but rather to enhance the security of the data and applications\nthat reside on them5 .\nA load balancer is a device or software that distributes network traffic across multiple servers or devices, based on criteria\nsuch as availability, performance, or capacity. A load balancer can improve the scalability, reliability, and efficiency of\nnetwork services, such as web servers, application servers, or database servers. A load balancer is not used to access\nremote servers, but rather to optimize the delivery of the services that run on them . Reference =\nHow to access a remote server using a jump host\nJump server\nRADIUS\nRemote Authentication Dial-In User Service (RADIUS)\nHardware Security Module (HSM)\n[What is an HSM?]\n[Load balancing (computing)] [What is Load Balancing?]", "type": "mc", "q": "A company prevented direct access from the database administrators? workstations to the network segment that contains\ndatabase servers. Which of the following should a database administrator use to access the database servers?", "options": ["Jump server", "RADIUS", "HSM", "Load balancer"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA151", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A forensics investigator is examining a number of unauthorized payments the were reported on the company's website.\nSome unusual log entries show users received an email for an unwanted mailing list and clicked on a link to attempt to\nunsubscribe. One of the users reported the email to the phishing team, and the forwarded email revealed the link to be:\nWhich of the following will the forensics investigator MOST likely determine has occurred?", "options": ["SQL injection", "CSRF", "XSS", "XSRF"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA152", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A NAC (network access control) platform is a technology that enforces security policies on devices that attempt to access a\nnetwork. A NAC platform can verify the identity, role, and compliance of the devices, and grant or deny access based on\npredefined rules. A NAC platform can protect both wired and wireless networks, but in this scenario, the systems\n\n\nadministrator is trying to protect the wired attack surface, which is the set of vulnerabilities that can be exploited through a\nphysical connection to the network12.189; CompTIA Security+ Certification Kit: Exam SY0-701, 7th Edition, Chapter 5, page 237.", "type": "mc", "q": "After a security incident, a systems administrator asks the company to buy a NAC platform. Which of the following attack\nsurfaces is the systems administrator trying to protect?", "options": ["Bluetooth", "Wired", "NFC", "SCADA"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA153", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Code signing is a technique that uses cryptography to verify the authenticity and integrity of the code created by the\ncompany. Code signing involves applying a digital signature to the code using a private key that only the company\npossesses. The digital signature can be verified by anyone who has the corresponding public key, which can be distributed\nthrough a trusted certificate authority. Code signing can prevent unauthorized modifications, tampering, or malware injection\ninto the code, and it can also assure the users that the code is from a legitimate source. Reference = CompTIA Security+\nStudy Guide with over 500 Practice Test Questions: Exam SY0-701, 9th Edition, Chapter 2, page 74.\nCompTIA Security+ (SY0-701) Certification Exam Objectives, Domain 3.2, page 11. Application Security ? SY0-601\nCompTIA Security+ : 3.2", "type": "mc", "q": "A software development manager wants to ensure the authenticity of the code created by the company. Which of the\nfollowing options is the most appropriate?", "options": ["Testing input validation on the user input fields", "Performing code signing on company-developed software", "Performing static code analysis on the software", "Ensuring secure cookies are use"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA154", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Tuning is the activity of adjusting the configuration or parameters of a security tool or system to optimize its performance and\nreduce false positives or false negatives. Tuning can help to filter out the normal or benign activity that is detected by the\nsecurity tool or system, and focus on the malicious or anomalous activity that requires further investigation or response.\nTuning can also help to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the security operations center by reducing the workload\nand alert fatigue of the analysts. Tuning is different from aggregating, which is the activity of collecting and combining data\nfrom multiple sources or sensors to provide a comprehensive view of the security posture. Tuning is also different from\nquarantining, which is the activity of isolating a potentially infected or compromised device or system from the rest of the\nnetwork to prevent further damage or spread. Tuning is also different from archiving, which is the activity of storing and\npreserving historical data or records for future reference or compliance. The act of ignoring detected activity in the future that\nis deemed normal by the security operations center is an example of tuning, as it involves modifying the settings or rules of\nthe security tool or system to exclude the activity from the detection scope. Therefore, this is the best answer among the\ngiven options. Reference = Security Alerting and Monitoring Concepts and Tools ? CompTIA Security+ SY0- 701: 4.3, video\nat 7:00; CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide, page 191.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security operations center determines that the malicious activity detected on a server is normal. Which of the following\nactivities describes the act of ignoring detected activity in the future?", "options": ["Tuning", "Aggregating", "Quarantining", "Archiving"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA155", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Whaling is a type of phishing attack that targets high-profile individuals, such as executives, celebrities, or politicians. The\nattacker impersonates someone with authority or influence and tries to trick the victim into performing an action, such as\ntransferring money, revealing sensitive information, or clicking on a malicious link. Whaling is also called CEO fraud or\nbusiness email compromise2.", "type": "mc", "q": "An attacker posing as the Chief Executive Officer calls an employee and instructs the employee to buy gift cards. Which of\nthe following techniques is the attacker using?", "options": ["Smishing", "Disinformation", "Impersonating", "Whaling"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "DA156", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Detective controls are security measures that are designed to identify and monitor any malicious activity or anomalies on a\nsystem or network. They can help to discover the source, scope, and impact of an attack, and provide evidence for further\nanalysis or investigation. Detective controls include log files, security audits, intrusion detection systems, network monitoring\ntools, and antivirus software. In this case, the administrator used log files as a detective control to review the ransomware\nattack on the company?s system. Log files are records of events and activities that occur on a system or network, such as\nuser actions, system errors, network traffic, and security alerts. They can provide valuable information for troubleshooting,\nauditing, and forensics.Security+ (Plus) Certification | CompTIA IT Certifications, under ?About the exam?, bullet point 3:\n?Operate with an awareness of applicable regulations and policies, including principles of governance, risk, and\ncompliance.?\nCompTIA Security+ Certification Kit: Exam SY0-701, 7th Edition, Chapter 1, page 14: ?Detective controls are designed to\nidentify and monitor any malicious activity or anomalies on a system or network.?\nControl Types ? CompTIA Security+ SY0-401: 2.1 - Professor Messer IT ?, under ?Detective Controls?: ?Detective controls\nare security measures that are designed to identify and monitor any malicious activity or anomalies on a system or network.?", "type": "mc", "q": "After a recent ransomware attack on a company's system, an administrator reviewed the log files. Which of the following\ncontrol types did the administrator use?", "options": ["Compensating", "Detective", "Preventive", "Corrective"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA157", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A nation-state is a threat actor that is sponsored by a government or a political entity to conduct cyberattacks against other\ncountries or organizations. Nation-states have large financial resources, advanced technical skills, and strategic objectives\nthat may target critical systems such as military, energy, or infrastructure. Nation-states are often motivated by espionage,\nsabotage, or warfare12. Reference = 1: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide, page 542: Threat Actors ?\nCompTIA Security+ SY0-701 ? 2.1, video by Professor Messer.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following threat actors is the most likely to use large financial resources to attack critical systems located in\nother countries?", "options": ["Insider", "Unskilled attacker", "Nation-state", "Hacktivist"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "DA158", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Shadow IT is the term used to describe the use of unauthorized or unapproved IT resources within an\norganization. The marketing department set up its own project management software without telling the appropriate\ndepartments, such as IT, security, or compliance. This could pose a risk to the organization?s security posture, data integrity,\nand regulatory compliance1.", "type": "mc", "q": "The marketing department set up its own project management software without telling the appropriate departments. Which of\nthe following describes this scenario?", "options": ["Shadow IT", "Insider threat", "Data exfiltration", "Service disruption"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA159", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Most SLA include a monetary penalty if the vendor is unable to meet the agreed-upon expectations", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following would be BEST to establish between organizations to define the responsibilities of each party outline\nthe key deliverables and include monetary penalties for breaches to manage third-party risk?", "options": ["An ARO", "An MOU", "An SLA", "A BPA"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "DA160", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A VPN is a virtual private network that creates a secure tunnel between two or more devices over a public network. A VPN\ncan encrypt and authenticate the data, as well as hide the IP addresses and locations of the devices. A jump server is a\nserver that acts as an intermediary between a user and a target server, such as a production server. A jump server can\nprovide an additional layer of security and access control, as well as logging and auditing capabilities. A firewall is a device\nor software that filters and blocks unwanted network traffic based on predefined rules. A firewall can protect the internal\nnetwork from external threats and limit the exposure of sensitive services and ports. A security analyst should recommend\n\n\nsetting up a VPN and placing the jump server inside the firewall to improve the security of the remote desktop access to the\nproduction network. This way, the remote desktop service will not be exposed to the public network, and only authorized\nusers with VPN credentials can access the jump server and then the production server. Reference: CompTIA\nSecurity+ Study Guide: Exam SY0-701, 9th Edition, Chapter 8: Secure Protocols and Services, page 382-383 1; Chapter 9:\nNetwork Security, page 441-442 1", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst scans a company's public network and discovers a host is running a remote desktop that can be used to\naccess the production network. Which of the following changes should the security analyst recommend?", "options": ["Changing the remote desktop port to a non-standard number", "Setting up a VPN and placing the jump server inside the firewall", "Using a proxy for web connections from the remote desktop server", "Connecting the remote server to the domain and increasing the password length"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA161", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Brute force is a type of attack that tries to guess the password or other credentials of a user account by using a large number\nof possible combinations. An attacker can use automated tools or scripts to perform a brute force attack and gain\nunauthorized access to the account. The domain activity logs show that the user ismith has failed to log in 10 times in a row\nwithin a short period of time, which is a strong indicator of a brute force attack. The logs also show that the source IP\naddress of the failed logins is different from the usual IP address of ismith, which suggests that the attacker is using a\ndifferent device or location to launch the attack. The security analyst should take immediate action to block the attacker?s IP\naddress, reset ismith?s password, and notify ismith of the incident. Reference = CompTIA Security+ Study Guide with over\n500 Practice Test Questions: Exam\nSY0-701, 9th Edition, Chapter 1, page 14. CompTIA Security+ (SY0-701) Certification Exam Objectives,\nDomain 1.1, page 2. Threat Actors and Attributes ? SY0-601 CompTIA Security+ : 1.1", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst reviews domain activity logs and notices the following:\n\nWhich of the following is the best explanation for what the security analyst has discovered?", "options": ["The user jsmith's account has been locked out.", "A keylogger is installed on [smith's workstation", "An attacker is attempting to brute force ismith's account.", "Ransomware has been deployed in the domain."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "DA162", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A legal hold (also known as a litigation hold) is a notification sent from an organization?s legal team to employees instructing\nthem not to delete electronically stored information (ESI) or discard paper documents that may be relevant to a new or\nimminent legal case. A legal hold is intended to preserve evidence and prevent spoliation, which is the intentional or\nnegligent destruction of evidence that could harm a party?s case. A legal hold can be triggered by various events, such as a\nlawsuit, a regulatory investigation, or a subpoena12\nIn this scenario, the company?s attorneys have requested that the security team initiate a legal hold in response to the\nlawsuit filed by the customers after the company was compromised. This means that the security team will most likely be\nrequired to retain any communications related to the security breach until further notice. This could include emails, instant\nmessages, reports, logs, memos, or any other documents that could be relevant to the lawsuit. The security team should\nalso inform the relevant custodians (the employees who have access to or control over the ESI) of their preservation\nobligations and monitor their compliance. The security team should also document the legal hold process and its scope, as\nwell as take steps to protect the ESI from alteration, deletion, or loss341: CompTIA Security+ Study Guide: Exam SY0-701, 9th Edition, Chapter 6: Risk Management, page\n303 2: CompTIA Security+ Certification Kit: Exam SY0-701, 7th Edition, Chapter 6: Risk Management,\npage 305 3: Legal Hold (Litigation Hold) - The Basics of E-Discovery - Exterro 5 4: The Legal Implications and\nConsequences of a Data Breach 6", "type": "mc", "q": "After a company was compromised, customers initiated a lawsuit. The company's attorneys have requested that the security\nteam initiate a legal hold in response to the lawsuit. Which of the following describes the action the security team will most\nlikely be required to take?", "options": ["Retain the emails between the security team and affected customers for 30 days.", "Retain any communications related to the security breach until further notice.", "Retain any communications between security members during the breach response.", "Retain all emails from the company to affected customers for an indefinite period of time."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA163", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Cyber insurance is a type of insurance that covers the financial losses and liabilities that result from cyberattacks, such as\ndata breaches, ransomware, denial-of-service, phishing, or malware. Cyber insurance can help a company recover from the\ncosts of restoring data, repairing systems, paying ransoms, compensating customers, or facing legal actions. Cyber\ninsurance is one of the possible strategies that a company can use to address the items listed on the risk register. A risk\nregister is a document that records the identified risks, their probability, impact, and mitigation strategies for a project or an\norganization. The four common risk mitigation strategies are:\nAccept: The company acknowledges the risk and decides to accept the consequences without taking any action to reduce or\neliminate the risk. This strategy is usually chosen when the risk is low or the cost of mitigation is too high.\nTransfer: The company transfers the risk to a third party, such as an insurance company, a vendor, or a partner. This\nstrategy is usually chosen when the risk is high or the company lacks the resources or expertise to handle the risk.\nMitigate: The company implements controls or measures to reduce the likelihood or impact of the risk. This strategy is\nusually chosen when the risk is moderate or the cost of mitigation is reasonable. Avoid: The company eliminates the risk by\nchanging the scope, plan, or design of the project or the organization. This strategy is usually chosen when the risk is\nunacceptable or the cost of mitigation is too high.\nBy purchasing cyber insurance, the company is transferring the risk to the insurance company, which will cover the financial\nlosses and liabilities in case of a cyberattack. Therefore, the correct answer is\nB. Transfer. Reference = CompTIA Security+ Study Guide (SY0-701), Chapter 8: Governance, Risk, and Compliance, page\n377. Professor Messer?s CompTIA SY0-701 Security+ Training Course, Section 8.1: Risk Management, video: Risk\nMitigation Strategies (5:37).", "type": "mc", "q": "A company purchased cyber insurance to address items listed on the risk register. Which of the following strategies does this\nrepresent?", "options": ["Accept", "Mitigate", "Avoid"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA164", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A buffer overflow is a vulnerability that occurs when an application writes more data to a memory buffer than it can hold,\ncausing the excess data to overwrite adjacent memory locations. A register is a small storage area in the CPU that holds\ntemporary data or instructions. An attacker can exploit a buffer overflow to overwrite a register with a malicious address that\npoints to a shellcode, which is a piece of code that gives the attacker control over the system. By doing so, the attacker can\nbypass the normal execution flow of the application and execute arbitrary commands.2.3: Application Attacks, Page 76 1; Buffer Overflows - CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 - 2.3 2", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following vulnerabilities is exploited when an attacker overwrites a register with a malicious address?", "options": ["VM escape", "SQL injection", "Buffer overflow", "Race condition"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "DA165", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A dashboard is a graphical user interface that provides a visual representation of key performance indicators, metrics, and\ntrends related to security events and incidents. A dashboard can help the board of directors to understand the number and\nimpact of incidents that affected the organization in a given period, as well as the status and effectiveness of the security\ncontrols and processes. A dashboard can also allow the board of directors to drill down into specific details or filter the data\nby various criteria12.\nA packet capture is a method of capturing and analyzing the network traffic that passes through a device or a network\nsegment. A packet capture can provide detailed information about the source, destination, protocol, and content of each\npacket, but it is not a suitable way to present a summary of incidents to the board of directors13.\nA vulnerability scan is a process of identifying and assessing the weaknesses and exposures in a system or a network that\ncould be exploited by attackers. A vulnerability scan can help the organization to prioritize and remediate the risks and\nimprove the security posture, but it is not a relevant way to report the number of incidents that occurred in a quarter14.\n\n\nMetadata is data that describes other data, such as its format, origin, structure, or context. Metadata can provide useful\ninformation about the characteristics and properties of data, but it is not a meaningful way to communicate the impact and\nfrequency of incidents to the board of directors. Reference = 1: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide, page\n3722: SIEM\nDashboards ? SY0-601 CompTIA Security+ : 4.3, video by Professor Messer3: CompTIA Security+ SY0- 701 Certification\nStudy Guide, page 3464: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide, page 362. : CompTIA Security+ SY0-701\nCertification Study Guide, page 97.", "type": "mc", "q": "A newly appointed board member with cybersecurity knowledge wants the board of directors to receive a quarterly report\ndetailing the number of incidents that impacted the organization. The systems administrator is creating a way to present the\ndata to the board of directors. Which of the following should the systems administrator use?", "options": ["Packet captures", "Vulnerability scans", "Metadata", "Dashboard"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "DA166", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A geolocation policy is a policy that restricts or allows access to data or resources based on the geographic location of the\nuser or device. A geolocation policy can be implemented using various methods, such as IP address filtering, GPS tracking,\nor geofencing. A geolocation policy can help the company?s legal department to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive\ndocuments from individuals in high-risk countries12.\nThe other options are not effective ways to limit access based on location:\nData masking: This is a technique of obscuring or replacing sensitive data with fictitious or anonymized data. Data masking\ncan protect the privacy and confidentiality of data, but it does not prevent access to data based on location3.\nEncryption: This is a process of transforming data into an unreadable format using a secret key or algorithm. Encryption can\nprotect the integrity and confidentiality of data, but it does not prevent access to data based on location. Encryption can also\nbe bypassed by attackers who have the decryption key or method4.\nData sovereignty regulation: This is a set of laws or rules that govern the storage, processing, and transfer of data within a\nspecific jurisdiction or country. Data sovereignty regulation can affect the availability and compliance of data, but it does not\nprevent access to data based on location. Data sovereignty regulation can also vary depending on the country or region.SY0-601 CompTIA Security+ : 3.7, video by Professor Messer3: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide,\npage 1004: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide, page 101. : CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification\nStudy Guide, page 102.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company?s legal department drafted sensitive documents in a SaaS application and wants to ensure the documents\ncannot be accessed by individuals in high-risk countries. Which of the following is the most effective way to limit this access?", "options": ["Data masking", "Encryption", "Geolocation policy", "Data sovereignty regulation"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "DA167", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator checks the table of a network switch, which shows the following output:\n\nWhich of the following is happening to this switch?", "options": ["MAC Flooding", "DNS poisoning", "MAC cloning", "ARP poisoning"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA168", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Preparation is the phase in the incident response process when a security analyst reviews roles and responsibilities, as well\nas the policies and procedures for handling incidents. Preparation also involves gathering and maintaining the necessary\ntools, resources, and contacts for responding to incidents. Preparation can help a security analyst to be ready and proactive\nwhen an incident occurs, as well as to reduce the impact and duration of the incident.\nSome of the activities that a security analyst performs during the preparation phase are:\nDefining the roles and responsibilities of the incident response team members, such as the incident manager, the incident\ncoordinator, the technical lead, the communications lead, and the legal advisor.\nEstablishing the incident response plan, which outlines the objectives, scope, authority, and procedures for responding to\nincidents, as well as the escalation and reporting mechanisms. Developing the incident response policy, which defines the\ntypes and categories of incidents, the severity levels, the notification and reporting requirements, and the roles and\nresponsibilities of the stakeholders.\nCreating the incident response playbook, which provides the step-by-step guidance and checklists for handling specific types\nof incidents, such as denial-of-service, ransomware, phishing, or data breach. Acquiring and testing the incident response\ntools, such as network and host-based scanners, malware analysis tools, forensic tools, backup and recovery tools, and\ncommunication and collaboration tools.\nIdentifying and securing the incident response resources, such as the incident response team, the incident response\nlocation, the evidence storage, and the external support.\nBuilding and maintaining the incident response contacts, such as the internal and external stakeholders, the law enforcement\nagencies, the regulatory bodies, and the media.CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide, Chapter 6: Architecture and Design, Section 6.4: Secure Systems\nDesign, p. 279-280\nCompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Exam Objectives, Domain 3: Architecture and Design,\nObjective 3.5: Given a scenario, implement secure network architecture concepts, Sub-objective: Incident response, p. 16", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the phase in the incident response process when a security analyst reviews roles and\nresponsibilities?", "options": ["Preparation", "Recovery", "Lessons learned", "Analysis"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA169", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Ransomware is a type of malware that encrypts the victim?s files and demands a ransom for the decryption key. The\nransomware usually displays a message on the infected system with instructions on how to pay the ransom and recover the\nfiles. The .ryk extension is associated with a ransomware variant called Ryuk, which targets large organizations and\ndemands high ransoms1.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator finds that all user workstations and servers are displaying a message that is associated with files containing\nan extension of .ryk. Which of the following types of infections is present on the systems?", "options": ["Virus", "Trojan", "Spyware", "Ransomware"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "DA170", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A false positive is a result that indicates a vulnerability or a problem when there is none. In this case, the vulnerability\nscanning report shows that the telnet service on port 23 is open and uses an insecure network protocol. However, the\nsecurity analyst performs a test using nmap and a script that checks for telnet encryption support. The result shows that the\ntelnet server supports encryption, which means that the data transmitted between the client and the server can be protected\nfrom eavesdropping. Therefore, the reported vulnerability is a false positive and does not reflect the actual security posture\nof the server. The security analyst should verify the encryption settings of the telnet server and client and ensure that they\nare configured properly3. Reference: 3: Telnet Protocol - Can You Encrypt Telnet?", "type": "mc", "q": "After reviewing the following vulnerability scanning report:\nServer:192.168.14.6\nService: Telnet\nPort: 23 Protocol: TCP\nStatus: Open Severity: High\nVulnerability: Use of an insecure network protocol A security analyst performs the following test:\nnmap -p 23 192.168.14.6 ?script telnet-encryption\nPORT STATE SERVICE REASON\n23/tcp open telnet syn-ack I telnet encryption:\n| _ Telnet server supports encryption\nWhich of the following would the security analyst conclude for this reported vulnerability?", "options": ["It is a false positive.", "A rescan is required.", "It is considered noise.", "Compensating controls exist."], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA171", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A high-availability network is a network that is designed to minimize downtime and ensure continuous operation even in the\nevent of a failure or disruption. A high-availability network must consider the following factors12:\nEase of recovery: This refers to the ability of the network to restore normal functionality quickly and efficiently after a failure\nor disruption. Ease of recovery can be achieved by implementing backup and restore procedures, redundancy and failover\nmechanisms, fault tolerance and resilience, and disaster recovery plans.\nAttack surface: This refers to the amount of exposure and vulnerability of the network to potential threats and attacks. Attack\nsurface can be reduced by implementing security controls such as firewalls, encryption, authentication, access control,\nsegmentation, and hardening.\nThe other options are not directly related to high-availability network design:\nAbility to patch: This refers to the process of updating and fixing software components to address security issues, bugs, or\nperformance improvements. Ability to patch is important for maintaining the security and functionality of the network, but it is\nnot a specific factor for high-availability network design.\n\n\nPhysical isolation: This refers to the separation of network components or devices from other networks or physical\nenvironments. Physical isolation can enhance the security and performance of the network, but it can also reduce the\navailability and accessibility of the network resources. Responsiveness: This refers to the speed and quality of the network?s\nperformance and service delivery. Responsiveness can be measured by metrics such as latency, throughput, jitter, and\npacket loss. Responsiveness is important for ensuring customer satisfaction and user experience, but it is not a specific\nfactor for high-availability network design.\nExtensible authentication: This refers to the ability of the network to support multiple and flexible authentication methods and\nprotocols. Extensible authentication can improve the security and convenience of the network, but it is not a specific factor\nfor high-availability network design.SY0-701 ? 3.4, video by Professor Messer.", "type": "multi", "q": "Which of the following must be considered when designing a high-availability network? (Choose two).", "options": ["Ease of recovery", "Ability to patch", "Physical isolation", "Responsiveness", "Attack surface", "Extensible authentication"], "answer": [0, 4]}, {"id": "DA172", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The company should request a certification from the vendor that confirms the storage array has been disposed of securely\nand in compliance with the company?s policies and standards. A certification provides evidence that the vendor has followed\nthe proper procedures and methods to destroy the classified data and prevent unauthorized access or recovery. A\ncertification may also include details such as the date, time, location, and method of disposal, as well as the names and\nsignatures of the personnel involved. Reference: CompTIA Security+ Study Guide: Exam SY0-701, 9th Edition, Chapter 3,\npage 1441", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is discarding a classified storage array and hires an outside vendor to complete the disposal. Which of the\nfollowing should the company request from the vendor?", "options": ["Certification", "Inventory list", "Classification", "Proof of ownership"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA173", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "An endpoint detection and response (EDR) system is a security tool that monitors and analyzes the activities and behaviors\nof endpoints, such as computers, laptops, mobile devices, and servers. An EDR system can detect, prevent, and respond to\nvarious types of threats, such as malware, ransomware, phishing, and advanced persistent threats (APTs). One of the\nfeatures of an EDR system is to block the automatic execution of downloaded programs, which can prevent malicious code\nfrom running on the endpoint when a user clicks on a link in a phishing message. This can reduce the impact of a phishing\nattack and protect the endpoint from compromise. Updating the EDR policies to block automatic execution of downloaded\nprograms is a technical control that can mitigate the risk of phishing, regardless of the user?s awareness or behavior.\nTherefore, this is the best answer among the given options.\nThe other options are not as effective as updating the EDR policies, because they rely on\nadministrative or physical controls that may not be sufficient to prevent or stop a phishing attack. Placing posters around the\noffice to raise awareness of common phishing activities is a physical control that can increase the user?s knowledge of\nphishing, but it may not change their behavior or prevent them from clicking on a link in a phishing message. Implementing\nemail security filters to prevent phishing emails from being delivered is an administrative control that can reduce the\nexposure to phishing, but it may not be able to block all phishing emails, especially if they are crafted to bypass the filters.\nCreating additional training for users to recognize the signs of phishing attempts is an administrative control that can improve\nthe user?s skills of phishing detection, but it may not guarantee that they will always be vigilant or cautious when receiving\nan email. Therefore, these options are not the best answer for this question. Reference = Endpoint Detection and Response\n? CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 ? 2.2, video at 5:30; CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide, page 163.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst and the management team are reviewing the organizational performance of a recent phishing campaign.\nThe user click-through rate exceeded the acceptable risk threshold, and the management team wants to reduce the impact\nwhen a user clicks on a link in a phishing message. Which of the following should the analyst do?", "options": ["Place posters around the office to raise awareness of common phishing activities.", "Implement email security filters to prevent phishing emails from being delivered", "Update the EDR policies to block automatic execution of downloaded programs.", "Create additional training for users to recognize the signs of phishing attempts."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "DA174", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A firewall rule is a set of criteria that determines whether to allow or deny a packet to pass through the firewall. A firewall rule\nconsists of several elements, such as the action, the protocol, the source address, the destination address, and the port\nnumber. The syntax of a firewall rule may vary depending on the type and vendor of the firewall, but the basic logic is the\nsame. In this question, the security analyst is creating an inbound firewall rule to block the IP address 10.1.4.9 from\naccessing the organization?s network. This means that the action should be deny, the protocol should be any (or ig for IP),\nthe source address should be 10.1.4.9 (which means a single IP address), the destination address should be 0.0.0.0/0\n(which means any IP address), and the port number should be any. Therefore, the correct firewall rule is:\naccess-list inbound deny ig source 10.1.4.9 destination 0.0.0.0/0\nThis rule will match any packet that has the source IP address of 10.1.4.9 and drop it. The other options are incorrect\nbecause they either have the wrong action, the wrong source address, or the wrong destination address. For example,\noption A has the source and destination addresses reversed, which means that it will block any packet that has the\ndestination IP address of 10.1.4.9, which is not the intended goal. Option C has the wrong action, which is permit, which\nmeans that it will allow the packet to pass through the firewall, which is also not the intended goal. Option D has the same\nproblem as option A, with the source and destination addresses reversed.CompTIA Security+ SY0-501, Understanding Firewall Rules ? CompTIA Network+ N10-005: 5.5, Configuring Windows\nFirewall ? CompTIA A+ 220-1102 ? 1.6.", "type": "mc", "q": "During a security incident, the security operations team identified sustained network traffic from a malicious IP address:\n10.1.4.9. A security analyst is creating an inbound firewall rule to block the IP address from accessing the organization?s\nnetwork. Which of the following fulfills this request?", "options": ["access-list inbound deny ig source 0.0.0.0/0 destination 10.1.4.9", "access-list inbound deny ig source 10.1.4.9 destination 0.0.0.0/0", "access-list inbound permit ig source 10.1.4.9 destination 0.0.0.0/0", "access-list inbound permit ig source 0.0.0.0/0 destination 10.1.4.9"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA175", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Geographic dispersion is a strategy that involves distributing the servers or data centers across different geographic\nlocations. Geographic dispersion can help the company to mitigate the risk of weather events causing damage to the server\nroom and downtime, as well as improve the availability, performance, and resilience of the network. Geographic dispersion\n\n\ncan also enhance the disaster recovery and business continuity capabilities of the company, as it can provide backup and\nfailover options in case of a regional outage or disruption12.\nThe other options are not the best ways to address the company?s concern:\nClustering servers: This is a technique that involves grouping multiple servers together to act as a single system. Clustering\nservers can help to improve the performance, scalability, and fault tolerance of the network, but it does not protect the\nservers from physical damage or downtime caused by weather events, especially if the servers are located in the same room\nor building3. Load balancers: These are devices or software that distribute the network traffic or workload among multiple\nservers or resources. Load balancers can help to optimize the utilization, efficiency, and reliability of the network, but they do\nnot prevent the servers from being damaged or disrupted by weather events, especially if the servers are located in the\nsame room or building4.\nOff-site backups: These are copies of data or files that are stored in a different location than the original source. Off-site\nbackups can help to protect the data from being lost or corrupted by weather events, but they do not prevent the servers\nfrom being damaged or disrupted by weather events, nor do they ensure the availability or continuity of the network services.CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 ? 3.4, video by Professor Messer3: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701\nCertification Study Guide, page 984: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Certification Study Guide, page 99. : CompTIA Security+\nSY0-701 Certification Study Guide, page 100.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is concerned about weather events causing damage to the server room and downtime. Which of the following\nshould the company consider?", "options": ["Clustering servers", "Geographic dispersion", "Load balancers", "Off-site backups"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "DA176", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Compensating controls are alternative security measures that are implemented when the primary controls are not feasible,\ncost-effective, or sufficient to mitigate the risk. In this case, the organization\nused compensating controls to protect the legacy system from potential attacks by disabling unneeded services and placing\na firewall in front of it. This reduced the attack surface and the likelihood of exploitation.\n\nOfficial CompTIA Security+ Study Guide (SY0-701), page 29 Security Controls - CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 - 1.1 1", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization disabled unneeded services and placed a firewall in front of a business-critical legacy system. Which of the\nfollowing best describes the actions taken by the organization?", "options": ["Exception", "Segmentation", "Risk transfer", "Compensating controls"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "DA177", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Firmware is a type of software that is embedded in a hardware device, such as a router, a printer, or a BIOS chip. Firmware\ncontrols the basic functions and operations of the device, and it can be updated or modified by the manufacturer or the user.\nFirmware version is a hardware-specific vulnerability, as it can expose the device to security risks if it is outdated, corrupted,\nor tampered with. An attacker can exploit firmware vulnerabilities to gain unauthorized access, modify device settings, install\nmalware, or cause damage to the device or the network. Therefore, it is important to keep firmware updated and verify its\nintegrity and authenticity. Reference = CompTIA Security+ Study Guide with over 500 Practice Test Questions: Exam SY0701, 9th Edition, Chapter 2, page 67. CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Exam Objectives, Domain 2.1, page 10.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a hardware-specific vulnerability?", "options": ["Firmware version", "Buffer overflow", "SQL injection", "Cross-site scripting"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "DA178", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "DLP stands for Data Loss Prevention, which is a tool that can assist with detecting and preventing the unauthorized\ntransmission or leakage of sensitive data, such as a customer?s PII (Personally\nIdentifiable Information). DLP can monitor, filter, and block data in motion (such as emails), data at rest (such as files), and\ndata in use (such as applications). DLP can also alert the sender, the recipient, or the administrator of the data breach, and\napply remediation actions, such as encryption, quarantine, or deletion. DLP can help an organization comply with data\nprotection regulations, such as GDPR, HIPAA, or PCI DSS, and protect its reputation and assets. Reference = CompTIA\nSecurity+ Study Guide with over 500 Practice Test Questions: Exam SY0-701, 9th Edition, Chapter 2, page 78. CompTIA\nSecurity+ SY0-701 Exam Objectives, Domain 2.5, page 11.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following tools can assist with detecting an employee who has accidentally emailed a file containing a\ncustomer?s PII?", "options": ["SCAP", "Net Flow", "Antivirus", "DLP"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "DA179", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A high-availability network is a network that is designed to minimize downtime and ensure continuous operation of critical\nservices and applications. To achieve this goal, a high-availability network must consider two important factors: ease of\nrecovery and attack surface.\nEase of recovery refers to the ability of a network to quickly restore normal functionality after a failure, disruption, or disaster.\nA high-availability network should have mechanisms such as redundancy, failover, backup, and restore to ensure that any\nsingle point of failure does not cause a complete network outage. A high-availability network should also have procedures\nand policies for incident response, disaster recovery, and business continuity to minimize the impact of any network issue on\nthe organization?s operations and reputation.\nAttack surface refers to the exposure of a network to potential threats and vulnerabilities. A highavailability network should\nhave measures such as encryption, authentication, authorization,\nfirewall, intrusion detection and prevention, and patch management to protect the network from unauthorized access, data\nbreaches, malware, denial-of-service attacks, and other cyberattacks. A high-availability network should also have processes\nand tools for risk assessment, threat intelligence, vulnerability scanning, and penetration testing to identify and mitigate any\nweaknesses or gaps in the network security.", "type": "multi", "q": "Which of the following must be considered when designing a high-availability network? (Select two).", "options": ["Ease of recovery", "Ability to patch", "Physical isolation", "Responsiveness", "Attack surface", "Extensible authentication"], "answer": [0, 4]}, {"id": "DA180", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "This situation is an example of smishing, which is a type of phishing that uses text messages (SMS) to entice individuals into\nproviding personal or sensitive information to cybercriminals. The best responses to this situation are to add a smishing\nexercise to the annual company training and to issue a general email warning to the company. A smishing exercise can help\nraise awareness and educate employees on how to recognize and avoid smishing attacks. An email warning can alert\nemployees to the fraudulent text message and remind them to verify the identity and legitimacy of any requests for\ninformation or money. Reference = What Is Phishing | Cybersecurity | CompTIA, Phishing ? SY0601 CompTIA Security+ : 1.1 - Professor Messer IT Certification Training Courses", "type": "multi", "q": "Several employees received a fraudulent text message from someone claiming to be the Chief Executive Officer (CEO). The\nmessage stated:\n?I?m in an airport right now with no access to email. I need you to buy gift cards for employee recognition awards. Please\nsend the gift cards to following email address.?\nWhich of the following are the best responses to this situation? (Choose two).", "options": ["Cancel current employee recognition gift cards.", "Add a smishing exercise to the annual company training.", "Issue a general email warning to the company.", "Have the CEO change phone numbers.", "Conduct a forensic investigation on the CEO's phone.", "Implement mobile device management."], "answer": [1, 2]}, {"id": "DA181", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Salting is the process of adding extra random data to a password or other data before applying a oneway data\ntransformation algorithm, such as a hash function. Salting increases the complexity and randomness of the input data,\nmaking it harder for attackers to guess or crack the original data using precomputed tables or brute force methods. Salting\nalso helps prevent identical passwords from producing identical hash values, which could reveal the passwords to attackers\nwho have access to the hashed data. Salting is commonly used to protect passwords stored in databases or transmitted\nover networks. Reference =\nPasswords technical overview\nEncryption, hashing, salting ? what?s the difference?\nSalt (cryptography)", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is used to add extra complexity before using a one-way data transformation algorithm?", "options": ["Key stretching", "Data masking", "Steganography", "Salting"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "DA182", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Side loading is the process of installing software outside of a manufacturer?s approved software repository. This can expose\nthe device to potential vulnerabilities, such as malware, spyware, or unauthorized access. Side loading can also bypass\nsecurity controls and policies that are enforced by the manufacturer or the organization. Side loading is often done by users\nwho want to access applications or features that are not available or allowed on their devices. Reference = Sideloading -\n\n\nCompTIA Security + Video Training | Interface Technical Training, Security+ (Plus) Certification | CompTIA IT Certifications,\nLoad Balancers ? CompTIA Security+ SY0-501 ? 2.1, CompTIA Security+ SY0-601 Certification Study Guide.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following vulnerabilities is associated with installing software outside of a manufacturer?s approved software\nrepository?", "options": ["Jailbreaking", "Memory injection", "Resource reuse", "Side loading"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "DA183", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Labeling all laptops with asset inventory stickers and associating them with employee IDs can provide several security\nbenefits for a company. Two of these benefits are:\nA) If a security incident occurs on the device, the correct employee can be notified. An asset inventory sticker is a label that\ncontains a unique identifier for a laptop, such as a serial number, a barcode, or a QR code. By associating this identifier with\nan employee ID, the security team can easily track and locate the owner of the laptop in case of a security incident, such as\na malware infection, a data breach, or a theft. This way, the security team can notify the correct employee about the incident,\nand provide them with the necessary instructions or actions to take, such as changing passwords, scanning for viruses, or\nreporting the loss. This can help to contain the incident, minimize the damage, and prevent further escalation.\nF) Company data can be accounted for when the employee leaves the organization. When an\n\n\nemployee leaves the organization, the company needs to ensure that all the company data and assets are returned or\ndeleted from the employee?s laptop. By labeling the laptop with an asset inventory sticker and associating it with an\nemployee ID, the company can easily identify and verify the laptop that belongs to the departing employee, and perform the\nappropriate data backup, wipe, or transfer procedures. This can help to protect the company data from unauthorized access,\ndisclosure, or misuse by the former employee or any other party.\nThe other options are not correct because they are not related to the security benefits of labeling laptops with asset inventory\nstickers and associating them with employee IDs.\nB. The security team will be able to send user awareness training to the appropriate device. User awareness training is a\ntype of security education that aims to improve the knowledge and behavior of users regarding security threats and best\npractices. The security team can send user awareness training to the appropriate device by using the email address,\nusername, or IP address of the device, not the asset inventory sticker or the employee ID.\nC. Users can be mapped to their devices when configuring software MFA tokens. Software MFA tokens are a type of multifactor authentication that uses a software application to generate a one-time password or a push notification for verifying the\nidentity of a user. Users can be mapped to their devices when configuring software MFA tokens by using the device ID,\nphone number, or email address of the device, not the asset inventory sticker or the employee ID.\nD. User-based firewall policies can be correctly targeted to the appropriate laptops. User-based firewall policies are a type of\nfirewall rules that apply to specific users or groups of users, regardless of the device or location they use to access the\nnetwork. User-based firewall policies can be correctly targeted to the appropriate laptops by using the username, domain, or\ncertificate of the user, not the asset inventory sticker or the employee ID.\nE. When conducting penetration testing, the security team will be able to target the desired laptops. Penetration testing is a\ntype of security assessment that simulates a real-world attack on a network or system to identify and exploit vulnerabilities.\nWhen conducting penetration testing, the security team will be able to target the desired laptops by using the IP address,\nhostname, or MAC address of the laptop, not the asset\ninventory sticker or the employee ID. Reference = CompTIA Security+ Study Guide (SY0-701), Chapter 1: General Security\nConcepts, page 17. Professor Messer?s CompTIA SY0-701 Security+ Training Course, Section 1.4: Asset Management,\nvideo: Asset Inventory (6:12).", "type": "mc", "q": "A company has begun labeling all laptops with asset inventory stickers and associating them with employee IDs. Which of\nthe following security benefits do these actions provide? (Choose two.)", "options": ["If a security incident occurs on the device, the correct employee can be notified.", "The security team will be able to send user awareness training to the appropriate device. The security team will be able to send user awareness training to the appropriate device. User awareness training is a type of security education that aims to improve the knowledge and behavior of users regarding security threats and best practices. The security team can send user awareness training to the appropriate device by using the email address, username, or IP address of the device, not the asset inventory sticker or the employee ID.", "Users can be mapped to their devices when configuring software MFA tokens. Users can be mapped to their devices when configuring software MFA tokens. Software MFA tokens are a type of multifactor authentication that uses a software application to generate a one-time password or a push notification for verifying the identity of a user. Users can be mapped to their devices when configuring software MFA tokens by using the device ID, phone number, or email address of the device, not the asset inventory sticker or the employee ID.", "User-based firewall policies can be correctly targeted to the appropriate laptops. User-based firewall policies can be correctly targeted to the appropriate laptops. User-based firewall policies are a type of firewall rules that apply to specific users or groups of users, regardless of the device or location they use to access the network. User-based firewall policies can be correctly targeted to the appropriate laptops by using the username, domain, or certificate of the user, not the asset inventory sticker or the employee ID.", "Company data can be accounted for when the employee leaves the organization."], "answer": [0]} ]; const PASSLEADER_QUESTIONS = [ {"id": "PL1", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Endpoint logs are the most suitable data source for gathering additional information about the\nexecutable running on the employee's corporate laptop. These logs contain detailed information\nabout processes, executables, and activities occurring on the endpoint, enabling the security\nanalyst to understand the behavior of the executable and its potential impact on the system and\nnetwork.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst is reviewing alerts in the SIEM related to potential malicious network traffic\ncoming from an employee's corporate laptop. The security analyst has determined that additional\ndata about the executable running on the machine is necessary to continue the investigation.\nWhich of the following logs should the analyst use as a data source?", "options": ["Application", "IPS/IDS", "Network", "Endpoint"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL2", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Threat hunting is the process of proactively searching for signs of malicious activity or\ncompromise in a network, rather than waiting for alerts or indicators of compromise (IOCs) to\nappear. Threat hunting can help identify new tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) used by\nmalicious actors, as well as uncover hidden or stealthy threats that may have evaded detection\nby security tools. Threat hunting requires a combination of skills, tools, and methodologies, such\nas hypothesis generation, data collection and analysis, threat intelligence, and incident response.\nThreat hunting can also help improve the security posture of an organization by providing\nfeedback and recommendations for security improvements.", "type": "mc", "q": "A cyber operations team informs a security analyst about a new tactic malicious actors are using\nto compromise networks.\nSIEM alerts have not yet been configured. Which of the following best describes what the security\nanalyst should do to identify this behavior?", "options": ["Digital forensics", "E-discovery", "Incident response", "Threat hunting"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL3", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Transfer: Transferring a risk involves shifting some or all of the risk to another party, such as an\ninsurance provider, through contractual agreements or financial arrangements. If the company\npurchases cyber insurance to address items listed on the risk register, it represents a risk transfer\nstrategy. The company is transferring the financial burden of potential cyber incidents to the\ninsurance provider, who will compensate the company for covered losses.\nGiven the scenario described, the strategy represented by the company's purchase of cyber\ninsurance to address items listed on the risk register is Transfer. The company is transferring\nsome of the financial consequences of potential cyber incidents to the insurance provider through\nthe purchase of insurance coverage.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company purchased cyber insurance to address items listed on the risk register. Which of the\nfollowing strategies does this represent?", "options": ["Accept", "Transfer", "Mitigate", "Avoid"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL4", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Full disk encryption (FDE) encrypts all the data on a disk drive, protecting the data at rest on the\nentire laptop, including the operating system, applications, and user files. This ensures that if a\nlaptop is lost or stolen, the data cannot be accessed without the correct decryption key.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator would like to protect data on employees' laptops. Which of the following\nencryption techniques should the security administrator use?", "options": ["Partition", "Asymmetric", "Full disk", "Database"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL5", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Preventive - an acceptable use policy enforces rules to users to use company resources.\nexample - company A states that in order to access files in the company server you must connect\nto your company VPN when working from home. This prevents you from connecting from an\ninsecure network.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following security control types does an acceptable use policy best represent?", "options": ["Detective", "Compensating", "Corrective", "Preventive"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL6", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The principle of least privilege is a security concept that limits access to resources to the\nminimum level needed for a user, a program, or a device to perform a legitimate function. It is a\ncybersecurity best practice that protects high-value data and assets from compromise or insider\nthreat. Least privilege can be applied to different abstraction layers of a computing environment,\nsuch as processes, systems, or connected devices. However, it is rarely implemented in practice.\nIn this scenario, the IT manager is setting up the principle of least privilege by restricting access\nto the administrator console of the help desk software to only two authorized users: the IT\nmanager and the help desk lead. This way, the IT manager can prevent unauthorized or\naccidental changes to the software configuration, data, or functionality by other help desk staff.\nThe other help desk staff will only have access to the normal user interface of the software, which\nis sufficient for them to perform their job functions.\nThe other options are not correct. Hardening is the process of securing a system by reducing its\nsurface of vulnerability, such as by removing unnecessary software, changing default passwords,\nor disabling unnecessary services. Employee monitoring is the surveillance of workers' activity,\nsuch as by tracking web browsing, application use, keystrokes, or screenshots. Configuration\nenforcement is the process of ensuring that a system adheres to a predefined set of security\nsettings, such as by applying a patch, a policy, or a template.", "type": "mc", "q": "An IT manager informs the entire help desk staff that only the IT manager and the help desk lead\nwill have access to the administrator console of the help desk software. Which of the following\nsecurity techniques is the IT manager setting up?", "options": ["Hardening", "Employee monitoring", "Configuration enforcement", "Least privilege"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL7", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "According to the shared responsibility model, the client and the cloud provider have different roles\nand responsibilities for securing the cloud environment, depending on the service model. In an\nIaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) model, the cloud provider is responsible for securing the\nphysical infrastructure, such as the servers, storage, and network devices, while the client is\nresponsible for securing the operating systems, applications, and data that run on the cloud\ninfrastructure. Therefore, the client is responsible for securing the company's database in an IaaS\nmodel for a cloud environment, as the database is an application that stores data. The client can\nuse various security controls, such as encryption, access control, backup, and auditing, to protect\nthe database from unauthorized access, modification, or loss. The third-party vendor and the\nDBA (Database Administrator) are not roles defined by the shared responsibility model, but they\nmay be involved in the implementation or management of the database security.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following roles, according to the shared responsibility model, is responsible for\nsecuring the company's database in an IaaS model for a cloud environment?", "options": ["Client", "Third-party vendor", "Cloud provider", "DBA"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL8", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "An ISOW is a document that outlines the project, the cost, and the completion time frame for a\nsecurity company to provide a service to a client. ISOW stands for Information Security\nOperations Work, and it is a type of contract that specifies the scope, deliverables, milestones,\nand payment terms of a security project. An ISOW is usually used for one-time or short-term\nprojects that have a clear and defined objective and outcome. For example, an ISOW can be\nused for a security assessment, a penetration test, a security audit, or a security training. The\nother options are not correct because they are not documents that outline the project, the cost,\nand the completion time frame for a security company to provide a service to a client. A MSA is a\nmaster service agreement, which is a type of contract that establishes the general terms and\nconditions for a long-term or ongoing relationship between a security company and a client. A\nMSA does not specify the details of each individual project, but rather sets the framework for\nfuture projects that will be governed by separate statements of work (SOWs). A SLA is a service\nlevel agreement, which is a type of contract that defines the quality and performance standards\nfor a security service provided by a security company to a client. A SLA usually includes the\nmetrics, targets, responsibilities, and penalties for measuring and ensuring the service level. A\nBPA is a business partnership agreement, which is a type of contract that establishes the roles\nand expectations for a strategic alliance between two or more security companies that collaborate\nto provide a joint service to a client. A BPA usually covers the objectives, benefits, risks, and\nobligations of the partnership.", "type": "mc", "q": "A client asked a security company to provide a document outlining the project, the cost, and the\ncompletion time frame. Which of the following documents should the company provide to the\nclient?", "options": ["MSA", "SLA", "BPA", "SOW"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL9", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Input validation is a technique that checks the user input for any malicious or unexpected data\nbefore processing it by the web application. Input validation can prevent cross-site scripting (XSS)\nattacks, which exploit the vulnerability of a web application to execute malicious scripts in the\nbrowser of a victim. XSS attacks can compromise the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of\nthe web application and its users. Input validation can be implemented on both the client-side and\nthe server-side, but server-side validation is more reliable and secure. Input validation can use\nvarious methods, such as whitelisting, blacklisting, filtering, escaping, encoding, and sanitizing\nthe input data.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security team is reviewing the findings in a report that was delivered after a third party\nperformed a penetration test. One of the findings indicated that a web application form field is\nvulnerable to cross-site scripting. Which of the following application security techniques should\nthe security analyst recommend the developer implement to prevent this vulnerability?", "options": ["Secure cookies", "Version control", "Input validation", "Code signing"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL10", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Ease of recovery: High-availability networks should be designed in a way that allows for quick\nand easy recovery in the event of a failure. Redundancy, failover mechanisms, and backup\nsystems are some of the components that can help facilitate smooth recovery.\nResponsiveness: High-availability networks need to be responsive to ensure that any potential\nissues or failures are quickly detected, and appropriate actions are taken promptly to minimize\ndowntime and impact.", "type": "multi", "q": "Which of the following must be considered when designing a high-availability network? (Choose\ntwo).", "options": ["Ease of recovery", "Ability to patch", "Physical isolation", "Responsiveness", "Attack surface"], "answer": [0, 3]}, {"id": "PL11", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A change control request is a document that describes the proposed change to a system, the\nreason for the change, the expected impact, the approval process, the testing plan, the\nimplementation plan, the rollback plan, and the communication plan. A change control request is\na best practice for applying any patch to a production system, especially a high-priority one, as it\nensures that the change is authorized, documented, tested, and communicated. A change control\nrequest also minimizes the risk of unintended consequences, such as system downtime, data\nloss, or security breaches.", "type": "mc", "q": "A technician needs to apply a high-priority patch to a production system. Which of the following\nsteps should be taken first?", "options": ["Air gap the system.", "Move the system to a different network segment.", "Create a change control request.", "Apply the patch to the system."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL12", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Root cause analysis is a process of identifying and resolving the underlying factors that led to an\nincident. By conducting root cause analysis as part of incident response, security professionals\ncan learn from the incident and implement corrective actions to prevent future incidents of the\nsame nature. For example, if the root cause of a data breach was a weak password policy, the\nsecurity team can enforce a stronger password policy and educate users on the importance of\npassword security. Root cause analysis can also help to improve security processes, policies,\nand procedures, and to enhance security awareness and culture within the organization. Root\ncause analysis is not meant to gather loCs (indicators of compromise) for the investigation, as\nthis is a task performed during the identification and analysis phases of incident response. Root\ncause analysis is also not meant to discover which systems have been affected or to eradicate\nany trace of malware on the network, as these are tasks performed during the containment and\n\n\neradication phases of incident response.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following describes the reason root cause analysis should be conducted as part of\nincident response?", "options": ["To gather loCs for the investigation", "To discover which systems have been affected", "To eradicate any trace of malware on the network", "To prevent future incidents of the same nature"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL13", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "What are the consequences of not being PCI DSS compliant?\nThe fines imposed by the Card Brands and Acquiring Banks on merchants for non-compliance\ncan range from $5,000 to $100,000 per month. These fines, along with credit monitoring fees, can\nimpose substantial financial strain on businesses, underscoring the necessity of abiding by the\nPCI DSS requirements.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the most likely outcome if a large bank fails an internal PCI DSS\ncompliance assessment?", "options": ["Fines", "Audit findings", "Sanctions", "Reputation damage"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL14", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Capacity planning is the process of determining the resources needed to meet the current and\nfuture demands of an organization. Capacity planning can help a company develop a business\ncontinuity strategy by estimating how many staff members would be required to sustain the\nbusiness in the case of a disruption, such as a natural disaster, a cyberattack, or a pandemic.\nCapacity planning can also help a company optimize the use of its resources, reduce costs, and\nimprove performance.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is developing a business continuity strategy and needs to determine how many staff\nmembers would be required to sustain the business in the case of a disruption. Which of the\nfollowing best describes this step?", "options": ["Capacity planning", "Redundancy", "Geographic dispersion", "Tablet exercise"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL15", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A geolocation policy is a policy that restricts or allows access to data or resources based on the\n\n\ngeographic location of the user or device. A geolocation policy can be implemented using various\nmethods, such as IP address filtering, GPS tracking, or geofencing. A geolocation policy can help\nthe company's legal department to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive documents from\nindividuals in high-risk countries.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company's legal department drafted sensitive documents in a SaaS application and wants to\nensure the documents cannot be accessed by individuals in high-risk countries. Which of the\nfollowing is the most effective way to limit this access?", "options": ["Data masking", "Encryption", "Geolocation policy", "Data sovereignty regulation"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL16", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Firmware is a type of software that is embedded in a hardware device, such as a router, a printer,\nor a BIOS chip. Firmware controls the basic functions and operations of the device, and it can be\nupdated or modified by the manufacturer or the user. Firmware version is a hardware-specific\nvulnerability, as it can expose the device to security risks if it is outdated, corrupted, or tampered\nwith. An attacker can exploit firmware vulnerabilities to gain unauthorized access, modify device\nsettings, install malware, or cause damage to the device or the network. Therefore, it is important\nto keep firmware updated and verify its integrity and authenticity.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a hardware-specific vulnerability?", "options": ["Firmware version", "Buffer overflow", "SQL injection", "Cross-site scripting"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL17", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "While troubleshooting a firewall configuration, a technician determines that a \"deny any\" policy\nshould be added to the bottom of the ACL.
