The technician updates the policy, but the new policy\ncauses several company servers to become unreachable.\nWhich of the following actions would prevent this issue?", "options": ["Documenting the new policy in a change request and submitting the request to change management", "Testing the policy in a non-production environment before enabling the policy in the production network", "Disabling any intrusion prevention signatures on the 'deny any' policy prior to enabling the new policy", "Including an 'allow any1 policy above the 'deny any' policy"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL18", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Warm Sites\n- Not fully equipped, but fundamentals in place\n- Can be up and running within a few days\n- Cheaper than hot sites but with a slight delay\nCold Sites\n- Fewer facilities than warm sites\n- May be just an empty building, ready in 1-2 months\n- Cost-effective but adds more recovery time", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization is building a new backup data center with cost-benefit as the primary requirement\nand RTO and RPO values around two days. Which of the following types of sites is the best for\nthis scenario?", "options": ["Real-time recovery", "Hot", "Cold", "Warm"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL19", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Sanitization is the process of removing sensitive data from a storage device or a system before it\nis disposed of or reused. Sanitization can be done by using software tools or hardware devices\nthat overwrite the data with random patterns or zeros, making it unrecoverable. Sanitization is\ndifferent from destruction, which is the physical damage of the storage device to render it\nunusable. Sanitization is also different from enumeration, which is the identification of network\nresources or devices, and inventory, which is the tracking of assets and their locations. The policy\nof securely wiping hard drives before sending decommissioned systems to recycling is an\nexample of sanitization, as it ensures that no confidential data can be retrieved from the recycled\ndevices.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company requires hard drives to be securely wiped before sending decommissioned systems\nto recycling. Which of the following best describes this policy?", "options": ["Enumeration", "Sanitization", "Destruction", "Inventory"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL20", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator works for a local hospital and needs to ensure patient data is protected\nand secure. Which of the following data classifications should be used to secure patient data?", "options": ["Private", "Critical", "Sensitive", "Public"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL21", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Local data protection regulations are the first thing that a cloud-hosting provider should consider\nbefore expanding its data centers to new international locations. Data protection regulations are\nlaws or standards that govern how personal or sensitive data is collected, stored, processed, and\ntransferred across borders. Different countries or regions may have different data protection\nregulations, such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in the European Union, the\nPersonal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) in Canada, or the\nCalifornia Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) in the United States. A cloud-hosting provider must\ncomply with the local data protection regulations of the countries or regions where it operates or\nserves customers, or else it may face legal penalties, fines, or reputational damage. Therefore, a\ncloud-hosting provider should research and understand the local data protection regulations of\nthe new international locations before expanding its data centers there.", "type": "mc", "q": "A U.S.-based cloud-hosting provider wants to expand its data centers to new international\nlocations. Which of the following should the hosting provider consider first?", "options": ["Local data protection regulations", "Risks from hackers residing in other countries", "Impacts to existing contractual obligations", "Time zone differences in log correlation"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL22", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "An application allow list is a security technique that specifies which applications are permitted to\nrun on a system or a network. An application allow list can block unknown programs from\nexecuting by only allowing the execution of programs that are explicitly authorized and verified.\nAn application allow list can prevent malware, unauthorized software, or unwanted applications\nfrom running and compromising the security of the system or the network. The other options are\nnot the best ways to block unknown programs from executing:\nAccess control list: This is a security technique that specifies which users or groups are granted\nor denied access to a resource or an object. An access control list can control the permissions\nand privileges of users or groups, but it does not directly block unknown programs from\nexecuting. Host-based firewall: This is a security device that monitors and filters the incoming and\noutgoing network traffic on a single host or system. A host-based firewall can block or allow\nnetwork connections based on predefined rules, but it does not directly block unknown programs\nfrom executing.\nDLP solution: This is a security system that detects and prevents the unauthorized transmission\nor leakage of sensitive data. A DLP solution can protect the confidentiality and integrity of data,\nbut it does not directly block unknown programs from executing.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following would be the best way to block unknown programs from executing?", "options": ["Access control list", "Application allow list.", "Host-based firewall", "DLP solution"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL23", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A red team is a group of security professionals who perform offensive security assessments\ncovering penetration testing and social engineering. A red team simulates real-world attacks and\nexploits the vulnerabilities of a target organization, system, or network. A red team aims to test\nthe effectiveness of the security controls, policies, and procedures of the target, as well as the\nawareness and response of the staff and the blue team. A red team can be hired as an external\nconsultant or formed internally within the organization.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company hired a consultant to perform an offensive security assessment covering penetration\ntesting and social engineering.\nWhich of the following teams will conduct this assessment activity?", "options": ["White", "Purple", "Blue", "Red"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL24", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Code signing is a technique that uses cryptography to verify the authenticity and integrity of the\ncode created by the company. Code signing involves applying a digital signature to the code\nusing a private key that only the company possesses. The digital signature can be verified by\nanyone who has the corresponding public key, which can be distributed through a trusted\ncertificate authority. Code signing can prevent unauthorized modifications, tampering, or malware\ninjection into the code, and it can also assure the users that the code is from a legitimate source.", "type": "mc", "q": "A software development manager wants to ensure the authenticity of the code created by the\ncompany. Which of the following options is the most appropriate?", "options": ["Testing input validation on the user input fields", "Performing code signing on company-developed software", "Performing static code analysis on the software", "Ensuring secure cookies are use"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL25", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A honey pot is a system or a network that is designed to mimic a real production server and\nattract potential attackers. A honey pot can be used to identify the attacker's methods,\ntechniques, and objectives without affecting the actual production servers. A honey pot can also\ndivert the attacker's attention from the real targets and waste their time and resources. The other\noptions are not effective ways to identify potential attacker activities without affecting production\nservers:\nVideo surveillance: This is a physical security technique that uses cameras and monitors to\nrecord and observe the activities in a certain area. Video surveillance can help to deter, detect,\nand investigate physical intrusions, but it does not directly identify the attacker's activities on the\nnetwork or the servers.\nZero Trust: This is a security strategy that assumes that no user, device, or network is trustworthy\nby default and requires strict verification and validation for every request and transaction. Zero\nTrust can help to improve the security posture and reduce the attack surface of an organization,\nbut it does not directly identify the attacker's activities on the network or the servers. Geofencing:\nThis is a security technique that uses geographic location as a criterion to restrict or allow access\nto data or resources. Geofencing can help to protect the data sovereignty and compliance of an\norganization, but it does not directly identify the attacker's activities on the network or the servers.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following can be used to identify potential attacker activities without affecting\nproduction servers?", "options": ["Honey pot", "Video surveillance", "Zero Trust", "Geofencing"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL26", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Analysis is the incident response activity that describes the process of understanding the source\nof an incident. Analysis involves collecting and examining evidence, identifying the root cause,\ndetermining the scope and impact, and assessing the threat actor's motives and capabilities.\nAnalysis helps the incident response team to formulate an appropriate response strategy, as well\nas to prevent or mitigate future incidents. Analysis is usually performed after detection and before\ncontainment, eradication, recovery, and lessons learned.", "type": "mc", "q": "During an investigation, an incident response team attempts to understand the source of an\nincident. Which of the following incident response activities describes this process?", "options": ["Analysis", "Lessons learned", "Detection", "Containment"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL27", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A security practitioner completes a vulnerability assessment on a company's network and finds\nseveral vulnerabilities, which the operations team remediates. Which of the following should be\ndone next?", "options": ["Conduct an audit.", "Initiate a penetration test.", "Rescan the network.", "Submit a report."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL28", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "An insider threat is a security risk that originates from within the organization, such as an\nemployee, contractor, or business partner, who has authorized access to the organization's data\nand systems. An insider threat can be malicious, such as stealing, leaking, or sabotaging\nsensitive data, or unintentional, such as falling victim to phishing or social engineering. An insider\nthreat can cause significant damage to the organization's reputation, finances, operations, and\nlegal compliance. The user's activity of logging in remotely after hours and copying large amounts\nof data to a personal device is an example of a malicious insider threat, as it violates the\norganization's security policies and compromises the confidentiality and integrity of the data.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator was notified that a user logged in remotely after hours and copied large amounts\nof data to a personal device.\nWhich of the following best describes the user's activity?", "options": ["Penetration testing", "Phishing campaign", "External audit", "Insider threat"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL29", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Non-repudiation is the ability to prove that a message or document was sent or signed by a\nparticular person, and that the person cannot deny sending or signing it. Non-repudiation can be\nachieved by using cryptographic techniques, such as hashing and digital signatures, that can\nverify the authenticity and integrity of the message or document. Non-repudiation can be useful\nfor legal, financial, or contractual purposes, as it can provide evidence of the origin and content of\nthe message or document.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following allows for the attribution of messages to individuals?", "options": ["Adaptive identity", "Non-repudiation", "Authentication", "Access logs"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL30", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the best way to consistently determine on a daily basis whether security\nsettings on servers have been modified?", "options": ["Automation", "Compliance checklist", "Attestation", "Manual audit"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL31", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "DLP stands for Data Loss Prevention, which is a tool that can assist with detecting and\npreventing the unauthorized transmission or leakage of sensitive data, such as a customer's PII\n(Personally Identifiable Information). DLP can monitor, filter, and block data in motion (such as\nemails), data at rest (such as files), and data in use (such as applications). DLP can also alert the\nsender, the recipient, or the administrator of the data breach, and apply remediation actions, such\nas encryption, quarantine, or deletion. DLP can help an organization comply with data protection\nregulations, such as GDPR, HIPAA, or PCI DSS, and protect its reputation and assets.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following tools can assist with detecting an employee who has accidentally emailed\na file containing a customer's PII?", "options": ["SCAP", "Net Flow", "Antivirus", "DLP"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL32", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization recently updated its security policy to include the following statement:\n\n\nRegular expressions are included in source code to remove special characters such as $, |, ;. &,\n`, and ? from variables set by forms in a web application.\nWhich of the following best explains the security technique the organization adopted by making\nthis addition to the policy?", "options": ["Identify embedded keys", "Code debugging", "Input validation", "Static code analysis"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL33", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "An endpoint detection and response (EDR) system is a security tool that monitors and analyzes\nthe activities and behaviors of endpoints, such as computers, laptops, mobile devices, and\nservers. An EDR system can detect, prevent, and respond to various types of threats, such as\nmalware, ransomware, phishing, and advanced persistent threats (APTs). One of the features of\nan EDR system is to block the automatic execution of downloaded programs, which can prevent\nmalicious code from running on the endpoint when a user clicks on a link in a phishing message.\nThis can reduce the impact of a phishing attack and protect the endpoint from compromise.\nUpdating the EDR policies to block automatic execution of downloaded programs is a technical\ncontrol that can mitigate the risk of phishing, regardless of the user's awareness or behavior.\nTherefore, this is the best answer among the given options.\nThe other options are not as effective as updating the EDR policies, because they rely on\nadministrative or physical controls that may not be sufficient to prevent or stop a phishing attack.\nPlacing posters around the office to raise awareness of common phishing activities is a physical\ncontrol that can increase the user's knowledge of phishing, but it may not change their behavior\nor prevent them from clicking on a link in a phishing message. Implementing email security filters\nto prevent phishing emails from being delivered is an administrative control that can reduce the\nexposure to phishing, but it may not be able to block all phishing emails, especially if they are\ncrafted to bypass the filters. Creating additional training for users to recognize the signs of\nphishing attempts is an administrative control that can improve the user's skills of phishing\ndetection, but it may not guarantee that they will always be vigilant or cautious when receiving an\nemail. Therefore, these options are not the best answer for this question.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst and the management team are reviewing the organizational performance of a\nrecent phishing campaign. The user click-through rate exceeded the acceptable risk threshold,\nand the management team wants to reduce the impact when a user clicks on a link in a phishing\nmessage. Which of the following should the analyst do?", "options": ["Place posters around the office to raise awareness of common phishing activities.", "Implement email security filters to prevent phishing emails from being delivered", "Update the EDR policies to block automatic execution of downloaded programs.", "Create additional training for users to recognize the signs of phishing attempts."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL34", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A compensating control is a security measure that is implemented to mitigate the risk of a\nvulnerability or a weakness that cannot be resolved by the primary control. A compensating\ncontrol does not prevent or eliminate the vulnerability or weakness, but it can reduce the\nlikelihood or impact of an attack. A host-based firewall on a legacy Linux system that allows\nconnections from only specific internal IP addresses is an example of a compensating control, as\nit can limit the exposure of the system to potential threats from external or unauthorized sources.\nA host-based firewall is a software application that monitors and filters the incoming and outgoing\nnetwork traffic on a single host, based on a set of rules or policies. A legacy Linux system is an\nolder version of the Linux operating system that may not be compatible with the latest security\nupdates or patches, and may have known vulnerabilities or weaknesses that could be exploited\nby attackers.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following has been implemented when a host-based firewall on a legacy Linux\nsystem allows connections from only specific internal IP addresses?", "options": ["Compensating control", "Network segmentation", "Transfer of risk", "SNMP traps"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL35", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A user provisioning script is an automation technique that uses a predefined set of instructions or\ncommands to create, modify, or delete user accounts and assign appropriate access or\npermissions. A user provisioning script can help to streamline account creation by reducing\nmanual errors, ensuring consistency and compliance, and saving time and resources. The other\noptions are not automation techniques that can streamline account creation:\nGuard rail script: This is a script that monitors and enforces the security policies and rules on a\nsystem or a network. A guard rail script can help to prevent unauthorized or malicious actions,\nsuch as changing security settings, accessing restricted resources, or installing unwanted\nsoftware. Ticketing workflow: This is a process that tracks and manages the requests, issues, or\nincidents that are reported by users or customers. A ticketing workflow can help to improve the\ncommunication, collaboration, and resolution of problems, but it does not automate the account\ncreation process. Escalation script: This is a script that triggers an alert or a notification when a\ncertain condition or threshold is met or exceeded. An escalation script can help to inform the\nrelevant parties or authorities of a critical situation, such as a security breach, a performance\ndegradation, or a service outage.", "type": "mc", "q": "The management team notices that new accounts that are set up manually do not always have\ncorrect access or permissions.\nWhich of the following automation techniques should a systems administrator use to streamline\naccount creation?", "options": ["Guard rail script", "Ticketing workflow", "Escalation script", "User provisioning script"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL36", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A detective control is a type of control that monitors and analyzes the events and activities in a\nsystem or a network, and alerts or reports when an incident or a violation occurs. A SIEM\n(Security Information and Event Management) system is a tool that collects, correlates, and\nanalyzes the logs from various sources, such as firewalls, routers, servers, or applications, and\nprovides a centralized view of the security status and incidents. An analyst who reviews the logs\non a weekly basis can identify and investigate any anomalies, trends, or patterns that indicate a\npotential threat or a breach. A detective control can help the company to respond quickly and\neffectively to the incidents, and to improve its security posture and resilience.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is planning to set up a SIEM system and assign an analyst to review the logs on a\nweekly basis. Which of the following types of controls is the company setting up?", "options": ["Corrective", "Preventive", "Detective", "Deterrent"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL37", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator is looking for a low-cost application-hosting solution that is cloud-based.\nWhich of the following meets these requirements?", "options": ["Serverless framework", "Type 1 hvpervisor", "SD-WAN", "SDN"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL38", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Tuning is the activity of adjusting the configuration or parameters of a security tool or system to\noptimize its performance and reduce false positives or false negatives. Tuning can help to filter\nout the normal or benign activity that is detected by the security tool or system, and focus on the\nmalicious or anomalous activity that requires further investigation or response. Tuning can also\nhelp to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the security operations center by reducing the\nworkload and alert fatigue of the analysts. Tuning is different from aggregating, which is the\nactivity of collecting and combining data from multiple sources or sensors to provide a\ncomprehensive view of the security posture. Tuning is also different from quarantining, which is\nthe activity of isolating a potentially infected or compromised device or system from the rest of the\nnetwork to prevent further damage or spread. Tuning is also different from archiving, which is the\nactivity of storing and preserving historical data or records for future reference or compliance. The\nact of ignoring detected activity in the future that is deemed normal by the security operations\ncenter is an example of tuning, as it involves modifying the settings or rules of the security tool or\nsystem to exclude the activity from the detection scope. Therefore, this is the best answer among\n\n\nthe given options.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security operations center determines that the malicious activity detected on a server is normal.\nWhich of the following activities describes the act of ignoring detected activity in the future?", "options": ["Tuning", "Aggregating", "Quarantining", "Archiving"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL39", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Brute force is a type of attack that tries to guess the password or other credentials of a user\naccount by using a large number of possible combinations. An attacker can use automated tools\nor scripts to perform a brute force attack and gain unauthorized access to the account. The\ndomain activity logs show that the user ismith has failed to log in 10 times in a row within a short\nperiod of time, which is a strong indicator of a brute force attack. The logs also show that the\nsource IP address of the failed logins is different from the usual IP address of ismith, which\nsuggests that the attacker is using a different device or location to launch the attack. The security\nanalyst should take immediate action to block the attacker's IP address, reset ismith's password,\nand notify ismith of the incident.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst reviews domain activity logs and notices the following:\n\nWhich of the following is the best explanation for what the security analyst has discovered?", "options": ["The user jsmith's account has been locked out.", "A keylogger is installed on jsmith's workstation", "An attacker is attempting to brute force jsmith's account.", "Ransomware has been deployed in the domain."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL40", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Geographic dispersion is a strategy that involves distributing the servers or data centers across\ndifferent geographic locations. Geographic dispersion can help the company to mitigate the risk of\nweather events causing damage to the server room and downtime, as well as improve the\navailability, performance, and resilience of the network. Geographic dispersion can also enhance\nthe disaster recovery and business continuity capabilities of the company, as it can provide\nbackup and failover options in case of a regional outage or disruption. The other options are not\nthe best ways to address the company's concern:\nClustering servers: This is a technique that involves grouping multiple servers together to act as a\nsingle system. Clustering servers can help to improve the performance, scalability, and fault\ntolerance of the network, but it does not protect the servers from physical damage or downtime\ncaused by weather events, especially if the servers are located in the same room or building.\n\n\nLoad balancers: These are devices or software that distribute the network traffic or workload\namong multiple servers or resources. Load balancers can help to optimize the utilization,\nefficiency, and reliability of the network, but they do not prevent the servers from being damaged\nor disrupted by weather events, especially if the servers are located in the same room or building.\nOff-site backups: These are copies of data or files that are stored in a different location than the\noriginal source. Off-site backups can help to protect the data from being lost or corrupted by\nweather events, but they do not prevent the servers from being damaged or disrupted by weather\nevents, nor do they ensure the availability or continuity of the network services.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is concerned about weather events causing damage to the server room and\ndowntime.\nWhich of the following should the company consider?", "options": ["Clustering servers", "Geographic dispersion", "Load balancers", "Off-site backups"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL41", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Jailbreaking is a primary security concern for a company setting up a BYOD (Bring Your Own\nDevice) program. Jailbreaking is the process of removing the manufacturer's or the carrier's\nrestrictions on a device, such as a smartphone or a tablet, to gain root access and install\nunauthorized or custom software. Jailbreaking can compromise the security of the device and the\ndata stored on it, as well as expose it to malware, viruses, or hacking. Jailbreaking can also\nviolate the warranty and the terms of service of the device, and make it incompatible with the\ncompany's security policies and standards. Therefore, a company setting up a BYOD program\nshould prohibit jailbreaking and enforce device compliance and encryption.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a primary security concern for a company setting up a BYOD program?", "options": ["End of life", "Buffer overflow", "VM escape", "Jailbreaking"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL42", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A company decided to reduce the cost of its annual cyber insurance policy by removing the\ncoverage for ransomware attacks.\nWhich of the following analysis elements did the company most likely use in making this\ndecision?", "options": ["IMTTR", "RTO", "ARO", "MTBF"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL43", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the most likely to be included as an element of communication in a\nsecurity awareness program?", "options": ["Reporting phishing attempts or other suspicious activities", "Detecting insider threats using anomalous behavior recognition", "Verifying information when modifying wire transfer data", "Performing social engineering as part of third-party penetration testing"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL46", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A buffer overflow is a vulnerability that occurs when an application writes more data to a memory\nbuffer than it can hold, causing the excess data to overwrite adjacent memory locations. A\nregister is a small storage area in the CPU that holds temporary data or instructions. An attacker\ncan exploit a buffer overflow to overwrite a register with a malicious address that points to a\nshellcode, which is a piece of code that gives the attacker control over the system. By doing so,\nthe attacker can bypass the normal execution flow of the application and execute arbitrary\ncommands.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following vulnerabilities is exploited when an attacker overwrites a register with a\nmalicious address?", "options": ["VM escape", "SQL injection", "Buffer overflow", "Race condition"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL47", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "The device is STILL running a critical application. therefore it needs to be connected to the\nnetwork. a compensating mechanism for this scenario would be segmentation as this would limit\nthe ability of an attacker to pivot from the vulnerable server to the rest of the network.as possible.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following would be the best way to handle a critical business application that is\nrunning on a legacy server?", "options": ["Segmentation", "Isolation", "Hardening", "Decommissioning"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL48", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Steganography is the process of hiding information within another medium, such as an image,\naudio, video, or text file. The hidden information is not visible or noticeable to the casual observer,\nand can only be extracted by using a specific technique or key. Steganography can be used for\nvarious purposes, such as concealing secret messages, watermarking, or evading detection by\nantivirus software.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following describes the process of concealing code or text inside a graphical image?", "options": ["Symmetric encryption", "Hashing", "Data masking", "Steganography"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL49", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A legal hold (also known as a litigation hold) is a notification sent from an organization's legal\nteam to employees instructing them not to delete electronically stored information (ESI) or discard\npaper documents that may be relevant to a new or imminent legal case. A legal hold is intended\nto preserve evidence and prevent spoliation, which is the intentional or negligent destruction of\nevidence that could harm a party's case. A legal hold can be triggered by various events, such as\na lawsuit, a regulatory investigation, or a subpoena.\nIn this scenario, the company's attorneys have requested that the security team initiate a legal\nhold in response to the lawsuit filed by the customers after the company was compromised. This\nmeans that the security team will most likely be required to retain any communications related to\nthe security breach until further notice. This could include emails, instant messages, reports, logs,\nmemos, or any other documents that could be relevant to the lawsuit. The security team should\nalso inform the relevant custodians (the employees who have access to or control over the ESI)\nof their preservation obligations and monitor their compliance. The security team should also\ndocument the legal hold process and its scope, as well as take steps to protect the ESI from\nalteration, deletion, or loss.", "type": "mc", "q": "After a company was compromised, customers initiated a lawsuit. The company's attorneys have\nrequested that the security team initiate a legal hold in response to the lawsuit. Which of the\nfollowing describes the action the security team will most likely be required to take?", "options": ["Retain the emails between the security team and affected customers for 30 days.", "Retain any communications related to the security breach until further notice.", "Retain any communications between security members during the breach response.", "Retain all emails from the company to affected customers for an indefinite period of time."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL50", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A network manager wants to protect the company's VPN by implementing multifactor\nauthentication that uses:\n- Something you know\n- Something you have\n- Something you are\nWhich of the following would accomplish the manager's goal?", "options": ["Domain name, PKI, GeolP lookup", "VPN IP address, company ID, facial structure", "Password, authentication token, thumbprint", "Company URL, TLS certificate, home address"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL51", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A tabletop exercise is a simulated scenario that tests the effectiveness of a security incident\nresponse plan. It involves gathering the relevant stakeholders and walking through the steps of\nthe plan, identifying any gaps or issues that need to be addressed. A tabletop exercise is a good\nway to validate the documentation created by the security manager and ensure that the team is\nprepared for various types of security incidents.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security manager created new documentation to use in response to various types of security\nincidents. Which of the following is the next step the manager should take?", "options": ["Set the maximum data retention policy.", "Securely store the documents on an air-gapped network.", "Review the documents' data classification policy.", "Conduct a tabletop exercise with the team."], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL52", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A content filter is a device or software that blocks or allows access to web content based on\npredefined rules or categories. In this case, the new retail website is mistakenly categorized as\ngambling by the content filter, which prevents users from accessing it. To resolve this issue, the\ncontent filter's categorization needs to be updated to reflect the correct category of the website,\n\n\nsuch as shopping or retail. This will allow the content filter to allow access to the website instead\nof blocking it.", "type": "mc", "q": "Users at a company are reporting they are unable to access the URL for a new retail website\nbecause it is flagged as gambling and is being blocked.\nWhich of the following changes would allow users to access the site?", "options": ["Creating a firewall rule to allow HTTPS traffic", "Configuring the IPS to allow shopping", "Tuning the DLP rule that detects credit card data", "Updating the categorization in the content filter"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL53", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "An insider threat is a type of attack that originates from someone who has legitimate access to an\norganization's network, systems, or data. In this case, the domain user who encrypted the files on\nthe database server is an example of an insider threat, as they abused their access privileges to\ncause harm to the organization. Insider threats can be motivated by various factors, such as\nfinancial gain, revenge, espionage, or sabotage.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator discovers that some files on a database server were recently encrypted. The\nadministrator sees from the security logs that the data was last accessed by a domain user.\nWhich of the following best describes the type of attack that occurred?", "options": ["Insider threat", "Social engineering", "Watering-hole", "Unauthorized attacker"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL54", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Disabling access is an automation use case that would best enhance the security posture of an\norganization by rapidly updating permissions when employees leave a company. Disabling\naccess is the process of revoking or suspending the access rights of a user account, such as\nlogin credentials, email, VPN, cloud services, etc. Disabling access can prevent unauthorized or\nmalicious use of the account by former employees or attackers who may have compromised the\naccount. Disabling access can also reduce the attack surface and the risk of data breaches or\nleaks. Disabling access can be automated by using scripts, tools, or workflows that can trigger\nthe action based on predefined events, such as employee termination, resignation, or transfer.\nAutomation can ensure that the access is disabled in a timely, consistent, and efficient manner,\nwithout relying on manual intervention or human error.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following automation use cases would best enhance the security posture of an\norganization by rapidly updating permissions when employees leave a company?", "options": ["Provisioning resources", "Disabling access", "Reviewing change approvals", "Escalating permission requests"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL55", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "To provide employees with computers that do not have access to the internet and prevent\ninformation leaks to an online forum, implementing an air gap would be the best solution. An air\ngap physically isolates the computer or network from any outside connections, including the\ninternet, ensuring that data cannot be transferred to or from the system.\nAir gap: A security measure that isolates a computer or network from the internet or other\nnetworks, preventing any form of electronic communication with external systems.\nJump server: A secure server used to access and manage devices in a different security zone,\nbut it does not provide isolation from the internet.\nLogical segmentation: Segregates networks using software or network configurations, but it does\nnot guarantee complete isolation from the internet.\nVirtualization: Creates virtual instances of systems, which can be isolated, but does not inherently\nprevent internet access without additional configurations.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company would like to provide employees with computers that do not have access to the\ninternet in order to prevent information from being leaked to an online forum. Which of the\nfollowing would be best for the systems administrator to implement?", "options": ["Air gap", "Jump server", "Logical segmentation", "Virtualization"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL56", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Masking is a method to secure credit card data that involves replacing some or all of the digits\nwith symbols, such as asterisks, dashes, or Xs, while leaving some of the original digits visible.\nMasking is best to use when a requirement is to see only the last four numbers on a credit card,\nas it can prevent unauthorized access to the full card number, while still allowing identification\nand verification of the cardholder. Masking does not alter the original data, unlike encryption,\nhashing, or tokenization, which use algorithms to transform the data into different formats.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following methods to secure credit card data is best to use when a requirement is to\nsee only the last four numbers on a credit card?", "options": ["Encryption", "Hashing", "Masking", "Tokenization"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL57", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Ransomware is a type of malware that encrypts the victim's files and demands a ransom for the\ndecryption key. The ransomware usually displays a message on the infected system with\ninstructions on how to pay the ransom and recover the files. The .ryk extension is associated with\na ransomware variant called Ryuk, which targets large organizations and demands high ransoms.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator finds that all user workstations and servers are displaying a message that is\nassociated with files containing an extension of .ryk. Which of the following types of infections is\npresent on the systems?", "options": ["Virus", "Trojan", "Spyware", "Ransomware"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL58", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Availability is the ability of a system or service to be accessible and usable when needed. For a\nweb application that allows individuals to digitally report health emergencies, availability is the\nmost important consideration during development, because any downtime or delay could have\nserious consequences for the health and safety of the users. The web application should be\ndesigned to handle high traffic, prevent denial-of-service attacks, and have backup and recovery\nplans in case of failures.", "type": "mc", "q": "A healthcare organization wants to provide a web application that allows individuals to digitally\nreport health emergencies.\nWhich of the following is the most important consideration during development?", "options": ["Scalability", "Availability", "Cost", "Ease of deployment"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL59", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A regulatory requirement is a mandate imposed by a government or an authority that must be\nfollowed by an organization or an individual. In a banking environment, audits are often required\nby regulators to ensure compliance with laws, standards, and policies related to security, privacy,\nand financial reporting. Audits help to identify and correct any gaps or weaknesses in the security\nposture and the internal controls of the organization.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the best reason to complete an audit in a banking environment?", "options": ["Regulatory requirement", "Organizational change", "Self-assessment requirement", "Service-level requirement"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL60", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Data classification is the process of assigning labels or tags to data based on its sensitivity, value,\nand risk. Data classification is the first step in a data loss prevention (DLP) solution, as it helps to\nidentify what data needs to be protected and how. By applying classifications to the data, the\nsecurity administrator can define appropriate policies and rules for the DLP solution to prevent the\nexfiltration of sensitive customer data.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator is deploying a DLP solution to prevent the exfiltration of sensitive\ncustomer data. Which of the following should the administrator do first?", "options": ["Block access to cloud storage websites.", "Create a rule to block outgoing email attachments.", "Apply classifications to the data.", "Remove all user permissions from shares on the file server."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL61", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "SIEM stands for Security Information and Event Management. It is a security alerting and\nmonitoring tool that collects system, application, and network logs from multiple sources in a\ncentralized system. SIEM can analyze the collected data, correlate events, generate alerts, and\nprovide reports and dashboards. SIEM can also integrate with other security tools and support\ncompliance requirements. SIEM helps organizations to detect and respond to cyber threats,\nimprove security posture, and reduce operational costs.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following describes a security alerting and monitoring tool that collects system,\napplication, and network logs from multiple sources in a centralized system?", "options": ["SIEM", "DLP", "IDS", "SNMP"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL62", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "B. The device is configured to use cleartext passwords. This is a major security vulnerability and\nposes a significant risk of unauthorized access. Devices using cleartext passwords should be\ndecommissioned and replaced with devices using secure authentication methods.\nE. The device's encryption level cannot meet organizational standards. If the device cannot\nencrypt data to the required level, it compromises the confidentiality of sensitive information and\nshould be decommissioned. Organizational security policies should dictate the minimum\nacceptable encryption level for network devices.", "type": "multi", "q": "Which of the following are cases in which an engineer should recommend the decommissioning\nof a network device? (Select two).", "options": ["The device has been moved from a production environment to a test environment.", "The device is configured to use cleartext passwords.", "The device is moved to an isolated segment on the enterprise network.", "The device is moved to a different location in the enterprise.", "The device's encryption level cannot meet organizational standards.", "The device is unable to receive authorized updates."], "answer": [1, 4]}, {"id": "PL63", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A data retention policy is a set of rules that defines how long data should be stored and when it\nshould be deleted or archived. An administrator assists the legal and compliance team with\nensuring information about customer transactions is archived for the proper time period by\nfollowing the data retention policy of the organization. This policy helps the organization to comply\nwith legal and regulatory requirements, optimize storage space, and protect data privacy and\nsecurity.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator assists the legal and compliance team with ensuring information about customer\ntransactions is archived for the proper time period. Which of the following data policies is the\nadministrator carrying out?", "options": ["Compromise", "Retention", "Analysis", "Transfer", "Inventory"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL64", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Zero Trust is a security model that assumes no trust for any entity inside or outside the network\nperimeter and requires continuous verification of identity and permissions. Zero Trust can provide\na secure zone by isolating and protecting sensitive data and resources from unauthorized access.\nZero Trust can also enforce a company-wide access control policy by applying the principle of\nleast privilege and granular segmentation for users, devices, and applications. Zero Trust can\nreduce the scope of threats by preventing lateral movement and minimizing the attack surface.", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator is working on a solution with the following requirements:\n- Provide a secure zone.\n- Enforce a company-wide access control policy.\n- Reduce the scope of threats.\nWhich of the following is the systems administrator setting up?", "options": ["Zero Trust", "AAA", "Non-repudiation", "CIA"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL65", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "FIM stands for File Integrity Monitoring, which is a method to secure data by detecting any\nchanges or modifications to files, directories, or registry keys. FIM can help a security\nadministrator track any unauthorized or malicious changes to the data, as well as verify the\nintegrity and compliance of the data. FIM can also alert the administrator of any potential\nbreaches or incidents involving the data.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator needs a method to secure data in an environment that includes some\nform of checks so that the administrator can track any changes. Which of the following should the\nadministrator set up to achieve this goal?", "options": ["SPF", "GPO", "NAC", "FIM"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL66", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Preparation is the phase in the incident response process when a security analyst reviews roles\nand responsibilities, as well as the policies and procedures for handling incidents. Preparation\nalso involves gathering and maintaining the necessary tools, resources, and contacts for\nresponding to incidents. Preparation can help a security analyst to be ready and proactive when\nan incident occurs, as well as to reduce the impact and duration of the incident. Some of the\nactivities that a security analyst performs during the preparation phase are:", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the phase in the incident response process when a security analyst\n\n\nreviews roles and responsibilities?", "options": ["Preparation", "Recovery", "Lessons learned", "Analysis"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL67", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The company should request a certification from the vendor that confirms the storage array has\nbeen disposed of securely and in compliance with the company's policies and standards. A\ncertification provides evidence that the vendor has followed the proper procedures and methods\nto destroy the classified data and prevent unauthorized access or recovery. A certification may\nalso include details such as the date, time, location, and method of disposal, as well as the\nnames and signatures of the personnel involved.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is discarding a classified storage array and hires an outside vendor to complete the\ndisposal. Which of the following should the company request from the vendor?", "options": ["Certification", "Inventory list", "Classification", "Proof of ownership"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL68", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Badge access and access control vestibule are two of the best ways to ensure only authorized\npersonnel can access a secure facility. Badge access requires the personnel to present a valid\nand authenticated badge to a reader or scanner that grants or denies access based on\npredefined rules and permissions. Access control vestibule is a physical security measure that\nconsists of a small room or chamber with two doors, one leading to the outside and one leading\n\n\nto the secure area. The personnel must enter the vestibule and wait for the first door to close and\nlock before the second door can be opened. This prevents tailgating or piggybacking by\nunauthorized individuals.", "type": "multi", "q": "Which of the following would be the best ways to ensure only authorized personnel can access a\nsecure facility? (Select two).", "options": ["Fencing", "Video surveillance", "Badge access", "Access control vestibule", "Sign-in sheet", "Sensor"], "answer": [2, 3]}, {"id": "PL69", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A company's marketing department collects, modifies, and stores sensitive customer data. The\ninfrastructure team is responsible for securing the data while in transit and at rest. Which of the\nfollowing data roles describes the customer?", "options": ["Processor", "Custodian", "Subject", "Owner"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL70", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A watering-hole attack is a type of cyberattack that targets groups of users by infecting websites\nthat they commonly visit. The attackers exploit vulnerabilities to deliver a malicious payload to the\norganization's network. The attack aims to infect users' computers and gain access to a\nconnected corporate network. The attackers target websites known to be popular among\nmembers of a particular organization or demographic. The attack differs from phishing and spearphishing attacks, which typically attempt to steal data or install malware onto users' devices1 In\nthis scenario, the compromised industry blog is the watering hole that the attackers used to\nspread malware across the company's network. The attackers likely chose this blog because they\nknew that the employees of the company were interested in its content and visited it frequently.\nThe attackers may have injected malicious code into the blog or redirected the visitors to a\nspoofed website that hosted the malware. The malware then infected the employees' computers\nand propagated to the network.", "type": "mc", "q": "Malware spread across a company's network after an employee visited a compromised industry\nblog. Which of the following best describes this type of attack?", "options": ["Impersonation", "Disinformation", "Watering-hole", "Smishing"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL71", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Detective controls are security measures that are designed to identify and monitor any malicious\nactivity or anomalies on a system or network. They can help to discover the source, scope, and\nimpact of an attack, and provide evidence for further analysis or investigation. Detective controls\ninclude log files, security audits, intrusion detection systems, network monitoring tools, and\nantivirus software. In this case, the administrator used log files as a detective control to review the\nransomware attack on the company's system. Log files are records of events and activities that\noccur on a system or network, such as user actions, system errors, network traffic, and security\nalerts. They can provide valuable information for troubleshooting, auditing, and forensics.", "type": "mc", "q": "After a recent ransomware attack on a company's system, an administrator reviewed the log files.\nWhich of the following control types did the administrator use?", "options": ["Compensating", "Detective", "Preventive", "Corrective"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL72", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A service level agreement (SLA) is a type of agreement that defines the expectations and\nresponsibilities between a service provider and a customer. It usually includes the quality,\navailability, and performance metrics of the service, as well as the time frame in which the\nprovider needs to respond to service requests, incidents, or complaints. An SLA can help ensure\nthat the customer receives the desired level of service and that the provider is accountable for\nmeeting the agreed-upon standards.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following agreement types defines the time frame in which a vendor needs to\nrespond?", "options": ["SOW", "SLA", "MOA", "MOU"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL73", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Full packet capture is a technique that records all network traffic passing through a device, such\nas a router or firewall. It allows for detailed analysis and investigation of network events, such as\nSQLi attacks, by providing the complete content and context of the packets. Full packet capture\ncan help identify the source, destination, payload, and timing of an SQLi attack, as well as the\nimpact on the server and database. Logging NetFlow traffic, network traffic sensors, and endpoint\nand OS-specific security logs can provide some information about network activity, but they do\nnot capture the full content of the packets, which may limit the scope and depth of the\ninvestigation.", "type": "mc", "q": "A Chief Information Security Officer wants to monitor the company's servers for SQLi attacks and\nallow for comprehensive investigations if an attack occurs. The company uses SSL decryption to\nallow traffic monitoring. Which of the following strategies would best accomplish this goal?", "options": ["Logging all NetFlow traffic into a SIEM", "Deploying network traffic sensors on the same subnet as the servers", "Logging endpoint and OS-specific security logs", "Enabling full packet capture for traffic entering and exiting the servers"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL74", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A service level agreement (SLA) is a document that defines the level of service expected by a\ncustomer from a service provider, indicating the metrics by which that service is measured, and\nthe remedies or penalties, if any, should the agreed-upon levels not be achieved. An SLA can\nspecify the minimum uptime or availability of a service, such as 99.99%, and the consequences\nfor failing to meet that standard. A memorandum of agreement (MOA), a statement of work\n(SOW), and a memorandum of understanding (MOU) are other types of documents that can be\nused to establish a relationship between parties, but they do not typically include the details of\nservice levels and performance metrics that an SLA does.", "type": "mc", "q": "A client demands at least 99.99% uptime from a service provider's hosted security services.\nWhich of the following documents includes the information the service provider should return to\nthe client?", "options": ["MOA", "SOW", "MOU", "SLA"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL75", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Jailbreaking is the process of removing the restrictions imposed by the manufacturer or carrier on\na mobile device, such as an iPhone or iPad. Jailbreaking allows users to install unauthorized\napplications, modify system settings, and access root privileges. However, jailbreaking also\nexposes the device to potential security risks, such as malware, spyware, unauthorized access,\ndata loss, and voided warranty. Therefore, an organization may prohibit employees from\njailbreaking their mobile devices to prevent these vulnerabilities and protect the corporate data\nand network.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is adding a clause to its AUP that states employees are not allowed to modify the\noperating system on mobile devices. Which of the following vulnerabilities is the organization\naddressing?", "options": ["Cross-site scripting", "Buffer overflow", "Jailbreaking", "Side loading"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL76", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Peer review and approval is a practice that involves having other developers or experts review\nthe code before it is deployed or released. Peer review and approval can help detect and prevent\nmalicious code, errors, bugs, vulnerabilities, and poor quality in the development process. Peer\nreview and approval can also enforce coding standards, best practices, and compliance\n\n\nrequirements. Peer review and approval can be done manually or with the help of tools, such as\ncode analysis, code review, and code signing.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following practices would be best to prevent an insider from introducing malicious\ncode into a company's development process?", "options": ["Code scanning for vulnerabilities", "Open-source component usage", "Quality assurance testing", "Peer review and approval"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL77", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Orchestration is the process of automating multiple tasks across different systems and\napplications. It can help save time and reduce human error by executing predefined workflows\nand scripts. In this case, the systems administrator can use orchestration to create accounts for a\nlarge number of end users without having to manually enter their information and assign\npermissions.", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator is creating a script that would save time and prevent human error when\nperforming account creation for a large number of end users. Which of the following would be a\ngood use case for this task?", "options": ["Off-the-shelf software", "Orchestration", "Baseline", "Policy enforcement"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL78", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Access control lists (ACLs) are rules that specify which users or groups can access which\nresources on a file server. They can help restrict access to confidential data by granting or\ndenying permissions based on the identity or role of the user. In this case, the administrator can\nuse ACLs to quickly modify the access rights of the users and prevent them from accessing the\ndata they are not authorized to see.", "type": "mc", "q": "After an audit, an administrator discovers all users have access to confidential data on a file\nserver. Which of the following should the administrator use to restrict access to the data quickly?", "options": ["Group Policy", "Content filtering", "Data loss prevention", "Access control lists"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL79", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Ransomware-as-a-service is a type of cybercrime where hackers sell or rent ransomware tools or\n\n\nservices to other criminals who use them to launch attacks and extort money from victims. This is\na typical example of organized crime, which is a group of criminals who work together to conduct\nillegal activities for profit. Organized crime is different from other types of threat actors, such as\ninsider threats, hacktivists, or nation-states, who may have different motives, methods, or targets.", "type": "mc", "q": "A Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) wants to explicitly raise awareness about the increase\nof ransomware-as-a-service in a report to the management team. Which of the following best\ndescribes the threat actor in the CISO's report?", "options": ["Insider threat", "Hacktivist", "Nation-state", "Organized crime"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL80", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "End-of-life operating systems are those that are no longer supported by the vendor or\nmanufacturer, meaning they do not receive any security updates or patches. This makes them\nvulnerable to exploits and attacks that take advantage of known or unknown flaws in the software.\nPatch availability is the security implication of using end-of-life operating systems, as it affects the\nability to fix or prevent security issues. Other factors, such as product software compatibility, ease\nof recovery, or cost of replacement, are not directly related to security, but rather to functionality,\navailability, or budget.", "type": "mc", "q": "A small business uses kiosks on the sales floor to display product information for customers. A\nsecurity team discovers the kiosks use end-of-life operating systems. Which of the following is the\nsecurity team most likely to document as a security implication of the current architecture?", "options": ["Patch availability", "Product software compatibility", "Ease of recovery", "Cost of replacement"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL81", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Data classification is the process of assigning labels to data based on its sensitivity and business\nimpact. Different organizations and sectors may have different data classification schemes, but a\ncommon one is the following:\nPublic: Data that can be freely disclosed to anyone without any harm or risk. Private: Data that is\nintended for internal use only and may cause some harm or risk if disclosed.\nConfidential: Data that is intended for authorized use only and may cause significant harm or risk\nif disclosed.\nRestricted: Data that is intended for very limited use only and may cause severe harm or risk if\ndisclosed.\nIn this scenario, the company is developing a critical system for the government and storing\nproject information on a fileshare. This data is likely to be classified as confidential and restricted,\nbecause it is not meant for public or private use, and it may cause serious damage to national\nsecurity or public safety if disclosed. The government may also have specific requirements or\n\n\nregulations for handling such data, such as encryption, access control, and auditing.", "type": "multi", "q": "A company is developing a critical system for the government and storing project information on a\nfileshare. Which of the following describes how this data will most likely be classified? (Select\ntwo).", "options": ["Private", "Confidential", "Public", "Operational", "Urgent", "Restricted"], "answer": [1, 5]}, {"id": "PL82", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "After reviewing the following vulnerability scanning report:\n\nA security analyst performs the following test:\n\nWhich of the following would the security analyst conclude for this reported vulnerability?", "options": ["It is a false positive.", "A rescan is required.", "It is considered noise.", "Compensating controls exist."], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL83", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "IPSec is a protocol suite that provides secure communication over IP networks. IPSec can be\nused to create virtual private networks (VPNs) that encrypt and authenticate the data exchanged\nbetween two or more parties. IPSec can also provide data integrity, confidentiality, replay\nprotection, and access control. A security consultant can use IPSec to gain secure, remote\naccess to a client environment by establishing a VPN tunnel with the client's network.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security consultant needs secure, remote access to a client environment. Which of the following\nshould the security consultant most likely use to gain access?", "options": ["EAP", "DHCP", "IPSec", "NAT"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL84", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Scheduled downtime is a planned period of time when a system or service is unavailable for\nmaintenance, updates, upgrades, or other changes. Scheduled downtime gives administrators a\nset period to perform changes to an operational system without disrupting the normal business\noperations or affecting the availability of the system or service. Scheduled downtime also allows\nadministrators to inform the users and stakeholders about the expected duration and impact of\nthe changes.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following best practices gives administrators a set period to perform changes to an\noperational system to ensure availability and minimize business impacts?", "options": ["Impact analysis", "Scheduled downtime", "Backout plan", "Change management boards"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL85", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Endpoint management software is a tool that allows security engineers to monitor and control the\nconfiguration, security, and performance of workstations and servers from a central console.\nEndpoint management software can help detect and prevent unauthorized changes and software\ninstallations, enforce policies and compliance, and provide reports and alerts on the status of the\nendpoints. The other options are not as effective or comprehensive as endpoint management\nsoftware for this purpose.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following actions could a security engineer take to ensure workstations and servers\nare properly monitored for unauthorized changes and software?", "options": ["Configure all systems to log scheduled tasks.", "Collect and monitor all traffic exiting the network.", "Block traffic based on known malicious signatures.", "Install endpoint management software on all systems."], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL86", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Social engineering is the practice of manipulating people into performing actions or divulging\nconfidential information, often by impersonating someone else or creating a sense of urgency or\n\n\ntrust. The suspicious caller in this scenario was trying to use social engineering to trick the user\ninto giving away credit card information by pretending to be the CFO and asking for a payment.\nThe user recognized this as a potential scam and reported it to the IT help desk. The other topics\nare not relevant to this situation.", "type": "mc", "q": "After a security awareness training session, a user called the IT help desk and reported a\nsuspicious call. The suspicious caller stated that the Chief Financial Officer wanted credit card\ninformation in order to close an invoice. Which of the following topics did the user recognize from\nthe training?", "options": ["Insider threat", "Email phishing", "Social engineering", "Executive whaling"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL87", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A tabletop exercise is a simulated scenario that tests the organization's incident response plan\nand procedures. It involves key stakeholders and decision-makers who discuss their roles and\nactions in response to a hypothetical incident. It can help identify gaps, weaknesses, and\nimprovement areas in the incident response process. It can also enhance communication,\ncoordination, and collaboration among the participants.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following exercises should an organization use to improve its incident response\nprocess?", "options": ["Tabletop", "Replication", "Failover", "Recovery"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL88", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "OCSP stands for Online Certificate Status Protocol. It is a protocol that allows applications to\ncheck the revocation status of a certificate in real-time. It works by sending a query to an OCSP\nresponder, which is a server that maintains a database of revoked certificates. The OCSP\nresponder returns a response that indicates whether the certificate is valid, revoked, or unknown.\nOCSP is faster and more efficient than downloading and parsing Certificate Revocation Lists\n(CRLs), which are large files that contain the serial numbers of all revoked certificates issued by a\nCertificate Authority (CA).", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is used to validate a certificate when it is presented to a user?", "options": ["OCSP", "CSR", "CA", "CRC"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL89", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Segmentation is a technique that divides a network into smaller subnetworks or segments, each\nwith its own security policies and controls. Segmentation can help mitigate network access\nvulnerabilities in legacy loT devices by isolating them from other devices and systems, reducing\ntheir attack surface and limiting the potential impact of a breach. Segmentation can also improve\nnetwork performance and efficiency by reducing congestion and traffic. Patching, insurance, and\nreplacement are other possible strategies to deal with network access vulnerabilities, but they\nmay not be feasible or effective in the short term. Patching may not be available or compatible for\nlegacy loT devices, insurance may not cover the costs or damages of a cyberattack, and\nreplacement may be expensive and time-consuming.", "type": "mc", "q": "A newly identified network access vulnerability has been found in the OS of legacy IoT devices.\nWhich of the following would best mitigate this vulnerability quickly?", "options": ["Insurance", "Patching", "Segmentation", "Replacement"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL90", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Encryption at rest is a strategy that protects data stored on a device, such as a laptop, by\nconverting it into an unreadable format that can only be accessed with a decryption key or\npassword. Encryption at rest can prevent data loss on stolen laptops by preventing unauthorized\naccess to the data, even if the device is physically compromised. Encryption at rest can also help\ncomply with data privacy regulations and standards that require data protection. Masking, data\nclassification, and permission restrictions are other strategies that can help protect data, but they\nmay not be sufficient or applicable for data stored on laptops. Masking is a technique that\nobscures sensitive data elements, such as credit card numbers, with random characters or\nsymbols, but it is usually used for data in transit or in use, not at rest. Data classification is a\nprocess that assigns labels to data based on its sensitivity and business impact, but it does not\nprotect the data itself. Permission restrictions are rules that define who can access, modify, or\ndelete data, but they may not prevent unauthorized access if the laptop is stolen and the security\ncontrols are bypassed.", "type": "mc", "q": "A bank insists all of its vendors must prevent data loss on stolen laptops. Which of the following\nstrategies is the bank requiring?", "options": ["Encryption at rest", "Masking", "Data classification", "Permission restrictions"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL91", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Containers are a method of virtualization that allows applications to run in isolated environments\nwith their own dependencies, libraries, and configurations. Containers are best suited for\nconstantly changing environments because they are lightweight, portable, scalable, and easy to\ndeploy and update. Containers can also support microservices architectures, which enable faster\nand more frequent delivery of software features.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following would be best suited for constantly changing environments?", "options": ["RTOS", "Containers", "Embedded systems", "SCADA"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL92", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A VPN is a virtual private network that creates a secure tunnel between two or more devices over\na public network. A VPN can encrypt and authenticate the data, as well as hide the IP addresses\nand locations of the devices. A jump server is a server that acts as an intermediary between a\nuser and a target server, such as a production server. A jump server can provide an additional\nlayer of security and access control, as well as logging and auditing capabilities. A firewall is a\ndevice or software that filters and blocks unwanted network traffic based on predefined rules. A\nfirewall can protect the internal network from external threats and limit the exposure of sensitive\nservices and ports. A security analyst should recommend setting up a VPN and placing the jump\nserver inside the firewall to improve the security of the remote desktop access to the production\nnetwork. This way, the remote desktop service will not be exposed to the public network, and only\nauthorized users with VPN credentials can access the jump server and then the production\nserver.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst scans a company's public network and discovers a host is running a remote\ndesktop that can be used to access the production network. Which of the following changes\nshould the security analyst recommend?", "options": ["Changing the remote desktop port to a non-standard number", "Setting up a VPN and placing the jump server inside the firewall", "Using a proxy for web connections from the remote desktop server", "Connecting the remote server to the domain and increasing the password length"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL93", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "SQL injection is a type of attack that exploits a database misconfiguration or a flaw in the\napplication code that interacts with the database. An attacker can inject malicious SQL\nstatements into the user input fields or the URL parameters that are sent to the database server.\nThese statements can then execute unauthorized commands, such as reading, modifying,\ndeleting, or creating data, or even taking over the database server. SQL injection can\ncompromise the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the data and the system.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following involves an attempt to take advantage of database misconfigurations?", "options": ["Buffer overflow", "SQL injection", "VM escape", "Memory injection"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL94", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Segmentation is a network design technique that divides the network into smaller and isolated\nsegments based on logical or physical boundaries. Segmentation can help improve network\nsecurity by limiting the scope of an attack, reducing the attack surface, and enforcing access\ncontrol policies. Segmentation can also enhance network performance, scalability, and\nmanageability. To accomplish the goal of storing customer data on a separate part of the\nnetwork, the administrator can use segmentation technologies such as subnetting, VLANs,\nfirewalls, routers, or switches.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization would like to store customer data on a separate part of the network that is not\naccessible to users on the main corporate network. Which of the following should the\nadministrator use to accomplish this goal?", "options": ["Segmentation", "Isolation", "Patching", "Encryption"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL95", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "CVSS stands for Common Vulnerability Scoring System, which is a framework that provides a\nstandardized way to assess and communicate the severity and risk of vulnerabilities. CVSS uses\na set of metrics and formulas to calculate a numerical score ranging from 0 to 10, where higher\nscores indicate higher criticality. CVSS can help organizations prioritize remediation efforts and\ncompare vulnerabilities across different systems and vendors. The other options are not used to\nmeasure the criticality of a vulnerability, but rather to identify, classify, or report them.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is used to quantitatively measure the criticality of a vulnerability?", "options": ["CVE", "CVSS", "CIA", "CERT"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL96", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A technician is opening ports on a firewall for a new system being deployed and supported by a\nSaaS provider. Which of the following is a risk in the new system?", "options": ["Default credentials", "Non-segmented network", "Supply chain vendor", "Vulnerable software"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL97", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Confidentiality is the security concept that ensures data is protected from unauthorized access or\ndisclosure. The principle of least privilege is a technique that grants users or systems the\nminimum level of access or permissions that they need to perform their tasks, and nothing more.\n\n\nBy applying the principle of least privilege to a human resources fileshare, the permissions can be\nrestricted to only those who have a legitimate need to access the sensitive data, such as HR\nstaff, managers, or auditors. This can prevent unauthorized users, such as hackers, employees,\nor contractors, from accessing, copying, modifying, or deleting the data. Therefore, the principle\nof least privilege can enhance the confidentiality of the data on the fileshare. Integrity, availability,\nand non-repudiation are other security concepts, but they are not the best reason for permissions\non a human resources fileshare to follow the principle of least privilege. Integrity is the security\nconcept that ensures data is accurate and consistent, and protected from unauthorized\nmodification or corruption. Availability is the security concept that ensures data is accessible and\nusable by authorized users or systems when needed. Non-repudiation is the security concept that\nensures the authenticity and accountability of data and actions, and prevents the denial of\ninvolvement or responsibility. While these concepts are also important for data security, they are\nnot directly related to the level of access or permissions granted to users or systems.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following security concepts is the best reason for permissions on a human resources\nfileshare to follow the principle of least privilege?", "options": ["Integrity", "Availability", "Confidentiality", "Non-repudiation"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL98", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Safety controls are security controls that are designed to protect human life and physical assets\nfrom harm or damage. Examples of safety controls include fire alarms, sprinklers, emergency\nexits, backup generators, and surge protectors. Safety controls should fail open, which means\nthat they should remain operational or allow access when a failure or error occurs. Failing open\ncan prevent or minimize the impact of a disaster, such as a fire, flood, earthquake, or power\noutage, on human life and physical assets. For example, if a fire alarm fails, it should still trigger\nthe sprinklers and unlock the emergency exits, rather than remain silent and locked. Failing open\ncan also ensure that essential services, such as healthcare, transportation, or communication,\nare available during a crisis.
