Which of the following protocols should be implemented to best meet this\nobjective?", "options": ["SSH", "SRTP", "S/MIME", "PPTP"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL259", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The scenario suggests that only the employees who used the kiosks inside the building had their\ncredentials compromised. Since the time-keeping website is accessible from the internet, it is\npossible that a malicious actor exploited an unpatched vulnerability in the site, allowing them to\ninject malicious code that captured the credentials of those who logged in from the kiosks. This is\na common attack vector for stealing credentials from web applications.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst discovers that a large number of employee credentials had been stolen and\nwere being sold on the dark web. The analyst investigates and discovers that some hourly\nemployee credentials were compromised, but salaried employee credentials were not affected.\nMost employees clocked in and out while they were Inside the building using one of the kiosks\nconnected to the network. However, some clocked out and recorded their time after leaving to go\nhome. Only those who clocked in and out while Inside the building had credentials stolen. Each of\nthe kiosks are on different floors, and there are multiple routers, since the business segments\nenvironments for certain business functions.\nHourly employees are required to use a website called acmetimekeeping.com to clock in and out.\nThis website is accessible from the internet. Which of the following Is the most likely reason for\nthis compromise?", "options": ["A brute-force attack was used against the time-keeping website to scan for common passwords.", "A malicious actor compromised the time-keeping website with malicious code using an unpatched vulnerability on the site, stealing the credentials.", "The internal DNS servers were poisoned and were redirecting acmetimkeeping.com to malicious domain that intercepted the credentials and then passed them through to the real site.", "ARP poisoning affected the machines in the building and caused the kiosks lo send a copy of all the submitted credentials to a machine."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL260", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The presence of another device providing internet access that bypasses the content filtering\nsystem indicates the existence of a rogue access point. Rogue access points are unauthorized\ndevices that can create a backdoor into the network, allowing users to bypass security controls\nlike content filtering. This presents a significant security risk as it can expose the network to\nunauthorized access and potential data breaches.", "type": "mc", "q": "A business uses Wi-Fi with content filleting enabled. An employee noticed a coworker accessed a\nblocked sue from a work computer and repotted the issue. While Investigating the issue, a\nsecurity administrator found another device providing internet access to certain employees.\nWhich of the following best describes the security risk?", "options": ["The host-based security agent Is not running on all computers.", "A rogue access point Is allowing users to bypass controls.", "Employees who have certain credentials are using a hidden SSID.", "A valid access point is being jammed to limit availability."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL261", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Shadow IT refers to the use of information technology systems, devices, software, applications,\nand services without explicit IT department approval. This is the most likely cause of introducing\nvulnerabilities on a corporate network by deploying unapproved software, as such software may\nnot have been vetted for security compliance, increasing the risk of vulnerabilities.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is most likely associated with introducing vulnerabilities on a corporate\nnetwork by the deployment of unapproved software?", "options": ["Hacktivists", "Script kiddies", "Competitors", "Shadow IT"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL262", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) provides a structured approach to standardizing and aligning\nsecurity programs across different organizations. By both companies adopting the same CSF,\nthey can ensure that their security measures, policies, and practices are consistent, which is\nessential during a merger when aligning two different security programs.", "type": "mc", "q": "Two companies are in the process of merging. The companies need to decide how to standardize\ntheir information security programs. Which of the following would best align the security\nprograms?", "options": ["Shared deployment of CIS baselines", "Joint cybersecurity best practices", "Both companies following the same CSF", "Assessment of controls in a vulnerability report"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL263", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "The tool that the network administrator deployed is described as one that logs suspicious\nwebsites and sends a daily report based on various weighted metrics. This fits the description of\na detective control. Detective controls are designed to identify and log security events or incidents\nafter they have occurred. By analyzing these logs and generating reports, the tool helps in\ndetecting potential security breaches, thus allowing for further investigation and response.", "type": "mc", "q": "A network administrator deployed a DNS logging tool that togs suspicious websites that are\nvisited and then sends a daily report based on various weighted metrics. Which of the following\nbest describes the type of control the administrator put in place?", "options": ["Preventive", "Deterrent", "Corrective", "Detective"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL264", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Job rotation is a strategy used in organizations to detect and prevent fraud by periodically\nassigning employees to different roles within the organization. This approach helps ensure that\nno single employee has exclusive control over a specific process or set of tasks for an extended\nperiod, thereby reducing the opportunity for fraudulent activities to go unnoticed. By rotating roles,\norganizations can uncover irregularities and discrepancies that might have been concealed by an\nemployee who had prolonged access to sensitive functions. Job rotation also promotes crosstraining, which can enhance the organization's overall resilience and flexibility.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is best used to detect fraud by assigning employees to different roles?", "options": ["Least privilege", "Mandatory vacation", "Separation of duties", "Job rotation"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL265", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "An image backup, also known as a full system backup, captures the entire contents of a system,\nincluding the operating system, applications, settings, and all data. This type of backup allows for\na complete recovery of the system in case of a disaster, as it includes everything needed to\nrestore the system to its previous state. This makes it the ideal choice for a systems administrator\nwho needs to ensure the ability to recover the entire system, including the OS.", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrate wants to implement a backup solution. the solution needs to allow\nrecovery of the entire system, including the operating system, in case of a disaster. Which of the\nfollowing backup types should the administrator consider?", "options": ["Incremental", "Storage area network", "Differential", "Image"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL266", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A self-signed certificate is a certificate that is signed by its own private key rather than by a\ntrusted certificate authority (CA). This means that the authenticity of the certificate relies solely on\nthe issuer's own authority. If a spoofed identity was detected, it could indicate that a private key\nassociated with a self-signed certificate was compromised. Self-signed certificates are often used\ninternally within organizations, but they carry higher risks since they are not validated by a thirdparty CA, making them more susceptible to spoofing.", "type": "mc", "q": "A spoofed identity was detected for a digital certificate. Which of the following are the type of\nunidentified key and the certificate mat could be in use on the company domain?", "options": ["Private key and root certificate", "Public key and expired certificate", "Private key and self-signed certificate", "Public key and wildcard certificate"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL267", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Data Loss Prevention (DLP) systems are typically configured to protect sensitive data such as\nPersonally Identifiable Information (PII) within an organization. DLP tools enforce policies that\n\n\nmonitor, detect, and block the unauthorized transmission of sensitive data. By leveraging the\norganization's existing labeling and classification system, DLP solutions can identify and protect\ndata based on its classification, ensuring that PII is appropriately secured according to\norganizational policies.", "type": "mc", "q": "The Chief Information Security Officer wants to put security measures in place to protect PlI. The\norganization needs to use its existing labeling and classification system to accomplish this goal.\nWhich of the following would most likely be configured to meet the requirements?", "options": ["Tokenization", "S/MIME", "DLP", "MFA"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL268", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "To determine whether the connection was successful after a user clicked on a link in a phishing\nemail, the most relevant log source to analyze would be the network logs. These logs would\nprovide information on outbound and inbound traffic, allowing the analyst to see if the user's\nsystem connected to the remote server specified in the phishing link. Network logs can include\ndetails such as IP addresses, domains accessed, and the success or failure of connections,\nwhich are crucial for understanding the impact of the phishing attempt.", "type": "mc", "q": "An analyst is reviewing an incident in which a user clicked on a link in a phishing email. Which of\nthe following log sources would the analyst utilize to determine whether the connection was\nsuccessful?", "options": ["Network", "System", "Application", "Authentication"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL269", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Before applying any updates or patches to a production VM, especially one with a 99% uptime\nSLA, it is crucial to first take a snapshot of the VM. This snapshot serves as a backup that can be\nquickly restored in case the update causes any issues, ensuring that the system can be returned\nto its previous state without violating the SLA. This step mitigates risk and is a standard best\npractice in change management for critical systems.", "type": "mc", "q": "The Cruel Information Security Officer (CISO) asks a security analyst to install an OS update to a\nproduction VM that has a 99% uptime SLA. The CISO tells me analyst the installation must be\ndone as quickly as possible. Which of the following courses of action should the security analyst\ntake first?", "options": ["Log in to the server and perform a health check on the VM.", "Install the patch Immediately.", "Confirm that the backup service is running.", "Take a snapshot of the VM."], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL270", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "When multiple Wireless Access Points (WAPs) are using similar frequencies with high power\nsettings, it can cause channel overlap, leading to interference and connectivity issues. This is\nlikely the reason why mobile users are unable to access the internet in the lobby. Evaluating and\nadjusting the channel settings on the WAPs to avoid overlap is crucial to resolving the\nconnectivity problems.", "type": "mc", "q": "Sine?a recent upgrade (o a WLAN infrastructure, several mobile users have been unable to\naccess the internet from the lobby. The networking team performs a heat map survey of the\nbuilding and finds several WAPs in the area. The WAPs are using similar frequencies with high\npower settings. Which of the following installation considerations should the security team\nevaluate next?", "options": ["Channel overlap", "Encryption type", "New WLAN deployment", "WAP placement"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL271", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The scenario describes an instance where an employee receives a fraudulent invoice from a\nvendor that is not recognized in the company's vendor management system. This is a classic\nexample of an invoice scam, where attackers attempt to trick organizations into making payments\nfor fake or non- existent services. These scams often rely on social engineering tactics to bypass\nfinancial controls.", "type": "mc", "q": "An employee in the accounting department receives an email containing a demand for payment\ntot services performed by a vendor However, the vendor is not in the vendor management\ndatabase. Which of the following in this scenario an example of?", "options": ["Pretexting", "Impersonation", "Ransomware", "Invoice scam"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL272", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Software as a Service (SaaS) is the cloud model that best meets the goal of outsourcing the\nmanagement, including patching, of firmware, operating systems, and applications to the cloud\nvendor. In a SaaS environment, the cloud provider is responsible for maintaining and updating\nthe entire software stack, allowing the organization to focus on using the software rather than\nmanaging its infrastructure.", "type": "mc", "q": "While considering the organization's cloud-adoption strategy, the Chief Information Security\nOfficer sets a goal to outsource patching of firmware, operating systems, and applications to the\nchosen cloud vendor. Which of the following best meets this goal?", "options": ["Community cloud", "PaaS", "Containerization", "Private cloud", "SaaS", "laaS"], "answer": [4]}, {"id": "PL273", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Monitoring outbound traffic is essential for detecting unauthorized data exfiltration from a system.\nA new vulnerability that allows malware to move data unauthorizedly would typically attempt to\nsend this data out of the network. By monitoring outbound traffic, security tools can detect\nunusual data transfers, trigger alerts, and help prevent the exfiltration of sensitive information.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst is assessing several company firewalls. Which of the following cools would The\nanalyst most likely use to generate custom packets to use during the assessment?", "options": ["hping", "Wireshark", "PowerShell", "netstat"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL274", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Monitoring outbound traffic is essential for detecting unauthorized data exfiltration from a system.\nA new vulnerability that allows malware to move data unauthorizedly would typically attempt to\nsend this data out of the network. By monitoring outbound traffic, security tools can detect\nunusual data transfers, trigger alerts, and help prevent the exfiltration of sensitive information.", "type": "mc", "q": "A new vulnerability enables a type of malware that allows the unauthorized movement of data\nfrom a system. Which of the following would detect this behavior?", "options": ["Implementing encryption", "Monitoring outbound traffic", "Using default settings", "Closing all open ports"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL275", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) is a standardized framework for assessing\nthe severity of security vulnerabilities. It helps organizations prioritize vulnerability patching by\nproviding a numerical score that reflects the potential impact and exploitability of a vulnerability.\nCVSS scores are used to gauge the urgency of patching vulnerabilities within a company's IT\nenvironment.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following can a security director use to prioritize vulnerability patching within a\ncompany's IT environment?", "options": ["SOAR", "CVSS", "SIEM", "CVE"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL276", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Air-gapping is the most effective way to protect an application server running unsupported\nsoftware from network threats. By physically isolating the server from any network connection (no\nwired or wireless communication), it is protected from external cyber threats. While other options\nlike port security or a screened subnet can provide some level of protection, an air gap offers the\nhighest level of security by preventing any network-based attacks entirely.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the most effective way to protect an application server running software\nthat is no longer supported from network threats?", "options": ["Air gap", "Barricade", "Port security", "Screen subnet"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL277", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The most important security concern when using legacy systems is the lack of vendor support.\nWithout support from the vendor, systems may not receive critical security patches and updates,\nleaving them vulnerable to exploitation. This lack of support can result in increased risk of security\nbreaches, as vulnerabilities discovered in the software may never be addressed.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is die most important security concern when using legacy systems to\nprovide production service?", "options": ["Instability", "Lack of vendor support", "Loss of availability", "Use of insecure protocols"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL278", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "If cadets are using company phone numbers to make unsolicited calls, and the logs confirm the\nnumbers are not being spoofed, it suggests that the SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) server's\nsecurity settings might be weak. This could allow unauthorized access or exploitation of the\ncompany's telephony services, potentially leading to misuse by unauthorized individuals.", "type": "mc", "q": "Cadets speaking a foreign language are using company phone numbers to make unsolicited\nphone calls lo a partner organization. A security analyst validates through phone system logs that\nthe calls are occurring and the numbers are not being spoofed. Which of the following is the most\nlikely explanation?", "options": ["The executive team is traveling internationally and trying to avoid roaming charges", "The company's SIP server security settings are weak.", "Disgruntled employees are making calls to the partner organization.", "The service provider has assigned multiple companies the same numbers"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL279", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "To mitigate the risk of confidential documents being left unattended in Multi-Function Printers\n(MFPs), implementing an authentication factor that requires in-person action before printing (such\nas PIN codes or badge scanning) is the most effective measure. This ensures that documents are\nonly printed when the authorized user is present to collect them, reducing the risk of sensitive\ninformation being exposed.", "type": "mc", "q": "An IT security team is concerned about the confidentiality of documents left unattended in MFPs.\nWhich of the following should the security team do to mitigate the situation?", "options": ["Educate users about the importance of paper shredder devices.", "Deploy an authentication factor that requires ln-person action before printing.", "Install a software client m every computer authorized to use the MFPs.", "Update the management software to utilize encryption."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL280", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The chmod command is used to change file permissions on Unix and Linux systems. If the\n/etc/shadow file has permissions beyond the baseline recommendation, the systems\nadministrator should use chmod to modify the file's permissions, ensuring it adheres to the\nsecurity baseline and limits access to authorized users only.", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator is auditing all company servers to ensure. They meet the minimum\nsecurity baseline While auditing a Linux server, the systems administrator observes the\n/etc/shadow file has permissions beyond the baseline recommendation. Which of the following\ncommands should the systems administrator use to resolve this issue?", "options": ["chmod", "grep", "dd", "passwd"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL281", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Dumpster diving is an attack method where attackers search through physical waste, such as\ndiscarded documents and printouts, to find sensitive information that has not been properly\ndisposed of. In the context of printing centers, this could involve attackers retrieving printed\ndocuments containing confidential data that were improperly discarded without shredding or other\nsecure disposal methods. This emphasizes the importance of proper disposal and physical\nsecurity measures in cyber hygiene practices.", "type": "mc", "q": "During a recent company safety stand-down, the cyber-awareness team gave a presentation on\nthe importance of cyber hygiene. One topic the team covered was best practices for printing\ncenters. Which of the following describes an attack method that relates to printing centers?", "options": ["Whaling", "Credential harvesting", "Prepending", "Dumpster diving"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL282", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "An obfuscation toolkit is used by developers to make source code difficult to understand and\nreverse engineer. This technique involves altering the code's structure and naming conventions\nwithout changing its functionality, making it much harder for attackers to decipher the code or use\ndebugging tools to analyze it. Obfuscation is an important practice in protecting proprietary\nsoftware and intellectual property from reverse engineering.", "type": "mc", "q": "A software developer would like to ensure. The source code cannot be reverse engineered or\ndebugged. Which of the following should the developer consider?", "options": ["Version control", "Obfuscation toolkit", "Code reuse", "Continuous integration", "Stored procedures"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL283", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Code repositories are a common source of unintentional corporate credential leakage, especially\nin cloud environments. Developers may accidentally commit and push sensitive information, such\nas API keys, passwords, and other credentials, to public or poorly secured repositories. These\ncredentials can then be accessed by unauthorized users, leading to security breaches. Ensuring\nthat repositories are properly secured and that sensitive data is never committed is critical for\nprotecting against this type of leakage.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a common source of unintentional corporate credential leakage in cloud\nenvironments?", "options": ["Code repositories", "Dark web", "Threat feeds", "State actors", "Vulnerability databases"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL284", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Using a Privileged Access Management (PAM) vault to secure domain administrator credentials\nand enforcing role-based access control (RBAC) is the most comprehensive solution. PAM\nsystems help manage and control access to privileged accounts, ensuring that only authorized\npersonnel can access sensitive credentials. This approach also facilitates password rotation,\nauditing, and ensures that credentials are not misused or left unchanged. Integrating PAM with\nRBAC ensures that access is granted based on the user's role, further enhancing security.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security audit of an organization revealed that most of the IT staff members have domain\nadministrator credentials and do not change the passwords regularly. Which of the following\nsolutions should the security learn propose to resolve the findings in the most complete way?", "options": ["Creating group policies to enforce password rotation on domain administrator credentials", "Reviewing the domain administrator group, removing all unnecessary administrators, and rotating all passwords", "Integrating the domain administrator's group with an IdP and requiring SSO with MFA for all access", "Securing domain administrator credentials in a PAM vault and controlling access with role-based access control"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL285", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A honeypot is a security mechanism set up to attract and detect potential attackers by simulating\nvulnerable assets. By hosting a part of the infrastructure online with known vulnerabilities that\nappear to be company assets, the company can observe and analyze the behavior of attackers\nconducting reconnaissance. This approach allows the company to get alerts and gather\nintelligence on potential threats.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company wants to get alerts when others are researching and doing reconnaissance on the\ncompany One approach would be to host a part of the Infrastructure online with known\nvulnerabilities that would appear to be company assets. Which of the following describes this\napproach?", "options": ["Watering hole", "Bug bounty", "DNS sinkhole", "Honeypot"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL286", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The SMS OTP (One-Time Password) method is more vulnerable to interception compared to\nTOTP (Time-based One-Time Password) because SMS messages can be intercepted through\nvarious attack vectors like SIM swapping or SMS phishing. TOTP, on the other hand, generates\ncodes directly on the device and does not rely on a communication channel like SMS, making it\nless susceptible to interception.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following best describes why me SMS DIP authentication method is more risky to\nimplement than the TOTP method?", "options": ["The SMS OTP method requires an end user to have an active mobile telephone service and SIM card.", "Generally. SMS OTP codes are valid for up to 15 minutes while the TOTP time frame is 30 to 60 seconds", "The SMS OTP is more likely to be intercepted and lead to unauthorized disclosure of the code than the TOTP method.", "The algorithm used to generate on SMS OTP code is weaker than the one used to generate a TOTP code"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL287", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The scenario describes a situation where a user unknowingly triggers an unwanted action, such\nas changing their password, by clicking a malicious link. This is indicative of a Cross-Site Request\nForgery (CSRF) attack, where an attacker tricks the user into executing actions they did not\nintend to perform on a web application in which they are authenticated.", "type": "mc", "q": "A website user is locked out of an account after clicking an email link and visiting a different\nwebsite Web server logs show the user's password was changed, even though the user did not\nchange the password. Which of the following is the most likely cause?", "options": ["Cross-sue request forgery", "Directory traversal", "ARP poisoning", "SQL injection"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL288", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) solution is the most suitable option for securing an\norganization that has adopted a cloud-first strategy and does not have an on-premises IT\ninfrastructure. CASBs provide visibility and control over shadow IT services, enforce security\npolicies, and protect data across cloud services.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security engineer is working to address the growing risks that shadow IT services are\nintroducing to the organization. The organization has taken a cloud-first approach end does not\nhave an on- premises IT infrastructure. Which of the following would best secure the\norganization?", "options": ["Upgrading to a next-generation firewall", "Deploying an appropriate in-line CASB solution", "Conducting user training on software policies", "Configuring double key encryption in SaaS platforms"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL289", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The first step in responding to a cybersecurity incident, particularly when malware is detected, is\nto contain the impacted hosts. This action prevents the spread of malware to other parts of the\nnetwork, limiting the potential damage while further investigation and remediation actions are\nplanned.", "type": "mc", "q": "A cybersecurity incident response team at a large company receives notification that malware is\npresent on several corporate desktops No known Indicators of compromise have been found on\nthe network. Which of the following should the team do first to secure the environment?", "options": ["Contain the Impacted hosts", "Add the malware to the application blocklist.", "Segment the core database server.", "Implement firewall rules to block outbound beaconing"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL290", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "When conducting a forensic analysis after an incident, it's essential to prioritize the data collection\nprocess based on the \"order of volatility.\" This principle dictates that more volatile data (e.g., data\nin memory, network connections) should be captured before less volatile data (e.g., disk drives,\nlogs). The idea is to preserve the most transient and potentially valuable evidence first, as it is\nmore likely to be lost or altered quickly.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a reason why a forensic specialist would create a plan to preserve data\nafter an modem and prioritize the sequence for performing forensic analysis?", "options": ["Order of volatility", "Preservation of event logs", "Chain of custody", "Compliance with legal hold"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL291", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The security analyst is creating a \"secure configuration guide,\" which is a set of instructions or\nguidelines used to configure devices securely before deployment. This guide ensures that the\ndevices are set up according to best practices to minimize vulnerabilities and protect against\npotential security threats.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst is creating base for the server team to follow when hardening new devices for\ndeployment. Which of the following beet describes what the analyst is creating?", "options": ["Change management procedure", "Information security policy", "Cybersecurity framework", "Secure configuration guide"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL292", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Tokenization is a process that replaces sensitive data, such as credit card information, with a\nnon- sensitive equivalent (token) that can be used in place of the actual data. This technique is\nparticularly useful in securely storing payment information because the token can be safely stored\nand transmitted without exposing the original credit card number.", "type": "mc", "q": "In which of the following scenarios is tokenization the best privacy technique 10 use?", "options": ["Providing pseudo-anonymization tor social media user accounts", "Serving as a second factor for authentication requests", "Enabling established customers to safely store credit card Information", "Masking personal information inside databases by segmenting data"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL293", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "The scenario shows MD5 hashed password values. The most likely reason the security\nadministrator is focusing on these values is to protect against pass-the-hash attacks. In this type\nof attack, an attacker can use a captured hash to authenticate without needing to know the actual\nplaintext password. By managing and monitoring these hashes, the administrator can implement\nstrategies to mitigate this type of threat.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator recently reset local passwords and the following values were recorded in\nthe system:\n\n\nWhich of the following in the security administrator most likely protecting against?", "options": ["Account sharing", "Weak password complexity", "Pass-the-hash attacks", "Password compromise"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL294", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Secure Shell (SSH) is a protocol used for secure command-line access to remote systems, while\nSecure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) is an extension of SSH used specifically for securely\ntransferring files. Both SSH and SFTP ensure that data is encrypted during transmission,\nprotecting it from interception or tampering.", "type": "multi", "q": "A vendor needs to remotely and securely transfer files from one server to another using the\ncommand line. Which of the following protocols should be Implemented to allow for this type of\naccess? (Select two).", "options": ["SSH", "SNMP", "RDP", "S/MIME", "SMTP", "SFTP"], "answer": [0, 5]}, {"id": "PL295", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The data owner is the role responsible for identifying risks to data and determining who should\nhave access to that data. The owner has the authority to make decisions about the protection and\nusage of the data, including setting access controls and ensuring that appropriate security\nmeasures are in place.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following data roles is responsible for identifying risks and appropriate access to\ndata?", "options": ["Owner", "Custodian", "Steward", "Controller"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL296", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A tabletop exercise is a discussion-based exercise where stakeholders gather to walk through the\nroles and responsibilities they would have during a specific situation, such as a security incident\nor disaster. This type of exercise is designed to identify gaps in planning and improve\ncoordination among team members without the need for physical execution.", "type": "mc", "q": "Various stakeholders are meeting to discuss their hypothetical roles and responsibilities in a\nspecific situation, such as a security incident or major disaster. Which of the following best\ndescribes this meeting?", "options": ["Penetration test", "Continuity of operations planning", "Tabletop exercise", "Simulation"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL297", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The file left by the external vendor, containing detailed architecture information and code\nsnippets, is best described as proprietary data. Proprietary data is information that is owned by a\ncompany and is essential to its competitive advantage. It includes sensitive business information\nsuch as trade secrets, intellectual property, and confidential data that should be protected from\nunauthorized access.", "type": "mc", "q": "An external vendor recently visited a company's headquarters tor a presentation. Following the\nvisit a member of the hosting team found a file that the external vendor left behind on a server.\nThe file contained detailed architecture information and code snippets. Which of the following\ndata types best describes this file?", "options": ["Government", "Public", "Proprietary", "Critical"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL298", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Password spraying is a type of attack where an attacker tries a small number of commonly used\npasswords across a large number of accounts. The event logs showing failed login attempts for\nmany user accounts from the same IP address are indicative of a password spraying attack,\nwhere the attacker is attempting to gain access by guessing common passwords.", "type": "mc", "q": "The security operations center is researching an event concerning a suspicious IP address. A\nsecurity analyst looks at the following event logs and discovers that a significant portion of the\nuser accounts have experienced failed log-in attempts when authenticating from the same IP\naddress:\n\n\nWhich of the following most likely describes the attack that took place?", "options": ["Spraying", "Brute-force", "Dictionary", "Rainbow table"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL299", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Salting is a technique used to enhance the security of hashed passwords by adding a unique,\nrandom value (salt) to each password before hashing it. This prevents attackers from easily\ndecrypting passwords using rainbow tables, which are precomputed tables for reversing\ncryptographic hash functions. Since each password has a unique salt, the same password will\nproduce different hash values, making rainbow table attacks ineffective.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following explains why an attacker cannot easily decrypt passwords using a rainbow\ntable attack?", "options": ["Digital signatures", "Salting", "Hashing", "Perfect forward secrecy"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL300", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Push notifications offer a seamless and user-friendly method of multi-factor authentication (MFA)\n\n\nthat can easily integrate into a user's workflow. This method leverages employee-owned devices,\nlike smartphones, to approve authentication requests through a push notification. It's convenient,\nquick, and doesn't require the user to input additional codes, making it a preferred choice for\nseamless integration with existing workflows.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is currently utilizing usernames and passwords, and it wants to integrate an MFA\nmethod that is seamless, can Integrate easily into a user's workflow, and can utilize employeeowned devices. Which of the following will meet these requirements?", "options": ["Push notifications", "Phone call", "Smart card", "Offline backup codes"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL301", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Homomorphic encryption allows data to be encrypted and manipulated without needing to decrypt\nit first. This cryptographic technique would allow the financial institution to store customer data\nsecurely in the cloud while still permitting operations like searching and calculations to be\nperformed on the encrypted data. This ensures that the cloud service provider cannot decipher\nthe sensitive data, meeting the institution's security requirements.", "type": "mc", "q": "A financial institution would like to store its customer data m the cloud but still allow the data to be\naccessed and manipulated while encrypted. Doing so would prevent the cloud service provider\nfrom being able to decipher the data due to its sensitivity. The financial institution Is not\nconcerned about computational overheads and slow speeds. Which of the following cryptographic\ntechniques would best meet the requirement?", "options": ["Asymmetric", "Symmetric", "Homomorphic", "Ephemeral"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL302", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A backup strategy that combines weekly full backups with daily incremental backups stored on a\nNAS (Network Attached Storage) drive is likely to meet an organization's Recovery Point\nObjectives (RPOs) and Recovery Time Objectives (RTOs). This approach ensures that recent\ndata is regularly backed up and that recovery can be done efficiently, without significant data loss\nor lengthy downtime.", "type": "mc", "q": "The Chief Information Security Officer of an organization needs to ensure recovery from\nransomware would likely occur within the organization's agreed-upon RPOs end RTOs. Which of\nthe following backup scenarios would best ensure recovery?", "options": ["Hourly differential backups stored on a local SAN array", "Dally full backups stored on premises in magnetic offline media", "Daly differential backups maintained by a third-party cloud provider", "Weekly full backups with daily incremental stored on a NAS drive"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL303", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A two-person integrity security control is implemented to minimize the risk of errors or\nunauthorized actions. This control ensures that at least two individuals are involved in critical\noperations, which helps to verify the accuracy of the process and prevents unauthorized users\nfrom acting alone. It's a security measure commonly used in sensitive operations, like financial\ntransactions or access to critical systems, to ensure accountability and accuracy.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following best describe why a process would require a two-person integrity security\ncontrol?", "options": ["To Increase the chance that the activity will be completed in half of the time the process would take only one user to complete", "To permit two users from another department to observe the activity that is being performed by an authorized user", "To reduce the risk that the procedures are performed incorrectly or by an unauthorized user", "To allow one person to perform the activity while being recorded on the CCTV camera"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL304", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A Secure Web Gateway (SWG) protects users by filtering unwanted software/malware from userinitiated web traffic and enforcing corporate and regulatory policy compliance. This technology\nallows the company to secure remote users' data and web traffic without relying on a VPN,\nmaking it ideal for organizations supporting remote work.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company recently decided to allow employees to work remotely. The company wants to protect\nus data without using a VPN. Which of the following technologies should the company\nImplement?", "options": ["Secure web gateway", "Virtual private cloud end point", "Deep packet Inspection", "Next-gene ration firewall"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL305", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Risk appetite refers to the level of risk that an organization is willing to accept in order to achieve\nits objectives. In this scenario, the security engineer is concerned that the timeframe for\nimplementing a new application does not allow for sufficient cybersecurity due diligence. This\nreflects a situation where the organization's risk appetite might be too high if it proceeds without\nthe necessary security checks.", "type": "mc", "q": "In a rush to meet an end-of-year business goal, the IT department was told to implement a new\nbusiness application. The security engineer reviews the attributes of the application and decides\nthe time needed to perform due diligence is insufficient from a cybersecurity perspective. Which\nof the following best describes the security engineer's response?", "options": ["Risk tolerance", "Risk acceptance", "Risk importance", "Risk appetite"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL306", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Baseline configuration is the process of standardizing the configuration settings for a system or\nnetwork. In this scenario, the organization needs to standardize the operating system\nconfigurations before deploying them across the network. Establishing a baseline configuration\nensures that all systems adhere to the organization's security policies and operational\nrequirements.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization has too many variations of a single operating system and needs to standardize\nthe arrangement prior to pushing the system image to users. Which of the following should the\norganization implement first?", "options": ["Standard naming convention", "Mashing", "Network diagrams", "Baseline configuration"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL307", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response (SOAR) systems help organizations automate\nrepetitive security tasks, reduce manual intervention, and improve the efficiency of security\noperations. By integrating with various security tools, SOAR can automatically respond to\nincidents, helping to enhance threat detection while reducing the manual workload on security\nanalysts.", "type": "mc", "q": "A growing company would like to enhance the ability of its security operations center to detect\nthreats but reduce the amount of manual work required tor the security analysts. Which of the\nfollowing would best enable the reduction in manual work?", "options": ["SOAR", "SIEM", "MDM", "DLP"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL308", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "802.1X is a network access control protocol that provides an authentication mechanism to\ndevices trying to connect to a LAN or WLAN. It supports the use of certificates for authentication,\n\n\ncan quarantine unapproved devices, and ensures that only approved and updated devices can\naccess network resources. This protocol best meets the requirements of securing both wired and\nwireless networks with internal certificates.", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator is redesigning now devices will perform network authentication. The\nfollowing requirements need to be met:\n- An existing Internal certificate must be used.\n- Wired and wireless networks must be supported\n- Any unapproved device should be Isolated in a quarantine subnet\n- Approved devices should be updated before accessing resources\nWhich of the following would best meet the requirements?", "options": ["802.IX", "EAP", "RADIUS", "WPA2"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL309", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Integrating each SaaS solution with an Identity Provider (IdP) is the most effective way to address\nthe security issue. This approach allows for Single Sign-On (SSO) capabilities, where users can\naccess multiple SaaS applications with a single set of credentials while maintaining strong\npassword policies across all services. It simplifies the user experience and ensures consistent\nsecurity enforcement across different SaaS platforms.", "type": "multi", "q": "A company implemented an MDM policy 10 mitigate risks after repealed instances of employees\nlosing company-provided mobile phones. In several cases. The lost phones were used\nmaliciously to perform social engineering attacks against other employees. Which of the following\nMDM features should be configured to best address this issue? (Select two).", "options": ["Screen locks", "Remote wipe", "Full device encryption", "Push notifications", "Application management", "Geolocation"], "answer": [1, 0]}, {"id": "PL310", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Integrating each SaaS solution with an Identity Provider (IdP) is the most effective way to address\nthe security issue. This approach allows for Single Sign-On (SSO) capabilities, where users can\naccess multiple SaaS applications with a single set of credentials while maintaining strong\npassword policies across all services. It simplifies the user experience and ensures consistent\nsecurity enforcement across different SaaS platforms.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst needs to propose a remediation plan 'or each item in a risk register. The item\nwith the highest priority requires employees to have separate logins for SaaS solutions and\ndifferent password complexity requirements for each solution. Which of the following\nimplementation plans will most likely resolve this security issue?", "options": ["Creating a unified password complexity standard", "Integrating each SaaS solution with the Identity provider", "Securing access to each SaaS by using a single wildcard certificate", "Configuring geofencing on each SaaS solution"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL311", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "The most likely way a rogue device was able to connect to the network is through a MAC cloning\nattack. In this attack, a personal device copies the MAC address of an authorized device,\nbypassing the 802.1X access control that relies on known hardware addresses for network\naccess. The matching MAC addresses in the audit report suggest that this technique was used to\ngain unauthorized network access.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst finds a rogue device during a monthly audit of current endpoint assets that are\nconnected to the network. The corporate network utilizes 002.1X for access control. To be\nallowed on the network, a device must have a Known hardware address, and a valid user name\nand password must be entered in a captive portal. The following is the audit report:\n\n\nWhich of the following is the most likely way a rogue device was allowed to connect?", "options": ["A user performed a MAC cloning attack with a personal device.", "A DMCP failure caused an incorrect IP address to be distributed", "An administrator bypassed the security controls for testing.", "DNS hijacking let an attacker intercept the captive portal traffic."], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL312", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "The first step in creating an anomaly detection process is building a baseline of normal behavior\nwithin the system. This baseline serves as a reference point to identify deviations or anomalies\nthat could indicate a security incident. By understanding what normal activity looks like, security\nteams can more effectively detect and respond to suspicious behavior.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the first step to take when creating an anomaly detection process?", "options": ["Selecting events", "Building a baseline", "Selecting logging options", "Creating an event log"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL313", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "The final step in the incident response process is \"Lessons learned.\" This step involves reviewing\n\n\nand analyzing the incident to understand what happened, how it was handled, and what could be\nimproved. The goal is to improve future response efforts and prevent similar incidents from\noccurring. It's essential for refining the incident response plan and enhancing overall security\nposture.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the final step of the modem response process?", "options": ["Lessons learned", "Eradication", "Containment", "Recovery"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL314", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Input sanitization is a critical security measure to prevent SQL injection attacks, which occur\nwhen an attacker exploits vulnerabilities in a website's input fields to execute malicious SQL\ncode. By properly sanitizing and validating all user inputs, developers can prevent malicious code\nfrom being executed, thereby securing the website against such attacks.", "type": "mc", "q": "While investigating a recent security breach an analyst finds that an attacker gained access by\nSOL infection through a company website. Which of the following should the analyst recommend\nto the website developers to prevent this from reoccurring?", "options": ["Secure cookies", "Input sanitization", "Code signing", "Blocklist"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL315", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A staging environment is a controlled setting that closely mirrors the production environment but\nuses a subset of customer data. It is used to test major system upgrades, assess their impact,\nand demonstrate new features before they are rolled out to the live production environment. This\nensures that any issues can be identified and addressed in a safe environment before affecting\nend-users.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following environments utilizes a subset of customer data and is most likely to be\nused to assess the impacts of major system upgrades and demonstrate system features?", "options": ["Development", "Test", "Production", "Staging"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL316", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The security engineer is likely to deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to protect the new web\nportal service. A WAF specifically protects web applications by filtering, monitoring, and blocking\nHTTP requests based on a set of rules. This is crucial for preventing common attacks such as\nSQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), and other web-based attacks that could compromise the\nweb service.\nLayer 4 firewall operates primarily at the transport layer, focusing on IP address and port filtering,\nmaking it unsuitable for web application-specific threats.\nNGFW (Next-Generation Firewall) provides more advanced filtering than traditional firewalls,\nincluding layer 7 inspection, but the WAF is tailored specifically for web traffic.\nUTM (Unified Threat Management) offers a suite of security tools in one package (like antivirus,\nfirewall, and content filtering), but for web application-specific protection, a WAF is the best fit.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization recently started hosting a new service that customers access through a web\nportal. A security engineer needs to add to the existing security devices a new solution to protect\nthis new service. Which of the following is the engineer most likely to deploy?", "options": ["Layer 4 firewall", "NGFW", "WAF", "UTM"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL317", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "The IT manager is creating a Business Continuity Plan (BCP). A BCP describes how an\norganization will continue to operate during and after a disaster or global incident. It ensures that\ncritical business functions remain operational despite adverse conditions, with a focus on\nminimizing downtime and maintaining essential services.", "type": "mc", "q": "An IT manager is putting together a documented plan describing how the organization will keep\noperating in the event of a global incident. Which of the following plans is the IT manager\ncreating?", "options": ["Business continuity", "Physical security", "Change management", "Disaster recovery"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL318", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Within an organization's Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), an Information Security Policy\nis a vital component. It outlines the rules and procedures for ensuring that the organization's IT\nassets and data are protected throughout the development process. Ensuring secure coding\npractices, access controls, and regular security testing is fundamental in preventing vulnerabilities\nin applications.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following topics would most likely be included within an organization's SDLC?", "options": ["Service-level agreements", "Information security policy", "Penetration testing methodology", "Branch protection requirements"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL319", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A Service Level Agreement (SLA) is a formal document between a service provider and a client\nthat defines the expected level of service, including what resources will be provided and the\nagreed-upon time frames. It typically includes metrics to evaluate performance, uptime\nguarantees, and response times.\nMOU (Memorandum of Understanding) and MOA (Memorandum of Agreement) are less formal\nand may not specify the exact level of service.\nBPA (Business Partners Agreement) focuses more on the long-term relationship between\npartners.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following describes the understanding between a company and a client about what\nwill be provided and the accepted time needed to provide the company with the resources?", "options": ["SLA", "MOU", "MOA", "BPA"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL320", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A tabletop exercise involves the executive team or key stakeholders discussing and testing the\ncompany's incident response plan in a simulated environment. These exercises are low-stress,\ndiscussion-based, and help to validate the plan's effectiveness by walking through different\nscenarios without disrupting actual operations. It is an essential part of testing business continuity\nand incident response strategies.\nContinuity of operations refers to the ability of an organization to continue functioning during and\nafter a disaster but doesn't specifically involve simulations like tabletop exercises.\nCapacity planning is related to ensuring the infrastructure can handle growth, not incident\nresponse testing.\nParallel processing refers to running multiple processes simultaneously, which is unrelated to\ntesting an incident response plan.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following describes an executive team that is meeting in a board room and testing\nthe company's incident response plan?", "options": ["Continuity of operations", "Capacity planning", "Tabletop exercise", "Parallel processing"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL321", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A vulnerability scan is the most likely method to identify legacy systems. These scans assess an\norganization's network and systems for known vulnerabilities, including outdated or unsupported\nsoftware (i.e., legacy systems) that may pose a security risk. The scan results can highlight\nsystems that are no longer receiving updates, helping IT teams address these risks.\nBug bounty programs are used to incentivize external researchers to find security flaws, but they\n\n\nare less effective at identifying legacy systems.\nPackage monitoring tracks installed software packages for updates or issues but is not as\ncomprehensive for identifying legacy systems.\nDynamic analysis is typically used for testing applications during runtime to find vulnerabilities,\nbut not for identifying legacy systems.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following methods would most likely be used to identify legacy systems?", "options": ["Bug bounty program", "Vulnerability scan", "Package monitoring", "Dynamic analysis"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL322", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "This is one of the most important considerations because it involves how individuals can access,\ncorrect or delete their personal data as required by data protection regulations such as GDPR.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following considerations is the most important for an organization to evaluate as it\nestablishes and maintains a data privacy program?", "options": ["Reporting structure for the data privacy officer", "Request process for data subject access", "Role as controller or processor", "Physical location of the company"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL323", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The scenario described, where client files are only accessible to employees who \"need to know\"\nthe information, reflects the concept of confidentiality. Confidentiality ensures that sensitive\ninformation is only accessible to those who are authorized to view it, preventing unauthorized\naccess.\nAvailability ensures that data is accessible when needed but doesn't focus on restricting access.\nIntegrity ensures that data remains accurate and unaltered but doesn't pertain to access control.\nNon-repudiation ensures that actions cannot be denied after they are performed, but this concept\nis unrelated to access control.", "type": "mc", "q": "Client files can only be accessed by employees who need to know the information and have\nspecified roles in the company. Which of the following best describes this security concept?", "options": ["Availability", "Confidentiality", "Integrity", "Non-repudiation"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL324", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Jailbreaking is the process of removing restrictions imposed by the manufacturer on a\nsmartphone, allowing the user to install unauthorized software and features not available through\nofficial app stores. This action typically voids the warranty and can introduce security risks by\nbypassing built-in protections.\nSOU (Statement of Understanding) is not related to modifying devices.\nCross-site scripting is a web-based attack technique, unrelated to smartphone software.\nSide loading refers to installing apps from unofficial sources but without necessarily removing\nbuilt-in restrictions like jailbreaking does.", "type": "mc", "q": "A user would like to install software and features that are not available with a smartphone's\ndefault software. Which of the following would allow the user to install unauthorized software and\nenable new features?", "options": ["SOU", "Cross-site scripting", "Jailbreaking", "Side loading"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL325", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Port security is the best mitigation technique for preventing an attacker from flooding the MAC\naddress table of network switches. Port security can limit the number of MAC addresses learned\non a port, preventing an attacker from overwhelming the switch's MAC table (a form of MAC\nflooding attack). When the allowed number of MAC addresses is exceeded, port security can\nblock additional devices or trigger alerts.\nLoad balancer distributes network traffic but does not address MAC flooding attacks.\nIPS (Intrusion Prevention System) detects and prevents attacks but isn't specifically designed for\nMAC flooding mitigation.\nNGFW (Next-Generation Firewall) offers advanced traffic inspection but is not directly involved in\nMAC table security.", "type": "mc", "q": "A recent penetration test identified that an attacker could flood the MAC address table of network\nswitches. Which of the following would best mitigate this type of attack?", "options": ["Load balancer", "Port security", "IPS", "NGFW"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL326", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "In this scenario, employees are attempting to navigate to spoofed websites, which is being\nblocked by the web filter. To address this issue, the administrator should implement security\nawareness training. Training helps employees recognize phishing and other social engineering\nattacks, reducing the likelihood that they will attempt to access malicious websites in the future.\nDeploying multifactor authentication (MFA) would strengthen authentication but does not directly\naddress user behavior related to phishing websites.\nDecreasing the level of the web filter would expose the organization to more threats.\nUpdating the acceptable use policy may clarify guidelines but is not as effective as hands-on\ntraining for improving user behavior.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator at a small business notices an increase in support calls from employees who\nreceive a blocked page message after trying to navigate to a spoofed website. Which of the\nfollowing should the administrator do?", "options": ["Deploy multifactor authentication.", "Decrease the level of the web filter settings", "Implement security awareness training.", "Update the acceptable use policy"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL327", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "An Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) is an example of a managerial control. Managerial controls are\npolicies and procedures that govern an organization's operations, ensuring security through\ndirectives and rules. The AUP defines acceptable behavior and usage of company resources,\nsetting guidelines for employees.\nPhysical controls refer to security measures like locks, fences, or security guards.\nTechnical controls involve security mechanisms such as firewalls or encryption.\nOperational controls are procedures for maintaining security, such as backup and recovery plans.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following control types is AUP an example of?", "options": ["Physical", "Managerial", "Technical", "Operational"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL328", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "This example of a script injection attack would be best mitigated by input sanitization. Input\nsanitization involves cleaning or filtering user inputs to ensure that they do not contain harmful\ndata, such as malicious scripts. This prevents attackers from executing script-based attacks (e.g.,\nCross-Site Scripting or XSS).\nNmap command is unrelated to input sanitization, as it is a network scanning tool.\nEmail phishing attempts require different mitigations, such as user training.\nBrowser warnings about insecure connections involve encryption protocols, not input validation.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following examples would be best mitigated by input sanitization?", "options": ["", "nmap - 10.11.1.130", "Email message: \"Click this link to get your free gift card.\"", "Browser message: \"Your connection is not private.\""], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL329", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "To block signature-based attacks, the Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) must be in active mode.\nIn this mode, the IPS can actively monitor and block malicious traffic in real time based on\npredefined signatures. This is the best mode to prevent known attack types from reaching the\n\n\ninternal network.\nMonitor mode and sensor mode are typically passive, meaning they only observe and log traffic\nwithout actively blocking it.\nAudit mode is used for review purposes and does not actively block traffic.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security engineer is installing an IPS to block signature-based attacks in the environment.\nWhich of the following modes will best accomplish this task?", "options": ["Monitor", "Sensor", "Audit", "Active"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL330", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "To limit the potential impact on the log-in database in case of a breach, the security team would\nmost likely recommend hashing. Hashing converts passwords into fixed-length strings of\ncharacters, which cannot be easily reversed to reveal the original passwords. Even if the\ndatabase is breached, attackers cannot easily retrieve the actual passwords if they are properly\nhashed (especially with techniques like salting).\nTokenization is used to replace sensitive data with a token, but it is more common for protecting\ncredit card data than passwords.\nObfuscation is the process of making data harder to interpret but is weaker than hashing for\npassword protection.\nSegmentation helps isolate data but doesn't directly protect the contents of the login database.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization wants to limit potential impact to its log-in database in the event of a breach.\nWhich of the following options is the security team most likely to recommend?", "options": ["Tokenization", "Hashing", "Obfuscation", "Segmentation"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL331", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Port security is the best solution to prevent unauthorized devices, like a visitor's laptop, from\nconnecting to the company's network. Port security can limit the number of devices that can\nconnect to a network switch port and block unauthorized MAC addresses, effectively stopping\nunauthorized access attempts.\nWeb application firewall (WAF) protects against web-based attacks, not unauthorized network\naccess.\nTransport Layer Security (TLS) ensures encrypted communication but does not manage physical\nnetwork access.\nVirtual Private Network (VPN) secures remote connections but does not control access through\nphysical network ports.", "type": "mc", "q": "A visitor plugs a laptop into a network jack in the lobby and is able to connect to the company's\nnetwork. Which of the following should be configured on the existing network infrastructure to\nbest prevent this activity?", "options": ["Port security", "Web application firewall", "Transport layer security", "Virtual private network"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL332", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Attempting to enter an unauthorized area using an access badge during a penetration test is an\nexample of a physical test. This type of test evaluates the effectiveness of physical security\ncontrols, such as access badges, security guards, and locks, in preventing unauthorized access\nto restricted areas.\nDefensive and offensive testing typically refer to digital or network-based penetration testing\nstrategies.\nPassive testing involves observing or monitoring but not interacting with the environment.", "type": "mc", "q": "During a penetration test, a vendor attempts to enter an unauthorized area using an access\n\n\nbadge Which of the following types of tests does this represent?", "options": ["Defensive", "Passive", "Offensive", "Physical"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL333", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "OSINT involves gathering information from publicly available sources, such as social media,\nwebsites, and online databases, without actively interacting with the target system. This\ntechnique helps in identifying potential vulnerabilities and understanding the target's environment\nbefore more intrusive methods are used.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a common, passive reconnaissance technique employed by penetration\ntesters in the early phases of an engagement?", "options": ["Open-source intelligence", "Port scanning", "Pivoting", "Exploit validation"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL334", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A playbook is a documented set of procedures that outlines the step-by-step response to specific\ntypes of cybersecurity incidents. Security Operations Centers (SOCs) use playbooks to improve\nconsistency, efficiency, and accuracy during incident response. Playbooks help ensure that the\ncorrect procedures are followed based on the type of incident, ensuring swift and effective\nremediation.\nFrameworks provide general guidelines for implementing security but are not specific enough for\nincident response procedures.\nBaselines represent normal system behavior and are used for anomaly detection, not incident\n\n\nresponse guidance.\nBenchmarks are performance standards and are not directly related to incident response.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following should a security operations center use to improve its incident response\nprocedure?", "options": ["Playbooks", "Frameworks", "Baselines", "Benchmarks"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL335", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The administrator is using a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) tool, which is designed to identify,\nmonitor, and protect sensitive data. By fingerprinting specific files, DLP ensures that these files\ncannot be emailed or sent outside the organization without triggering an alert or blocking the\naction. This is a key feature of DLP systems, which prevent data exfiltration and ensure data\nsecurity compliance.\nSNMP traps are used for network management and monitoring, not data protection.\nSCAP (Security Content Automation Protocol) is a set of standards for automating vulnerability\nmanagement and policy compliance, unrelated to file monitoring.\nIPS (Intrusion Prevention System) blocks network-based attacks but does not handle file\nfingerprinting.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator has identified and fingerprinted specific files that will generate an alert if an\nattempt is made to email these files outside of the organization. Which of the following best\ndescribes the tool the administrator is using?", "options": ["DLP", "SNMP traps", "SCAP", "IPS"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL336", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Since the logs on the endpoint were deleted, the next best option for the analyst is to examine\nfirewall logs. Firewall logs can reveal external communication, including outbound traffic to a\ncommand-and-control (C2) server. These logs would contain information about the IP addresses,\nports, and protocols used, which can help in identifying suspicious connections.\nIPS logs may provide information about network intrusions, but firewall logs are better for tracking\ncommunication patterns.\nACL logs (Access Control List) are useful for tracking access permissions but not for identifying\nC2 communication.\nWindows security logs would have been ideal if they had not been deleted.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst is investigating a workstation that is suspected of outbound communication to a\ncommand-and-control server. During the investigation, the analyst discovered that logs on the\nendpoint were deleted. Which of the following logs would the analyst most likely look at next?", "options": ["IPS", "Firewall", "ACL", "Windows security"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL337", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "When hosting an on-premises software application in a cloud-based service, ensuring\nvisualization and isolation of resources is crucial for maintaining security best practices. This\ninvolves using virtualization techniques to create isolated environments (e.g., virtual machines or\ncontainers) for different applications and services, reducing the risk of cross-tenant attacks or\nresource leakage.\nNetwork segmentation is important but pertains more to securing network traffic rather than\nisolating computing resources.\nData encryption is also essential but doesn't specifically address resource isolation in a cloud\nenvironment.\nStrong authentication policies are critical for access control but do not address the need for\nisolating resources within the cloud environment.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security team is setting up a new environment for hosting the organization's on-premises\nsoftware application as a cloud-based service. Which of the following should the team ensure is\nin place in order for the organization to follow security best practices?", "options": ["Visualization and isolation of resources", "Network segmentation", "Data encryption", "Strong authentication policies"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL338", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "The lessons learned phase of an incident response process involves reviewing the incident and\ngenerating reports. This phase helps identify what went well, what needs improvement, and what\nchanges should be made to prevent future incidents. Documentation and reporting are essential\nparts of this phase to ensure that the findings are recorded and used for future planning.\nRecovery focuses on restoring services and normal operations.\nPreparation involves creating plans and policies for potential incidents, not reporting.\nContainment deals with isolating and mitigating the effects of the incident, not generating reports.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following phases of an incident response involves generating reports?", "options": ["Recovery", "Preparation", "Lessons learned", "Containment"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL339", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A warm site is the best option for a business that does not require immediate failover but wants to\nreduce the workload required for recovery. A warm site has some pre-installed equipment and\n\n\ndata, allowing for quicker recovery than a cold site, but it still requires some setup before\nbecoming fully operational.\nHot sites provide immediate failover but are more expensive and require constant maintenance.\nCold sites require significant time and effort to get up and running after an outage.\nGeographically dispersed sites refer to a specific location strategy rather than the readiness of\nthe recovery site.", "type": "mc", "q": "A business needs a recovery site but does not require immediate failover. The business also\nwants to reduce the workload required to recover from an outage. Which of the following recovery\nsites is the best option?", "options": ["Hot", "Cold", "Warm", "Geographically dispersed"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL340", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Due diligence refers to the process of researching and understanding the laws, regulations, and\nbest practices that govern information security within a specific industry. Organizations are\nrequired to conduct due diligence to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements,\nwhich helps mitigate risks and avoid penalties.\nCompliance reporting involves generating reports to demonstrate adherence to legal or regulatory\nstandards.\nGDPR is a specific regulation governing data privacy in the EU, not a general practice of\nresearching laws.\nAttestation is a formal declaration that an organization is compliant with a set of standards but is\nnot the act of researching the laws.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following best describes the practice of researching laws and regulations related to\ninformation security operations within a specific industry?", "options": ["Compliance reporting", "GDPR", "Due diligence", "Attestation"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL341", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Ensuring that other team members understand how a script works is essential to prevent a single\npoint of failure. If only one person knows how the script operates, the organization risks being\nunable to maintain or troubleshoot it if that person is unavailable. Sharing knowledge ensures\ncontinuity and reduces dependence on one individual.\nReducing implementation cost and remediating technical debt are secondary considerations in\nthis context.\nIdentifying complexity is important, but the main benefit is to avoid a single point of failure.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst developed a script to automate a trivial and repeatable task. Which of the\nfollowing best describes the benefits of ensuring other team members understand how the script\nworks?", "options": ["To reduce implementation cost", "To identify complexity", "To remediate technical debt", "To prevent a single point of failure"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL342", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "To ensure that sensitive data, such as Personally Identifiable Information (PII), is not modified,\nthe bank should implement file integrity monitoring (FIM). FIM tracks changes to files and\nprovides alerts if unauthorized modifications are detected, ensuring data integrity.\nFull disk encryption protects data at rest but does not prevent or monitor modifications.\nNetwork access control (NAC) manages access to the network but doesn't monitor file changes.\nUser behavior analytics (UBA) detects suspicious user activities but is not focused on file integrit.", "type": "mc", "q": "A bank set up a new server that contains customers' Pll. Which of the following should the bank\nuse to make sure the sensitive data is not modified?", "options": ["Full disk encryption", "Network access control", "File integrity monitoring", "User behavior analytics"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL343", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "When a legacy device is no longer receiving updates or patches, it is considered to be at the end\nof life (EOL). This means the manufacturer has ceased support for the device, and it will no\nlonger receive updates, security patches, or technical assistance. EOL devices pose security\nrisks and are often decommissioned or replaced.\nEnd of support may seem similar but typically refers to the cessation of technical support,\nwhereas EOL means the device is fully retired.\nEnd of business and End of testing do not apply in this context.", "type": "mc", "q": "A legacy device is being decommissioned and is no longer receiving updates or patches. Which\nof the following describes this scenario?", "options": ["End of business", "End of testing", "End of support", "End of life"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL344", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A Virtual Private Network (VPN) is the best solution to allow remote employees secure access to\ncompany resources without interception concerns. A VPN establishes an encrypted tunnel over\nthe internet, ensuring that data transferred between remote employees and the company is\nsecure from eavesdropping.\nProxy server helps with web content filtering and anonymization but does not provide encrypted\naccess.\n\n\nNGFW (Next-Generation Firewall) enhances security but is not the primary tool for enabling\nremote access.\nSecurity zone is a network segmentation technique but does not provide remote access\ncapabilities.", "type": "mc", "q": "Employees located off-site must have access to company resources in order to complete their\nassigned tasks These employees utilize a solution that allows remote access without interception\nconcerns. Which of the following best describes this solution?", "options": ["Proxy server", "NGFW", "VPN", "Security zone"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL345", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A false positive is an alert that incorrectly identifies benign activity as malicious.
