Over time, if an\nalerting system generates too many false positives, security teams are likely to ignore these\nalerts, resulting in \"alert fatigue.\" This increases the risk of missing genuine threats.\nTrue positives and true negatives are accurate and should be acted upon.\nFalse negatives are more dangerous because they fail to identify real threats, but they are not\n\"ignored\" since they do not trigger alerts.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following alert types is the most likely to be ignored over time?", "options": ["True positive", "True negative", "False positive", "False negative"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL346", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "An external examination (also known as an external audit or external review) is the best method\nfor the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) to gain an understanding of how the company's\nsecurity policies compare to external regulatory requirements. External examinations are\nconducted by third- party entities that assess an organization's compliance with laws, regulations,\nand industry standards.\nPenetration tests focus on identifying vulnerabilities, not compliance.\nInternal audits assess internal controls but are not impartial or focused on regulatory\nrequirements.\nAttestation is a formal declaration but does not involve the actual evaluation of compliance.", "type": "mc", "q": "The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) at a large company would like to gain an\nunderstanding of how the company's security policies compare to the requirements imposed by\nexternal regulators.\nWhich of the following should the CISO use?", "options": ["Penetration test", "Internal audit", "Attestation", "External examination"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL347", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "To enhance security for a system running an end-of-life operating system, placing the system in\nan isolated VLAN is the most effective approach. By isolating the system from the rest of the\nnetwork, you can limit its exposure to potential threats while maintaining its functionality. This\nsegmentation helps protect the rest of the network from any vulnerabilities in the outdated\nsystem.\nInstalling HIDS (Host-based Intrusion Detection System) can help detect intrusions but won't\nmitigate the risks posed by an unsupported OS.\nDecommissioning may not be feasible if the system is critical.\nEncrypting the system's hard drive protects data at rest but doesn't address vulnerabilities from\nan outdated OS.", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator notices that one of the systems critical for processing customer\ntransactions is running an end-of-life operating system. Which of the following techniques would\nincrease enterprise security?", "options": ["Installing HIDS on the system", "Placing the system in an isolated VLAN", "Decommissioning the system", "Encrypting the system's hard drive"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL348", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The first step in reducing the number of credentials each employee must maintain when using\nmultiple SaaS applications is to select an Identity Provider (IdP). An IdP provides a centralized\nauthentication service that supports Single Sign-On (SSO), enabling users to access multiple\napplications with a single set of credentials.\nEnabling SAML would be part of the technical implementation but comes after selecting an IdP.\nOAuth tokens are used for authorization, but selecting an IdP is the first step in managing\nauthentication.\nPassword vaulting stores multiple passwords securely but doesn't reduce the need for separate\nlogins.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization is adopting cloud services at a rapid pace and now has multiple SaaS\napplications in use. Each application has a separate log-in. so the security team wants to reduce\nthe number of credentials each employee must maintain. Which of the following is the first step\nthe security team should take?", "options": ["Enable SAML", "Create OAuth tokens.", "Use password vaulting.", "Select an IdP"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL349", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Running daily vulnerability scans on all corporate endpoints is primarily done to track the status of\n\n\npatching installations. These scans help identify any missing security patches or vulnerabilities\nthat could be exploited by attackers. Keeping the endpoints up-to-date with the latest patches is\ncritical for maintaining security.\nFinding shadow IT cloud deployments and monitoring hardware inventory are better achieved\nthrough other tools.\nHunting for active attackers would typically involve more real-time threat detection methods than\ndaily vulnerability scans.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following would best explain why a security analyst is running daily vulnerability\nscans on all corporate endpoints?", "options": ["To track the status of patching installations", "To find shadow IT cloud deployments", "To continuously the monitor hardware inventory", "To hunt for active attackers in the network"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL350", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Unidentified removable devices, such as USB drives, are a common threat vector for insider\nthreat actors attempting data exfiltration. Insiders can easily use these devices to transfer\nsensitive data out of the organization undetected, making it one of the most commonly utilized\nmethods for data theft.\nDefault network device credentials are a security vulnerability but not typically used for data\nexfiltration.\nSpear phishing emails are used for external attacks, not insider data exfiltration.\nImpersonation through typosquatting is typically used by external actors for phishing or fraud.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following threat vectors is most commonly utilized by insider threat actors attempting\ndata exfiltration?", "options": ["Unidentified removable devices", "Default network device credentials", "Spear phishing emails", "Impersonation of business units through typosquatting"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL351", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The employee notices that the links in the email do not correspond to the company's official\nURLs, indicating that this is likely a social engineering attack. Social engineering involves\nmanipulating individuals into divulging confidential information or performing actions that may\ncompromise security. Phishing emails, like the one described, often contain fraudulent links to\ntrick the recipient into providing sensitive information or downloading malware.\nBusiness email refers to business email compromise (BEC), which typically involves\nimpersonating a high-level executive to defraud the company.\nUnsecured network is unrelated to the email content.\nDefault credentials do not apply here, as the issue is with suspicious links, not login credentials.", "type": "mc", "q": "A new employee logs in to the email system for the first time and notices a message from human\nresources about onboarding. The employee hovers over a few of the links within the email and\ndiscovers that the links do not correspond to links associated with the company. Which of the\nfollowing attack vectors is most likely being used?", "options": ["Business email", "Social engineering", "Unsecured network", "Default credentials"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL352", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "To mitigate the risk of sensitive data being exfiltrated from the environment, the IT manager\nshould implement a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) solution. DLP monitors and controls the\nmovement of sensitive data, ensuring that unauthorized transfers are blocked and potential data\nbreaches are prevented.\nXDR (Extended Detection and Response) is useful for threat detection across multiple\nenvironments but doesn't specifically address data exfiltration.\nSPF (Sender Policy Framework) helps prevent email spoofing, not data exfiltration.\nDMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting & Conformance) also addresses\nemail security and spoofing, not data exfiltration.", "type": "mc", "q": "An IT manager is increasing the security capabilities of an organization after a data classification\ninitiative determined that sensitive data could be exfiltrated from the environment. Which of the\nfollowing solutions would mitigate the risk?", "options": ["XDR", "SPF", "DLP", "DMARC"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL353", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "To ensure that all systems requiring the patch are updated, the systems administrator must\nmaintain an accurate asset inventory. This inventory lists all hardware and software assets within\nthe organization, allowing the administrator to identify which systems are affected by the patch\nand ensuring that none are missed during the update process.\nNetwork enumeration is used to discover devices on a network but doesn't track software that\nrequires patching.\nData certification and procurement process are unrelated to tracking systems for patching\npurposes.", "type": "mc", "q": "An important patch for a critical application has just been released, and a systems administrator\nis identifying all of the systems requiring the patch. Which of the following must be maintained in\norder to ensure that all systems requiring the patch are updated?", "options": ["Asset inventory", "Network enumeration", "Data certification", "Procurement process"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL354", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "IOT devices often transmit sensitive data over networks and encryption ensures that this data is\nsecurely transmitted and protected from interception or tampering.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following security measures is required when using a cloud-based platform for IoT\nmanagement?", "options": ["Encrypted connection", "Federated identity", "Firewall", "Single sign-on"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL355", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) has determined the company is non-compliant with\nlocal data privacy regulations. The CISO needs to justify the budget request for more resources.\nWhich of the following should the CISO present to the board as the direct consequence of noncompliance?", "options": ["Fines", "Reputational damage", "Sanctions", "Contractual implications"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL356", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "The next-gen SIEM platforms can dynamically analyze vast datasets in real time, enabling the\nidentification of subtle, evolving threats that traditional systems might overlook.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a feature of a next-generation SIEM system?", "options": ["Virus signatures", "Automated response actions", "Security agent deployment", "Vulnerability scanning"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL357", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "PDFs can be used to deliver malware such as embedded scripts or exploits. Enabling malware\ndetection through a UTM helps to scan and block malicious content within uploaded files before\nthey reach the server.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company allows customers to upload PDF documents to its public e-commerce website. Which\nof the following would a security analyst most likely recommend?", "options": ["Utilizing attack signatures in an IDS", "Enabling malware detection through a UTM", "Limiting the affected servers with a load balancer", "Blocking command injections via a WAF"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL358", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A Chief Information Security Officer would like to conduct frequent, detailed reviews of systems\nand procedures to track compliance objectives. Which of the following will be the best method to\nachieve this objective?", "options": ["Third-party attestation", "Penetration testing", "Internal auditing", "Vulnerability scans"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL359", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Version control involves maintaining a record of changes made to the document, including details\nsuch as who made the changes, when they were made, and what was modified. This process\nensures that all revisions are documented, and the most current version of the contract is clearly\nidentified.", "type": "mc", "q": "After creating a contract for IT contractors, the human resources department changed several\nclauses. The contract has gone through three revisions. Which of the following processes should\nthe human resources department follow to track revisions?", "options": ["Version validation", "Version changes", "Version updates", "Version control"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL360", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "The executive management team is mandating the company develop a disaster recovery plan.\nThe cost must be kept to a minimum, and the money to fund additional internet connections is not\navailable. Which of the following would be the best option?", "options": ["Hot site", "Cold site", "Failover site", "Warm site"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL361", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following teams is best suited to determine whether a company has systems that\ncan be exploited by a potential, identified vulnerability?", "options": ["Purple team", "Blue team", "Red team", "White team"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL362", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Trusted devices allow users to log in seamlessly from devices that are already recognized and\ntrusted by the system. It supports remove and global access as the device does not need to be in\na specific location or equipped with extra hardware. It minimizes the need for additional\nequipment and provides for a streamlined login experience.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is reviewing options to enforce user logins after several account takeovers. The\n\n\nfollowing conditions must be met as part of the solution:\n- Allow employees to work remotely or from assigned offices around the\nworld.\n- Provide a seamless login experience.\n- Limit the amount of equipment required.\nWhich of the following best meets these conditions?", "options": ["Trusted devices", "Geotagging", "Smart cards", "Time-based logins"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL363", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Honeypot attracts and traps attacker and DNS sinkhole redirects malicious domain name queries\nto a controlled server to detect and block communication between compromised host and their\nC2 servers.", "type": "multi", "q": "Which of the following methods can be used to detect attackers who have successfully infiltrated\na network? (Choose two.)", "options": ["Tokenization", "CI/CD", "Honeypots", "Threat modeling", "DNS sinkhole", "Data obfuscation"], "answer": [2, 4]}, {"id": "PL364", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Hashing ensures integrity of software by detecting any unauthorized changes or tampering after\nits final version.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company wants to ensure that the software it develops will not be tampered with after the final\nversion is completed. Which of the following should the company most likely use?", "options": ["Hashing", "Encryption", "Baselines", "Tokenization"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL365", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "SAML is widely used protocol for enabling SSO across different applications and systems,\nparticularly in enterprise environments. It allows users to authentication once and gain access to\nmultiple application, including cloud based services.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization completed a project to deploy SSO across all business applications last year.\nRecently, the finance department selected a new cloud-based accounting software vendor. Which\nof the following should most likely be configured during the new software deployment?", "options": ["RADIUS", "SAML", "EAP", "OpenID"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL366", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "An on-path attack, also known as a man-in-the-middle (MITM) attack, occurs when an attacker\nintercepts the communication between two parties (in this case, the user and the airline's\nwebsite). Since the user was on a public Wi-Fi network and ignored security warnings, it's\npossible that the attacker was able to intercept the credit card information during the transaction,\nleading to unauthorized charges.", "type": "mc", "q": "A user, who is waiting for a flight at an airport, logs in to the airline website using the public Wi-Fi,\nignores a security warning and purchases an upgraded seat. When the flight lands, the user finds\nunauthorized credit card charges. Which of the following attacks most likely occurred?", "options": ["Replay attack", "Memory leak", "Buffer overflow attack", "On-path attack"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL367", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Since the budget only allows for one ISP connection, this create a single point of failure for the\nnetwork connectivity.", "type": "mc", "q": "A network engineer deployed a redundant switch stack to increase system availability. However,\nthe budget can only cover the cost of one ISP connection. Which of the following best describes\nthe potential risk factor?", "options": ["The equipment MTBF is unknown.", "The ISP has no SLA.", "An RPO has not been determined.", "There is a single point of failure."], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL368", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Technical controls involve the use of technology to manage or mitigate risks. By segmenting the\nserver into VALN and restricting access to specific devices, the network team has employed a\ntechnical control here.\nCompensating controls are alternative measures in place to address a risk when the primary\ncontrol is not feasible which in these case segmenting the server into VLAN and limiting access\ncan be seen as compensating control.", "type": "multi", "q": "A network team segmented a critical, end-of-life server to a VLAN that can only be reached by\nspecific devices but cannot be reached by the perimeter network. Which of the following best\ndescribe the controls the team implemented? (Choose two.)", "options": ["G.", "Managerial", "Physical", "Corrective", "Detective", "Compensating"], "answer": [4, 5]}, {"id": "PL369", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The question was not asking about the single phone that was stolen (in which case a remote wipe\nmay work after the fact); rather, it asks for \"the best solution to increase mobile data security on\nall employees' company mobile devices\".", "type": "mc", "q": "A threat actor was able to use a username and password to log in to a stolen company mobile\ndevice. Which of the following provides the best solution to increase mobile data security on all\nemployees' company mobile devices?", "options": ["Application management", "Full disk encryption", "Remote wipe", "Containerization"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL370", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "This is the risk that remains after controls and mitigation efforts have been applied.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following best describes the risk present after controls and mitigating factors have\nbeen applied?", "options": ["Residual", "Avoided", "Inherent", "Operational"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL371", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Code obfuscation deliberately makes the code more difficult to understand. This involves\nrenaming variables, methods etc. Altering the code structure in ways that do not affect\nfunctionality but make reverse engineering much harder. Attacker use reverse engineering to find\nvulnerabilities that can be exploited or remove or bypass security protections such as encryption\nor anti tamper mechanisms.", "type": "mc", "q": "A software development team asked a security administrator to recommend techniques that\nshould be used to reduce the chances of the software being reverse engineered. Which of the\nfollowing should the security administrator recommend?", "options": ["Digitally signing the software", "Performing code obfuscation", "Limiting the use of third-party libraries", "Using compile flags"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL372", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a possible factor for MFA?", "options": ["Something you exhibit", "Something you have", "Somewhere you are", "Someone you know"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL373", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Since the issue is with the passwords being easy to guess, the solution would be one that\naddresses password complexity (and not password history or age necessarily). Increasing the\nminimum length of the password and introducing a special character would be the best options\nfor this.", "type": "multi", "q": "Easy-to-guess passwords led to an account compromise. The current password policy requires at\nleast 12 alphanumeric characters, one uppercase character, one lowercase character, a\npassword history of two passwords, a minimum password age of one day, and a maximum\npassword age of 90 days. Which of the following would reduce the risk of this incident from\nhappening again? (Choose two.)", "options": ["Increasing the minimum password length to 14 characters.", "Upgrading the password hashing algorithm from MD5 to SHA-512.", "Increasing the maximum password age to 120 days.", "Reducing the minimum password length to ten characters.", "Reducing the minimum password age to zero days.", "Including a requirement for at least one special character."], "answer": [0, 5]}, {"id": "PL374", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "The software contained a backdoor bypassing normal authentication method.", "type": "mc", "q": "A user downloaded software from an online forum. After the user installed the software, the\nsecurity team observed external network traffic connecting to the user's computer on an\n\n\nuncommon port. Which of the following is the most likely explanation of this unauthorized\nconnection?", "options": ["The software had a hidden keylogger.", "The software was ransomware.", "The user's computer had a fileless virus.", "The software contained a backdoor."], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL375", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "This option addresses the 50% memory utilization baseline, provides scalable storage, and\ntypically includes built-in redundancy to handle single circuit failures. IaaS providers offer flexible\nresource allocation, easy scalability, and robust infrastructure with multiple layers of redundancy.", "type": "mc", "q": "A utility company is designing a new platform that will host all the virtual machines used by\nbusiness applications. The requirements include:\n- A starting baseline of 50% memory utilization\n- Storage scalability\n- Single circuit failure resilience\nWhich of the following best meets all of these requirements?", "options": ["Connecting dual PDUs to redundant power supplies", "Transitioning the platform to an IaaS provider", "Configuring network load balancing for multiple paths", "Deploying multiple large NAS devices for each host"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL376", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "DNS sinkhole intercepts attempts to visit harmful websites and redirects them so you don't end\nup reaching a malicious website and keeps your computer safe.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following best describes a use case for a DNS sinkhole?", "options": ["Attackers can see a DNS sinkhole as a highly valuable resource to identify a company's domain structure.", "A DNS sinkhole can be used to draw employees away from known-good websites to malicious ones owned by the attacker.", "A DNS sinkhole can be used to capture traffic to known-malicious domains used by attackers.", "A DNS sinkhole can be set up to attract potential attackers away from a company's network resources."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL377", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "An incident analyst finds several image files on a hard disk. The image files may contain\n\n\ngeolocation coordinates. Which of the following best describes the type of information the analyst\nis trying to extract from the image files?", "options": ["Log data", "Metadata", "Encrypted data", "Sensitive data"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL378", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "S/MIME digital signatures provides a way to ensure that the email has not been altered and that it\ngenuinely comes from the sender (Non-repudiation).", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following most likely describes why a security engineer would configure all outbound\nemails to use S/MIME digital signatures?", "options": ["To meet compliance standards", "To increase delivery rates", "To block phishing attacks", "To ensure non-repudiation"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL379", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "IoT devices often have limited processing power, memory and battery life. This makes it crucial to\nchoose cryptographic algorithms that are efficient and can operate within these constraints\nwithout degrading device performance.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following considerations is the most important regarding cryptography used in an IoT\ndevice?", "options": ["Resource constraints", "Available bandwidth", "The use of block ciphers", "The compatibility of the TLS version"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL380", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "This will allow the coffee shop to restrict internet access by redirecting users to a web page where\nthey must enter the receipt information to gain access.", "type": "mc", "q": "A coffee shop owner wants to restrict internet access to only paying customers by prompting\nthem for a receipt number. Which of the following is the best method to use given this\nrequirement?", "options": ["WPA3", "Captive portal", "PSK", "IEEE 802.1X"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL381", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "When the computer powers off, anything in the RAM is going to be lost. Therefore, collecting\npotential evidence out of the RAM is the first thing that should be done out of these options.", "type": "mc", "q": "While performing digital forensics, which of the following is considered the most volatile and\nshould have the contents collected first?", "options": ["Hard drive", "RAM", "SSD", "Temporary files"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL382", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "This report provides an audit of the service organization controls over a specified period of time\nlike six months or more and assess how well those controls protect customers data according to\npredefined criteria.", "type": "mc", "q": "A hosting provider needs to prove that its security controls have been in place over the last six\nmonths and have sufficiently protected customer data. Which of the following would provide the\nbest proof that the hosting provider has met the requirements?", "options": ["NIST CSF", "SOC 2 Type 2 report", "CIS Top 20 compliance reports", "Vulnerability report"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL383", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A Disaster Recovery Plan is going to do exactly what the name states, put in place a plan to\nrestore functions of assets after a disaster.", "type": "mc", "q": "A city municipality lost its primary data center when a tornado hit the facility. Which of the\nfollowing should the city staff use immediately after the disaster to handle essential public\nservices?", "options": ["BCP", "Communication plan", "DRP", "IRP"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL384", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Segregation of duties is going to PREVENT users from having the ability to potentially manipulate\nprocesses within the business by splitting duties amongst others. Somewhat of a \"checks and\nbalances\" kind of system.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is considered a preventive control?", "options": ["Configuration auditing", "Log correlation", "Incident alerts", "Segregation of duties"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL385", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "This is clearly indicative of DoS attack where the two Test hosts are being overwhelmed with\nexcessive traffic received causing them to become unresponsive and crash.", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator notices that a testing system is down. While investigating, the systems\nadministrator finds that the servers are online and accessible from any device on the server\nnetwork. The administrator reviews the following information from the monitoring system:\n\nWhich of the following is the most likely cause of the outage?", "options": ["Denial of service", "ARP poisoning", "Jamming", "Kerberoasting"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL386", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Block the URL shortener domain in the web proxy: By blocking the URL shortener domain, the\nsecurity team can prevent users from accessing potentially malicious links, even if the domain is\ncurrently dead. This proactive measure helps mitigate the risk of future attacks using the same\nURL shortener.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security team has been alerted to a flood of incoming emails that have various subject lines and\nare addressed to multiple email inboxes. Each email contains a URL shortener link that is\nredirecting to a dead domain. Which of the following is the best step for the security team to take?", "options": ["Create a blocklist for all subject lines.", "Send the dead domain to a DNS sinkhole.", "Quarantine all emails received and notify all employees.", "Block the URL shortener domain in the web proxy."], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL387", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator is working to secure company data on corporate laptops in case the\nlaptops are stolen. Which of the following solutions should the administrator consider?", "options": ["Disk encryption", "Data loss prevention", "Operating system hardening", "Boot security"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL388", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A company needs to keep the fewest records possible, meet compliance needs, and ensure\ndestruction of records that are no longer needed. Which of the following best describes the policy\nthat meets these requirements?", "options": ["Security policy", "Classification policy", "Retention policy", "Access control policy"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL389", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Code repositories often contain hardcoded credentials, API keys, or other sensitive information\nthat developers may accidentally commit without proper security measures. This can expose\nthese credentials when the code is shared or made public, leading to unintentional leakage of\ncorporate credentials in cloud environments.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a common source of unintentional corporate credential leakage in cloud\nenvironments?", "options": ["Code repositories", "Dark web", "Threat feeds", "State actors", "Vulnerability databases"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL390", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the best reason an organization should enforce a data classification\npolicy to help protect its most sensitive information?", "options": ["End users will be required to consider the classification of data that can be used in documents.", "The policy will result in the creation of access levels for each level of classification.", "The organization will have the ability to create security requirements based on classification levels.", "Security analysts will be able to see the classification of data within a document before opening it."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL391", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "An analyst is performing a vulnerability scan against the web servers exposed to the internet\nwithout a system account. Which of the following is most likely being performed?", "options": ["Non-credentialed scan", "Packet capture", "Privilege escalation", "System enumeration", "Passive scan"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL392", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "MITRE ATT&CK is a comprehensive and widely used framework that categorizes and describes\nthe various tactics, techniques and procedures (TTPs) employed by adversaries, it is used for\nthreat intelligence, defensive strategy etc.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator is hardening corporate systems and applying appropriate mitigations by\nconsulting a real-world knowledge base for adversary behavior. Which of the following would be\nbest for the administrator to reference?", "options": ["MITRE ATT&CK", "CSIRT", "CVSS", "SOAR"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL393", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "By using APIs will allow for increased speed of data transfer compared to file based transfer\nmethods liker SFTP.", "type": "mc", "q": "An architect has a request to increase the speed of data transfer using JSON requests externally.\nCurrently, the organization uses SFTP to transfer data files. Which of the following will most likely\nmeet the requirements?", "options": ["A website-hosted solution", "Cloud shared storage", "A secure email solution", "Microservices using API"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL394", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following addresses individual rights such as the right to be informed, the right of\naccess, and the right to be forgotten?", "options": ["GDPR", "PCI DSS", "NIST", "ISO"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL395", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "The administrator needs to the server's certificate in the local trust store of the machine where\nLDAP browser tool is being used. This will allow the client to trust the server's certificate and\nestablish a secure connection.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator is installing an LDAP browser tool in order to view objects in the corporate LDAP\ndirectory. Secure connections to the LDAP server are required. When the browser connects to\nthe server, certificate errors are being displayed, and then the connection is terminated. Which of\nthe following is the most likely solution?", "options": ["The administrator should allow SAN certificates in the browser configuration.", "The administrator needs to install the server certificate into the local truststore.", "The administrator should request that the secure LDAP port be opened to the server.", "The administrator needs to increase the TLS version on the organization's RA."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL396", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Legacy systems often rely on outdated protocols that may not have the necessary security\nfeatures to protect against modern threats. This can lead to vulnerabilities that attackers can\nexploit, compromising the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of the system and its data.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the most important security concern when using legacy systems to\nprovide production service?", "options": ["Instability", "Lack of vendor support", "Loss of availability", "Use of insecure protocols"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL397", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Telnet transmits data, including credentials, in plaintext, making it vulnerable to interception. A\npacket capture tool could easily capture the login credentials being transmitted, allowing an\nattacker to gain unauthorized access to the server.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security investigation revealed that malicious software was installed on a server using a server\nadministrator's credentials. During the investigation, the server administrator explained that\nTelnet was regularly used to log in. Which of the following most likely occurred?", "options": ["A spraying attack was used to determine which credentials to use.", "A packet capture tool was used to steal the password.", "A remote-access Trojan was used to install the malware.", "A dictionary attack was used to log in as the server administrator."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL398", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "SSH (Secure Shell) provides encrypted remote access to servers, making it a secure alternative\nto Telnet, which transmits data in plaintext. SSH is commonly used for secure management of\nremote systems and would be the appropriate choice given the restriction on insecure protocols.", "type": "mc", "q": "A user is requesting Telnet access to manage a remote development web server. Insecure\nprotocols are not allowed for use within any environment. Which of the following should be\nconfigured to allow remote access to this server?", "options": ["HTTPS", "SNMPv3", "SSH", "RDP", "SMTP"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL399", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Wildcard certificates allow you to secure a domain and all of its subdomains with a single\ncertificate. This can be a cost-effective solution for managing certificates for a large number of\ndomains and subdomains.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator is working to find a cost-effective solution to implement certificates for a\nlarge number of domains and subdomains owned by the company. Which of the following types\nof certificates should the administrator implement?", "options": ["Wildcard", "Client certificate", "Self-signed", "Code signing"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL400", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Nessus is a vulnerability scanner that can identify insecure ports, legacy protocols, and other\nsecurity issues on servers. It is designed to detect vulnerabilities and misconfigurations in\nsystems.", "type": "mc", "q": "An auditor discovered multiple insecure ports on some servers. Other servers were found to have\nlegacy protocols enabled. Which of the following tools did the auditor use to discover these\nissues?", "options": ["Nessus", "curl", "Wireshark", "netcat"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL401", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The PCAP shows traffic using IPv6 encapsulated within IPv4 (proto IPv6 (41)), which could be\nused to hide sensitive data within seemingly normal network traffic. This encapsulation technique\ncan potentially bypass certain security controls and filters, making it an effective method for data\nexfiltration.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst received a tip that sensitive proprietary information was leaked to the public.\nThe analyst is reviewing the PCAP and notices traffic between an internal server and an external\nhost that includes the following:\n...\n12:47:22.327233 PPPoE [ses 0x8122] IP (tos 0x0, ttl 64, id 0, offset 0,\nflags [DF], proto IPv6 (41), length 331) 10.5.1.1 > 52.165.16.154: IP6\n(hlim E3, next-header TCP (6) paylcad length: 271)\n2001:67c:2158:a019::ace.53104 >\n2001:0:5ef5:79fd:380c:dddd:a601:24fa.13788: Flags [P.], cksum 0xd7ee\n(correct), seq 97:348, ack 102, win 16444, length 251\n...\nWhich of the following was most likely used to exfiltrate the data?", "options": ["Encapsulation", "MAC address spoofing", "Steganography", "Broken encryption", "Sniffing via on-path position"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL402", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Serverless architecture allows the company to reduce the time and expense associated with code\ndeployment by handling the underlying infrastructure management automatically. This means the\ncompany only needs to focus on the code itself, without worrying about provisioning or managing\nservers. This approach can also scale automatically with demand, further reducing operational\n\n\noverhead and costs.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company wants to reduce the time and expense associated with code deployment. Which of\nthe following technologies should the company utilize?", "options": ["Serverless architecture", "Thin clients", "Private cloud", "Virtual machines"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL403", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Password crackers often attempt to access this file to obtain hashed passwords for cracking.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator is performing an audit on a stand-alone UNIX server, and the following\nmessage is immediately displayed:\n(Error 13): /etc/shadow: Permission denied.\nWhich of the following best describes the type of tool that is being used?", "options": ["Pass-the-hash monitor", "File integrity monitor", "Forensic analysis", "Password cracker"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL404", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "The protocols RTP (Real-time Transport Protocol), SIP (Session Initiation Protocol), H.323, and\nSRTP (Secure Real-time Transport Protocol) are commonly used in Voice over IP (VoIP)\ncommunications. RTP handles the transport of media streams, SIP manages call setup and\ncontrol, H.323 is a standard for multimedia communication, and SRTP provides encryption for\nRTP. Therefore, the firewall rules for these protocols support VoIP.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator needs to create firewall rules for the following protocols: RTP, SIP,\nH.323. and SRTP. Which of the following does this rule set support?", "options": ["RTOS", "VoIP", "SoC", "HVAC"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL405", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Whaling is a type of social engineering attack specifically targeting high-profile individuals such as\nCEOs or other executives. It is a form of spear phishing that focuses on these high-value targets\nwith highly personalized and convincing messages.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following best describes a social engineering attack that uses a targeted electronic\nmessaging campaign aimed at a Chief Executive Officer?", "options": ["Whaling", "Spear phishing", "Impersonation", "Identity fraud"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL406", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The first priority is to revoke any compromise certificates. This ensures that those certificates can\nno longer be used for unauthorized access.", "type": "mc", "q": "During a penetration test, a flaw in the internal PKI was exploited to gain domain administrator\nrights using specially crafted certificates. Which of the following remediation tasks should be\ncompleted as part of the cleanup phase?", "options": ["Updating the CRL", "Patching the CA", "Changing passwords", "Implementing SOAR"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL407", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "When using a smart card as part of MFA, the additional factor is typically a PIN. The smart card\nprovides something you have and the PIN provides something you know, which together\nconstitutes two factors of authentication.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company wants to implement MFA. Which of the following enables the additional factor while\nusing a smart card?", "options": ["PIN", "Hardware token", "User ID", "SMS"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL408", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "PAM software helps manage and secure privileged accounts and access credentials. It allows\nadmins to grant temporary, controlled access to critical systems without sharing passwords\ndirectly. PAM software can track, monitor and log all activities performed by the consultant.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company hired an external consultant to assist with required system upgrades to a critical\nbusiness application. A systems administrator needs to secure the consultant's access without\nsharing passwords to critical systems. Which of the following solutions should most likely be\nutilized?", "options": ["TACACS+", "SAML", "An SSO platform", "Role-based access control", "PAM software"], "answer": [4]}, {"id": "PL409", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Users logging into captive portal and accepting the terms and conditions before gaining access\nshould address the concern raise by legal department.", "type": "mc", "q": "A newly implemented wireless network is designed so that visitors can connect to the wireless\n\n\nnetwork for business activities. The legal department is concerned that visitors might connect to\nthe network and perform illicit activities. Which of me following should the security team\nimplement to address this concern?", "options": ["Configure a RADIUS server to manage device authentication.", "Use 802.1X on all devices connecting to wireless.", "Add a guest captive portal requiring visitors to accept terms and conditions.", "Allow for new devices to be connected via WPS."], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL410", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The data owner is indeed responsible for identifying risks and determining the appropriate access\nto data.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following data roles is responsible for identifying risks and appropriate access to\ndata?", "options": ["Owner", "Custodian", "Steward", "Controller"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL411", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Lighting will illuminate the area, detect people attempting to be under the cover of night, and deter\nthem from committing unwanted acts. Furthermore, a sensor will detect movement in an area,\nand sensors that are visible can ward off any potential bad actors.", "type": "multi", "q": "Which of the following physical controls can be used to both detect and deter? (Choose two.)", "options": ["Lighting", "Fencing", "Signage", "Sensor", "Bollard", "Lock"], "answer": [0, 3]}, {"id": "PL412", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A load balancer is the most appropriate solution to ensure accessibility of a business-critical\napplication during peak usage times. It distributes incoming network traffic across multiple\nservers, optimizing resource utilization, maximizing throughput, minimizing response time, and\navoiding overload on any single server. This is particularly crucial for a multinational bank's\ncustomer-facing application during high-traffic periods.", "type": "mc", "q": "A multinational bank hosts several servers in its data center. These servers run a business-critical\napplication used by customers to access their account information. Which of the following should\nthe bank use to ensure accessibility during peak usage times?", "options": ["Load balancer", "Cloud backups", "Geographic dispersal", "Disk multipathing"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL413", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Code signing helps ensure the integrity and authenticity of your software package. It prevents bad\nactors from successfully repackaging your software with malware, as the digital signature would\nno longer match. Users can verify that the software comes from you and hasn't been tampered\nwith since you signed it, increasing trust and security.", "type": "mc", "q": "The author of a software package is concerned about bad actors repackaging and inserting\nmalware into the software. The software download is hosted on a website, and the author\nexclusively controls the website's contents. Which of the following techniques would best ensure\nthe software's integrity?", "options": ["Input validation", "Code signing", "Secure cookies", "Fuzzing"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL414", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A third-party vendor is moving a particular application to the end-of-life stage at the end of the\ncurrent year. Which of the following is the most critical risk if the company chooses to continue\nrunning the application?", "options": ["Lack of security updates", "Lack of new features", "Lack of support", "Lack of source code access"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL415", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst recently read a report about a flaw in several of the organization's printer\nmodels that causes credentials to be sent over the network in cleartext, regardless of the\nencryption settings. Which of the following would be best to use to validate this finding?", "options": ["Wireshark", "netcat", "Nessus", "Nmap"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL416", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A development team is launching a new public-facing web product. The Chief Information\nSecurity Officer has asked that the product be protected from attackers who use malformed or\ninvalid inputs to destabilize the system. Which of the following practices should the development\nteam implement?", "options": ["Fuzzing", "Continuous deployment", "Static code analysis", "Manual peer review"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL417", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Change control process: The change control process ensures that any modifications to the\ndesign are systematically evaluated, approved, and documented. This helps in maintaining the\nintegrity of the system and ensures that changes are implemented in a controlled and coordinated\nmanner.", "type": "mc", "q": "During an annual review of the system design, an engineer identified a few issues with the\ncurrently released design. Which of the following should be performed next according to best\npractices?", "options": ["Risk management process", "Product design process", "Design review process", "Change control process"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL418", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is best to use when determining the severity of a vulnerability?", "options": ["CVE", "OSINT", "SOAR", "CVSS"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL419", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Mimikatz is an open-source tool that allows users to view and extract credentials stored on a\nWindows system. It can extract plaintext passwords, hashes, PIN codes, and Kerberos tickets\nfrom memory.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization experienced a security breach that allowed an attacker to send fraudulent wire\ntransfers from a hardened PC exclusively to the attacker's bank through remote connections. A\nsecurity analyst is creating a timeline of events and has found a different PC on the network\ncontaining malware. Upon reviewing the command history, the analyst finds the following:\n\n\nPS>.\\mimikatz.exe \"sekurlsa::pth /user:localadmin /domain:corpdomain.com /ntlm:B4B9B02E1F29A3CF193EAB28C8D617D3F327\nWhich of the following best describes how the attacker gained access to the hardened PC?", "options": ["The attacker created fileless malware that was hosted by the banking platform.", "The attacker performed a pass-the-hash attack using a shared support account.", "The attacker utilized living-off-the-land binaries to evade endpoint detection and response software.", "The attacker socially engineered the accountant into performing bad transfers."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL420", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "OWASP (Open Web Application Security Project). OWASP provides extensive resources,\nguidelines, and tools related to web application security, including the OWASP Top 10, which lists\nthe most critical security risks to web applications.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the best resource to consult for information on the most common\napplication exploitation methods?", "options": ["OWASP", "STIX", "OVAL", "Threat intelligence feed", "Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL421", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The AXFR request is typically used by attackers to obtain a complete list of DNS records, which\ncan reveal internal IP addresses and hostnames, thereby providing a detailed map of the internal\nnetwork. This information can then be used for further attacks, such as identifying critical systems\nor planning network intrusions.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst is reviewing the logs on an organization's DNS server and notices the following\nunusual snippet:\n\nWhich of the following attack techniques was most likely used?\nA. Determining the organization's ISP-assigned address space", "options": ["Bypassing the organization's DNS sinkholing", "Footprinting the internal network", "Attempting to achieve initial access to the DNS server", "Exfiltrating data from fshare.int.complia.org"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL422", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Conditional access policies restrict access based on certain conditions such as location, device\ntype or risk level and if anything suspicious is detected, conditional access can block those\nattempts.\nMFAL on the other hand is a strong security measure that adds an extra layer of verification.", "type": "multi", "q": "A security analyst at an organization observed several user logins from outside the organization's\nnetwork. The analyst determined that these logins were not performed by individuals within the\norganization. Which of the following recommendations would reduce the likelihood of future\nattacks? (Choose two.)", "options": ["Disciplinary actions for users", "Conditional access policies", "More regular account audits", "Implementation of additional authentication factors", "Enforcement of content filtering policies", "A review of user account permissions"], "answer": [1, 3]}, {"id": "PL423", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "NIDS (Network Intrusion Detection System):\nThis system monitors network traffic for potential malicious activity, including attempts to exploit\nvulnerabilities in the web application. While it primarily detects rather than prevents, it provides\nvaluable insights into potential threats and alerts the security team", "type": "multi", "q": "A security team is addressing a risk associated with the attack surface of the organization's web\napplication over port 443. Currently, no advanced network security capabilities are in place.\nWhich of the following would be best to set up? (Choose two.)", "options": ["NIDS", "Honeypot", "Certificate revocation list", "HIPS", "WAF", "SIEM"], "answer": [0, 4]}, {"id": "PL424", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator would like to create a point-in-time backup of a virtual machine. Which of\nthe following should the administrator use?", "options": ["Replication", "Simulation", "Snapshot", "Containerization"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL425", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Decommissioning unused and non-compliant desktops will reduce security risks by removing\npotential points of vulnerability from the network. This action helps to ensure that only compliant\nand necessary devices are connected, maintaining the integrity and security of the network.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security administrator notices numerous unused, non-compliant desktops are connected to the\nnetwork. Which of the following actions would the administrator most likely recommend to the\nmanagement team?", "options": ["Monitoring", "Decommissioning", "Patching", "Isolating"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL426", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Sanitization is the process of removing sensitive data from a storage device in a manner that\nensures the data cannot be recovered while allowing device to be reused. This involves methods\nlike overwriting the data with zeros or other patterns multiple times.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a common data removal option for companies that want to wipe\nsensitive data from hard drives in a repeatable manner but allow the hard drives to be reused?", "options": ["Sanitization", "Formatting", "Degaussing", "Defragmentation"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL427", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Time-based tokens work across various platforms including SaaS and internal applications, are\ndevice manufacturer agnostic and can be used offline.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization wants to improve the company's security authentication method for remote\nemployees. Given the following requirements:\n- Must work across SaaS and internal network applications\n- Must be device manufacturer agnostic\n- Must have offline capabilities\n\n\nWhich of the following would be the most appropriate authentication method?", "options": ["Username and password", "Biometrics", "SMS verification", "Time-based tokens"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL428", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "This is simulating phishing attacks to educate employees about recognizing and handling of\nphishing attempts.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security officer is implementing a security awareness program and has placed security-themed\nposters around the building and assigned online user training. Which of the following will the\nsecurity officer most likely implement?", "options": ["Password policy", "Access badges", "Phishing campaign", "Risk assessment"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL429", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "", "type": "mc", "q": "A malicious update was distributed to a common software platform and disabled services at many\norganizations. Which of the following best describes this type of vulnerability?", "options": ["DDoS attack", "Rogue employee", "Insider threat", "Supply chain"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL430", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "The shell would allow the attacker to gain unauthorized access and control over the server.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company web server is initiating outbound traffic to a low-reputation, public IP on non-standard\npat. The web server is used to present an unauthenticated page to clients who upload images the\ncompany. An analyst notices a suspicious process running on the server hat was not created by\nthe company development team. Which of the following is the most likely explanation for his\nsecurity incident?", "options": ["A web shell has been deployed to the server through the page.", "A vulnerability has been exploited to deploy a worm to the server.", "Malicious insiders are using the server to mine cryptocurrency.", "Attackers have deployed a rootkit Trojan to the server over an exposed RDP port."], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL431", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "SCAP offers framework for automating security compliance and vulnerability assessments which\nis crucial for a comprehensive analysis of security and compliance aspects across the supply\nchain.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization requests a third-party full-spectrum analysis of its supply chain. Which of the\nfollowing would the analysis team use to meet this requirement?", "options": ["Vulnerability scanner", "Penetration test", "SCAP", "Illumination tool"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL432", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "Agentless monitoring does not require the installation of software on the target device. It uses\nstandard protocols to collect information, making it less intrusive and less resource intensive.", "type": "mc", "q": "A systems administrator deployed a monitoring solution that does not require installation on the\nendpoints that the solution is monitoring. Which of the following is described in this scenario?", "options": ["Agentless solution", "Client-based soon", "Open port", "File-based solution"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL433", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Static code analysis entails the review of source code and it is static because it is not running on\na computer. This is the opposite of dynamic code analysis which is done while the code is\nactually executing on the computer system.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst is reviewing the source code of an application in order to identify\nmisconfigurations and vulnerabilities. Which of the following kinds of analysis best describes this\nreview?", "options": ["Dynamic", "Static", "Gap", "Impact"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL434", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "A Non-Disclosure Agreement is a legal document prohibiting the disclosure of details that are\nagreed upon under the NDA. This will include limiting external discussions.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following agreement types is used to limit external discussions?", "options": ["BPA", "NDA", "SLA", "MSA"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL435", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Due diligence in this context involves evaluating the security, availability, processing integrity,\nconfidentiality, and privacy of the SaaS application by reviewing the SOC 2 report provided by the\nvendor. This process helps ensure that the vendor meets the required security and operational\nstandards before the SaaS application is implemented.", "type": "mc", "q": "A security analyst is evaluating a SaaS application that the human resources department would\nlike to implement. The analyst requests a SOC 2 report from the SaaS vendor. Which of the\nfollowing processes is the analyst most likely conducting?", "options": ["Internal audit", "Penetration testing", "Attestation", "Due diligence"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL436", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Masking involves altering the credit card information in such a way that it is not easily readable or\nidentifiable while still retaining some format or structure for processing or display purposes. This\nis particularly useful for ensuring sensitive data is protected in log files or other records.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is used to conceal credit card information in a database log file?", "options": ["Tokenization", "Masking", "Hashing", "Obfuscation"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL438", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "A WAF is specifically designed to protect web applications by filtering and monitoring HTTP traffic\nbetween a web application and the internet. It can help prevent attacks such as SQL injection,\ncross-site scripting (XSS), and other web-based threats that could target the new service\naccessed through the web portal.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization recently started hosting a new service that customers access through a web\nportal. A security engineer needs to add to the existing security devices a new solution to protect\nthis new service. Which of the following is the engineer most likely to deploy?", "options": ["Layer 4 firewall", "NGFW", "WAF", "UTM"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL439", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Branch protection requirements are related to the version control and development process within\nthe SDLC, ensuring that code changes are reviewed, tested, and approved before being merged\ninto main branches. This helps maintain code quality and security throughout the development\nprocess.\nPenetration testing is usually conducted as part of the testing phase or after deployment to\nidentify vulnerabilities and security weaknesses. It is a separate process from the core stages of\nthe SDLC but is an important aspect of ensuring the security and robustness of the application\nonce development is completed.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following topics would most likely be included within an organization's SDLC?", "options": ["Service-level agreements", "Information security policy", "Penetration testing methodology", "Branch protection requirements"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL440", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Operational controls are procedures and policies that dictate how users should behave and how\nprocesses are carried out to ensure security. The AUP falls under this category as it defines\nacceptable and unacceptable behavior for users, making it an operational control.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following control types is AUP an example of?", "options": ["Physical", "Managerial", "Technical", "Operational"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL441", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Selecting an IdP is the initial step in implementing Single Sign-On (SSO) or federated identity\nmanagement, which will allow employees to use a single set of credentials to access multiple\nSaaS applications. After selecting an IdP, the security team can then enable SAML or other SSO\nprotocols to integrate with the applications and manage authentication.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization is adopting cloud services at a rapid pace and now has multiple SaaS\napplications in use. Each application has a separate log-in, so the security team wants to reduce\nthe number of credentials each employee must maintain. Which of the following is the first step\nthe security team should take?", "options": ["Enable SAML.", "Create OAuth tokens.", "Use password vaulting.", "Select an IdP."], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL442", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "The code provided (DROP DATABASE WebShopOnline) suggests that the database was\ndeliberately dropped on a specific date (January 30, 2023). This could potentially be the result of\na command injection attack, where an attacker inserts malicious code to manipulate or destroy\nthe database.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company's online shopping website became unusable shortly after midnight on January 30,\n2023. When a security analyst reviewed the database server, the analyst noticed the following\ncode used for backing up data:\n\nWhich of the following should the analyst do next?", "options": ["Check for recently terminated DBAs.", "Review WAF logs for evidence of command injection.", "Scan the database server for malware.", "Search the web server for ransomware notes."], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL443", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Production failover involves switching to a backup power source or system to ensure that\noperations continue seamlessly in the event of a primary power failure. This type of testing\nverifies that the failover mechanisms work as intended under real-world conditions and ensures\nthat the system can handle the transition smoothly.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following would be the best way to test resiliency in the event of a primary power\nfailure?", "options": ["Parallel processing", "Tabletop exercise", "Simulation testing", "Production failover"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL444", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Wiping involves securely erasing data by overwriting the hard drive, ensuring the information is\nunrecoverable. It is cost-effective compared to physical destruction methods like shredding.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company is concerned about the theft of client data from decommissioned laptops. Which of\nthe following is the most cost-effective method to decrease this risk?", "options": ["Wiping", "Recycling", "Shredding", "Deletion"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL445", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Zero-day vulnerabilities are unknown flaws in software, making them harder to patch, especially\nwhen using open-source libraries without dedicated support teams.", "type": "mc", "q": "A company relies on open-source software libraries to build the software used by its customers.\nWhich of the following vulnerability types would be the most difficult to remediate due to the\ncompany's reliance on open-source libraries?", "options": ["Buffer overflow", "SQL injection", "Cross-site scripting", "Zero day"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL446", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "The logs show an SQL injection attack. The first step is to verify if new accounts have been\ncreated, indicating a successful injection.", "type": "mc", "q": "While investigating a possible incident, a security analyst discovers the following log entries:\n67.118.34.157 ----- [28/Jul/2022:10:26:59 -0300] \"GET /query.php?q-wireless%20headphones /\nHTTP/1.0\" 200 12737\n132.18.222.103 ----[28/Jul/2022:10:27:10 -0300] \"GET /query.php?q=123 INSERT INTO users\nVALUES('temp', 'pass123')# / HTTP/1.0\" 200 935\n12.45.101.121 ----- [28/Jul/2022:10:27:22 -0300] \"GET /query.php?q=mp3%20players I\nHTTP/1.0\" 200 14650\n\n\nWhich of the following should the analyst do first?", "options": ["Implement a WAF", "Disable the query .php script", "Block brute-force attempts on temporary users", "Check the users table for new accounts"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL447", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Full Disk Encryption (FDE) ensures that all data on the drive is encrypted, preventing\nunauthorized access even if the device is lost.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following should be used to ensure an attacker is unable to read the contents of a\nmobile device's drive if the device is lost?", "options": ["TPM", "ECC", "FDE", "HSM"], "answer": [2]}, {"id": "PL448", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Cloud-based models provide strong security with features like encryption, redundancy, and\ndisaster recovery, making it a secure choice for international operations.", "type": "mc", "q": "An organization plans to expand its operations internationally and needs to keep data at the new\nlocation secure. The organization wants to use the most secure architecture model possible.\nWhich of the following models offers the highest level of security?", "options": ["Cloud-based", "Peer-to-peer", "On-premises", "Hybrid"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL449", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Packet capture data can be very large and may not need to be stored for extended periods\ncompared to other logs essential for security audits.", "type": "mc", "q": "The security team at a large global company needs to reduce the cost of storing data used for\nperforming investigations. Which of the following types of data should have its retention length\nreduced?", "options": ["Packet capture", "Endpoint logs", "OS security logs", "Vulnerability scan"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL450", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Accounting logs user activities such as log-ins and usage duration, which is part of the AAA\nframework (Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting).", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the primary purpose of a service that tracks log-ins and time spent using\nthe service?", "options": ["Availability", "Accounting", "Authentication", "Authorization"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL451", "domain": "SecOps", "explanation": "A Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) solution collects, aggregates, and\ncorrelates logs from multiple sources to detect anomalies and generate alerts. SIEMs are\nessential for security monitoring and incident detection.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following should be used to aggregate log data in order to create alerts and detect\nanomalous activity?", "options": ["SIEM", "WAF", "Network taps", "IDS"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL452", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Side loading refers to the process of installing applications on a device from outside the official\napp store, which can introduce security vulnerabilities by bypassing standard app validation\nprocesses.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a type of vulnerability that refers to the unauthorized installation of\napplications on a device through means other than the official application store?", "options": ["Cross-site scripting", "Buffer overflow", "Jailbreaking", "Side loading"], "answer": [3]}, {"id": "PL453", "domain": "GRC", "explanation": "Dynamic analysis is performed on software during execution to identify vulnerabilities based on\nhow the software behaves in real-world scenarios. It is useful in detecting security issues that\nonly appear when the application is running.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following types of identification methods can be performed on a deployed application\nduring runtime?", "options": ["Dynamic analysis", "Code review", "Package monitoring", "Bug bounty"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL454", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A VPN (Virtual Private Network) is a secure method to provide employees with remote access to\na company's network. It encrypts data, protecting it from interception and ensuring secure\ncommunication between the user and the internal network.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is the best way to provide secure remote access for employees while\nminimizing the exposure of a company's internal network?", "options": ["VPN", "LDAP", "FTP", "RADIUS"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL455", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A Certificate Signing Request (CSR) is a request sent to a certificate authority (CA) to issue an\nSSL certificate. The CSR contains information like the public key, which will be part of the\ncertificate.", "type": "mc", "q": "An administrator must replace an expired SSL certificate. Which of the following does the\nadministrator need to create the new SSL certificate?", "options": ["CSR", "OCSP", "Key", "CRL"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL456", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "Deploying a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) solution allows for efficient log\naggregation, correlation, and analysis across an organization's infrastructure, providing real-time\nsecurity insights.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following strategies should an organization use to efficiently manage and analyze\nmultiple types of logs?", "options": ["Deploy a SIEM solution", "Create custom scripts to aggregate and analyze logs", "Implement EDR technology", "Install a unified threat management appliance"], "answer": [0]}, {"id": "PL457", "domain": "SecArch", "explanation": "A responsibility matrix clarifies the division of responsibilities between the cloud service provider\n(CSP) and the customer, ensuring that each party understands and implements their respective\nsecurity controls.", "type": "mc", "q": "A customer has a contract with a CSP and wants to identify which controls should be\nimplemented in the IaaS enclave. Which of the following is most likely to contain this information?", "options": ["Statement of work", "Responsibility matrix", "Service-level agreement", "Master service agreement"], "answer": [1]}, {"id": "PL458", "domain": "TVM", "explanation": "Cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities allow attackers to inject malicious scripts into a website,\nwhich are then executed in the user's web browser, potentially leading to data theft or session\nhijacking.", "type": "mc", "q": "Which of the following is a type of vulnerability that involves inserting scripts into web-based\napplications in order to take control of the client's web browser?", "options": ["SQL injection", "Cross-site scripting", "Zero-day exploit", "On-path attack"], "answer": [1]} ]; // ─── State ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── let state = { count: 10, sources: ['messer', 'examcompass', 'udemy', 'bonus', 'dumpsbase', 'dumpsarena', 'passleader'], timed: false, timerMins: 10, questions: [], current: 0, answers: {}, startTime: null, timerInterval: null, totalSeconds: 0, elapsed: 0 }; // ─── Config Logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── function setCount(btn) { document.querySelectorAll('#count-group .toggle-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active')); btn.classList.add('active'); state.count = parseInt(btn.dataset.val); updateSummary(); } function toggleSource(btn) { const val = btn.dataset.val; if (btn.classList.contains('active')) { const others = document.querySelectorAll('.source-btn.active'); if (others.length <= 1) return; // keep at least one btn.classList.remove('active'); state.sources = state.sources.filter(s => s !== val); } else { btn.classList.add('active'); state.sources.push(val); } updateSummary(); } function toggleTimed() { state.timed = true; document.getElementById('timed-toggle').classList.add('active'); document.getElementById('untimed-toggle').classList.remove('active'); document.getElementById('timer-options').classList.add('visible'); updateSummary(); } function toggleUntimed() { state.timed = false; document.getElementById('untimed-toggle').classList.add('active'); document.getElementById('timed-toggle').classList.remove('active'); document.getElementById('timer-options').classList.remove('visible'); updateSummary(); } function setTimer(btn) { document.querySelectorAll('.timer-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active')); btn.classList.add('active'); state.timerMins = parseInt(btn.dataset.mins); updateSummary(); } function updateSummary() { document.getElementById('sum-count').textContent = state.count; const srcLabel = state.sources.length === 7 ? 'All' : state.sources.join(' + ').replace('messer','Messer').replace('examcompass','ExamCompass').replace('udemy','Udemy').replace('bonus','Sec Bonus 1').replace('dumpsbase','Sec Bonus 2').replace('dumpsarena','Sec Bonus 3').replace('passleader','Sec Bonus 4'); document.getElementById('sum-source').textContent = srcLabel; document.getElementById('sum-timer').textContent = state.timed ? state.timerMins + ' min' : 'Off'; } // ─── Test Generation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── function shuffle(arr) { const a = [...arr]; for (let i = a.length - 1; i > 0; i--) { const j = Math.floor(Math.random() * (i + 1)); [a[i], a[j]] = [a[j], a[i]]; } return a; } function generateTest() { let pool = []; if (state.sources.includes('messer')) pool = pool.concat(MESSER_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'Prof. Messer'}))); if (state.sources.includes('examcompass')) pool = pool.concat(EXAMCOMPASS_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'ExamCompass'}))); if (state.sources.includes('udemy')) pool = pool.concat( UDEMY_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'Udemy'})), UDEMY2_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'Udemy'})), UDEMY3_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'Udemy'})) ); if (state.sources.includes('bonus')) pool = pool.concat(BONUS_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'Security Bonus 1'}))); if (state.sources.includes('dumpsbase')) pool = pool.concat(DUMPSBASE_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'Security Bonus 2'}))); if (state.sources.includes('dumpsarena')) pool = pool.concat(DUMPSARENA_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'Security Bonus 3'}))); if (state.sources.includes('passleader')) pool = pool.concat(PASSLEADER_QUESTIONS.map(q => ({...q, source: 'Security Bonus 4'}))); if (pool.length === 0) return; pool = shuffle(pool); state.questions = pool.slice(0, Math.min(state.count, pool.length)); state.current = 0; state.answers = {}; state.elapsed = 0; state.startTime = Date.now(); document.getElementById('config-panel').style.display = 'none'; document.getElementById('quiz-section').style.display = 'block'; renderQuestions(); updateNav(); if (state.timed) { state.totalSeconds = state.timerMins * 60; document.getElementById('timer-wrap').style.display = 'flex'; document.getElementById('q-counter-notimer').style.display = 'none'; startTimer(); } else { document.getElementById('timer-wrap').style.display = 'none'; document.getElementById('q-counter-notimer').style.display = 'block'; updateNotimer(); } } function renderQuestions() { const wrap = document.getElementById('questions-wrap'); wrap.innerHTML = ''; state.questions.forEach((q, idx) => { const card = document.createElement('div'); card.className = 'question-card' + (idx === 0 ? ' active' : ''); card.id = 'q-' + idx; const isMulti = q.type === 'multi' || Array.isArray(q.answer) && q.answer.length > 1; const letters = ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E', 'F']; const optsHTML = q.options.map((opt, oi) => { return `
  • ${letters[oi]}. ${opt}
    • `; }).join(''); const multiHint = isMulti ? `
    ⚠ Select all correct answers, then click Submit
    ` : ''; const submitBtn = isMulti ? `` : ''; card.innerHTML = `
    QUESTION ${idx + 1} / ${state.questions.length} ${q.source}
    ${q.q}
    ${multiHint}
      ${optsHTML}
    ${submitBtn}
    Explanation: ${q.explanation}
    `; wrap.appendChild(card); }); } function selectAnswer(qIdx, optIdx, el) { const card = document.getElementById('q-' + qIdx); const q = state.questions[qIdx]; const isMulti = q.type === 'multi' || (Array.isArray(q.answer) && q.answer.length > 1); if (card.classList.contains('answered')) return; if (isMulti) { // Toggle selection for multi el.classList.toggle('multi-selected'); return; } // Single answer const items = card.querySelectorAll('.option-item'); card.classList.add('answered'); items.forEach(i => i.classList.add('answered')); const correct = Array.isArray(q.answer) ? q.answer[0] : q.answer; state.answers[qIdx] = optIdx; items[correct].classList.add('correct'); if (optIdx !== correct) { el.classList.add('selected-wrong'); } document.getElementById('exp-' + qIdx).classList.add('show'); updateNav(); } function submitMulti(qIdx) { const card = document.getElementById('q-' + qIdx); if (card.classList.contains('answered')) return; const q = state.questions[qIdx]; const items = card.querySelectorAll('.option-item'); const selected = []; items.forEach((item, i) => { if (item.classList.contains('multi-selected')) selected.push(i); }); if (selected.length === 0) return; card.classList.add('answered'); items.forEach(i => i.classList.add('answered')); const correctAnswers = Array.isArray(q.answer) ? q.answer : [q.answer]; state.answers[qIdx] = selected;
